Perhaps not even eliminate them, but just hold them down so that their reasoning works: that the bad ones end up weak because they are bad, instead of they end up weak because others wanted so. And so again comes the concept of the loner untrusting of no one awake. Using the system to destroy it. And just like in that game which inspired me this idea, the game INSIDE, and the best part of it all is when the character shuts down your control over him. When will you free yourself from the controller?
The overhuman is then seeing reality for what it is. The bad parts too. What goodness ends up doing is filtering, lying. The overhuman, in other words, doesn't shy away from evil; he is open to the possibility that the world is in essence unfair and ruthless, like some fragments of it suggest. That it was born out of blood. The possibility that we are already living in hell. The possibility that humans are hell themselves, and that they committed genocide of an order we haven't conceived to be here.
To not live in hell. Cause if all humans die right now, except one, where does morality go? When humans die, God dies with them. When humans die, all of a sudden you are free from judgment from the divine. The bible flamed and the priest unmoving. Instincts. No more cameras when humans die, no more surveillance, no more passport, or driver's license, no more in-world identity. No more property. No more names. No more labels. Would you be able to see the world for what it is? Or would you hide yourself in the thoughts of those that came before you? Would the world gain in colour or go to black and white?

* Human, All Too Human: The Truth of Isolation and Human Deception :Philosophy:
Just feel the possibility of that happening, with humans going away except for you, my dearest, maybe paradise comes back. Maybe earth is happy again. Maybe species start filling the sky and land, doing their species sounds, in what becomes the biggest win in a long time.
And how much would you wish that others could be there with you witnessing this harmony of things. But they can't. They wouldn't see it. You would think you were crazy. Others couldn't know the truth. You were now so distant, so authentically convinced. And what if you saw this and could go back and tell people about it? Tough truth to digest. And it might be better digested alone. They would throw up, unable to swallow. It would be of the strongest spices they ever let in.
Oh my overhuman, are you alone! Stuck in time. You are at the wrong place wrong time, and because of that they don't understand that. In a way that's what they mean when in movies that kind of power can only be obtained by the young mind. For an old mind would fever itself into death, overheating of desperation when confronted with the truth.
And perhaps Nietzsche is ashamed that mankind has let their men into such deception, making them die for a lie. And right before dying, did they seize their condition? Did they seize that which was promised? Then again, maybe they convinced themselves they did, for otherwise seeing the reality of things would be soul-fading, as in heartbreaking. Perhaps humanity is lost at this step, when it lies to humans in such a manner. When they discover there was paradise right before their eyes. Maybe that's how humanity gets away with it. If they only realize it when they die, they are not here to advise others. Maybe death was programmed by humanity. Imagine that. Just like in the movie The Island, they get chosen to go to the island. Now it's easy to see now how those of the past went to the island; what is more worth it is to see how you could go, and avoid it. Some went for religion for example.

* Human, All Too Human: Decadence, Taming the Beast, and Morality :Philosophy:
Thinking of these things terrifies a bit, makes you afraid to go on with your life, just a tiny bit, you know? It pauses you on you.
Ultimately Nietzsche goes back to the decadence concept. That he is fighting those who lost their smell, o seu faro, those who faded. We should shine hard, but the system wants us to fade as individuals. It makes us feel ashamed of shining so bright. It takes ease at us taking it easy. It takes ease at us not wanting to become ourselves. And ultimately this is what the book is about, how one becomes what one is. A bunch of yes-saying neutral colors. The world in essence becoming more black and white. No more radiation whether good or bad. Funnelling individuals down a pipe into a pool of everyone. Humanity misunderstanding itself. No longer are there villains and heroes. Each is different in their own way. Denying life, he dares say. Denying elements of human nature, he conjures, aggression, competition. Selfless beings unable to tune themselves with the frequency of their instincts. Playing around with ideas such as souls to ashame the body, the only soul you ever touched!
Letting existence be handled by a world that is not ours, the divine. Amenização. Inclusion as stealing, not just stealing, that would be euphemism, it's taking away. Wrapping around the spirit. Calming the beast. Literally, oh my god, perfect fucking analogy: it's caging a tiger. It's putting a tiger in a zoo, feeding him ração. That's it right there. Devoiding him of purpose, it's castration, domar tigres que quando chegaram eram indomáveis. Isn't that what we are doing to children? And why even though they are relentless and unforgiving and authentic, they then end up with social anxiety and stuff? No baby thinks oh I'm doing too much noise. Should a baby never fall on his own risk? That's a question worth thinking about. Should you protect your baby to the point he doesn't take any risks? Of course not, that's stripping him of his learning, of his experience. Free spirits then lack today, for they have to free themselves, and few do. Shadow. Don't deny the meat that makes you. The fiber within you.

* Human, All Too Human: Proxies, Hope, and Escaping Reality :Philosophy:
Meaning the harsh truth of things then takes that weight off of you.
That people search for proxies, come up with concepts to help them alleviate the impregnable reality of what is their life. They wanna escape it, transcend them, find in the unreal that which discontented them in the real. They want to replace every real object from a piece of the world of ideas. They wanna rebuild the world because they can't handle it. They search for gaps, they see not the things but the shadows; that's why they see amazing miracles, like shadows faster than light. They want to add, they haven't yet realized the value of subtracting, a la Baudrillard. They find all that they can have not enough. They miss the very point. They play gambles in the world of possibility, their ignorance the reason for their hope. They use more what they don't know than what they do. If life was a long set of stairs, they spend all their time looking up, never to see that which is sure. It is as if they almost refuse to believe that which is in front of them, they refuse to believe in the tools they are handed. Believing in them is a choice, and a necessary one. They want to find all higher purposes so as to make their earthly existence have none. They make for themselves the bed that alleviates the pressure of there being things that can be done and they can do. A self-denial of sorts. A self-inhibition, prohibition. The opposite of selfism; the safer "don't believe what you don't see", so see what isn't there to rather believe in it. Instead of believing in things they don't yet see, but see possible, and reasons why, they prefer to believe in things that they think they see and that are by their nature not possible in the same way, so as to protect oneself from the angst of action.

* Human, All Too Human: Weakness and the Need to Empower :Philosophy:
The most terrible of the consequences of this negation of the meat on the plate is the hopelessness and powerless feel that it can bring, the spreading, disease-like spreading of this feel. When the good man sides with the weak and sick, and doesn't know better, cause better is foreign. When weakness has penetrated so surely that one doesn't know another way. No soldier knows at all times no battle has been lost, and no such thing exists that can't be changed at your volition. Why am I a destiny. Future assured, future confirmed, I am in charge, forever in control. There is always a way. Charles de Gaulle type discourse. Empower yourself.

* Human, All Too Human: Daring to Go Far and the Beauty of Silence :Philosophy:
Expresses that feeling that "todo eu sou uma força que me escapa". As in when one dares advance to new territories, when one gives in to the dark side, one risks getting stuck and not coming back. Life as going as further as you can and earning the rewards when coming back. And those that keep going, those that lose themselves to their spirit. Those condemned to fulfill their destiny. For life naturally divides its spirits in half: those who preferred to hide from their potential, and those condemned to become its slave. To be the slave of greatness or the master of littleness. And with eye-opening suspicion comes faith, for a sane homeostatic being does not risk himself so wide. Fighting against nature, daring to see oneself as more real than nature.
It's so beautiful to hear the silence for a moment, when all you've been hearing was nothing. A blank day becomes peace. For whom silence is craziness, and for whom silence is peace.
It's cool and dandy to let digest, but honestly I like the idea of forcefeeding, I like the idea of food for thought being shoved up my mouth down and back. Like vomiting but putting more in there.
That everything poets ever did was creating into the poem what they wanted to have found but couldn't.

* Human, All Too Human: Bound Spirits and the Separation from Virtues :Philosophy:
That free spirits were bound spirits, that a great separation turned them otherwise; they were bound to the external things that served them, that were good. And how can we condemn the bound spirits? For we bounded them, and hardly gave them life without that to which they were bound to. Not many planted the seed for their release. Most are themselves bound spirits, so how could they? Hence the feeling of betrayal for the bound of the pão nosso de cada dia.
The separation of one with his virtues, his feats, his ways, so that one allows himself to own himself. Most people bother doing the proof of their propositions. They haven't proven to themselves that which they think about themselves.
"Straining off everything of the coincidental." That is clarity. Como espremer um fruto para obter uma gota of the essence. Espreme o dia que tiveste hoje. Só sai água? É oco? I look around my bedroom, and why do we possess books to occupy shelves, books that we only used as bound spirits, to school, presents. We like to occupy space for some reason. So much dead space, so much dead time. We humans lack aliveness. So much dead thought. So many dead words.

* Human, All Too Human: Flies and the Dead Space :Life:
And a fly that flies her way, it is alive, it is game of live-like. Like the most alive thing in your house. Like over the years I've developed this current theory that flies are trying to tell you something, that flies know you and are rooting for you. That they are your flies. Do flies even exist, for god's sake? Are flies just an extension of our imagination, to populate the room, for us to have something to be bothered about, something to entertain ourselves with? Flies do hold much more than at first glance one might suppose. Flies are very close to humans, we talk so much about them. Think about it. Maybe they are the most thought about, most common, but no one realizes it. They give them credit saying they do nothing in this world. Thanks fly, let me bro fist you. I don't think she was into it.

* Human, All Too Human: The Tyranny of Efficiency and Language :Writing:
"One remains a philosopher - only by being silent". What the hell does that mean? A free spirit cannot claim its freeness if every time he is to express his thought he is bound to language, if language precedes his thought. And yet so instinctive are we to recur to language to formulate a thought. So quick are we to say the thought out loud, even if only for ourselves. So inappreciative of the blessing of thought have we become in not letting us figure it out. To shape it, mold it, improve it, and finally translate it to language. Most thought nowadays is only thought for a split second; how dare we call it thought if we didn't let him breathe into existence. How insultuous of us to interrupt the chain of thought to find a word for it. No, finding words has become opaque. Losing the train of thought because one was trying to write it, with some bureaucratic criteria for modulation, and well the only thing faster than light is thought...
The plague of the efficiency here robs us the opportunity of having an intimate connection with our thought. Yes, you write faster in a keyboard, but is it volume that you really lack, or is it density? Is it production of words that we humans lack, coordination into phrases, or genuine deep thought, of that kind that takes hours to show some trace? Look at chat GPT and tell me what lacks more.
The limiting factor nowadays is what you can create, not how much bullshit you can spit out in ten-fold seconds. Optimization here fools, my friend. Consider going back to pen and paper, for I don't think any well produced idea doesn't come from boring ass hours and curiosity, and not an emotional availability, but an availability to be with your thoughts. The world today wants to feed us stuff to occupy us from ourselves. Do you think Nietzsche would have his wood tested iron forged ideas while sitting in a room with his great setup for productivity, his design of the room, its 3 monitors, Microsoft 365 premium and all the crap you would want for that perfect writing section? I mean maybe he would do something with it, and we ought to be able to do something with it, but I mean you can't even allow yourself to have an idea while bus travelling and looking at the paisagem, like a child would, and you want to be on that level right away?
And language, man, everyone uses it the same way, that's the point, we agree to it so we can communicate our thought, but if the thoughts are coming from the language, how can you have novelty? It's a tight restriction on such a huge space of possibilities. Like your unicity won't come from your words man. It's combinatorial that way. You are imposing that language barrier on yourself. All the sequences are already written on the fucking web page of the tower which I don't remember the name and I don't care, well Babel remembered. Prevision beats power, and timing beats speed. In power and speed mankind is going to handle itself, but those meticulously administered small doses of truth injections here and there, those few thoughts that will change mankind, they are accessible to everyone, and hardly power and speed will take care of them. I was once bound to reading pages, but look at this, read like 12 pages in a whole 120 minutes or something. Language is cheap computation nowadays, and hence the devalorization of the word market, it's fucking free now. But thought? Oh man is that mf treasure valuable. Like if you realized how little of the right thing it would take to flip this world...

* Human, All Too Human: Social Media and the Attention Span :Life:
In Instagram stories, you are not anything more than 5 seconds of the attention span of another person. I am curious to know how much time they take to see the story which I planned like 2 years before (as in one finger pull up for example). Like will I get more than a "cool, anyways". Stories in insta don't favour stories nor histories, they favor capas de livros. The 21st century is the machine for making people buy book covers. And I am a victim of this too, how much have I read just reading the title and seeing the image, and the author. Low investment high dopamine return.
And me, that I am the master of putting thought into language by this very means. God damn it, I'm already limited enough to the words I know, no need to enclausurate me further, to bury myself under the carpet of my words. Sometimes the more you talk the more you subside in the sands of it all. Maybe what he means by philosophers remain philosophers as long as they are silent, wow what a fucking loop of things. I arrived here.

* Human, All Too Human: The Decline of the Inventor and Misunderstanding Language :Philosophy:
"Everyone's got a story to tell, Everyone knows about it." And this simulacra and simulation is to get worse, for we are mass producing designers and those alike. Inventor is a degrading word these days. There was a time where growing up to be an inventor was a thing. And so much distance we seem to have put on that time, the train is accelerating, it's ever harder to stop it. I should make a pray for Nietzsche man, two bus trips where this man's words have made my thought alive. Thanks my brotha. And you welcome, next one in line. It doesn't cease to disappoint. It's like I'm catching nothing, but then an opening opens and holy moly am I running through that door. There should be such a thing as silence exposure therapy.
Again it seems, like me and Nietzsche, that first one dominates language before one revolts against it. In this sense one should look for misunderstanding the language of another, or else he would be limited to the meaning of words. We gotta use language to go beyond it.

* Human, All Too Human: Sleep, Memory, and the Harvest of Nonsense :Life:
Says that memory is most sacrificed in sleep. In my words, that when one's body wants one's body to sleep, one's mind simulates and dissimulates one's mind. One achieves a temporary yet working everyday effect of lightness of conscience in committing the deadly sin of sleep and what it entails. Dream. An harvest of nonsense, perhaps to dissimulate the sense in when we are awake. Perhaps an attempt of deconstructing reality the right way. It seems like a dimensions' worth leap to figure the meaning of these things. Could we just be getting the local picture? Can this mean more than what at first it might seem?
Nietzsche says that it has to do with the primitive brain. That it's a way of closing the gap between our lives of today and those from which today was built. We live in a very high level place during the day; when sleeping we return to ones and zeros, to assembly. We get a grasp of how we first made things, by incorporating them without questioning, by believing in the direct explanation.

* Human, All Too Human: Unity of Words and the Power of Ideas :Philosophy:
"The unity of the word does not guarantee the unity of the thing". This can be screwed up real badly by those who assigned words to meanings and those who made synonyms. They imposed similarities in the world, unconsciously absorbed by the all-open babies. Sometimes as humans we forget that language is only a way of understanding. Like we can legit have misconceptions so engrained that few notice it that stem from calling different things the same. This is no little theme. Study this point carefully.
"Strong belief proves only its own strength, not the truth of what it believes."
The one electron Universe. The one will Universe. The world as will and representation.

* Human, All Too Human: Bounded by Space and Time :Philosophy:
That today one isn't anymore bounded to space and time, or has this possibility. And what a concept it is, to be fluid, to be mobile in the great hall of humanity. Space and time have to be honored as the greatest binders one faces in his life. Going all the way with this idea of a raw world of no idea whatsoever, and then coming back, once again the hardest part, and molding the world to your truth driven built will.
Reaction as progress, he points out, as in even if people's ideas aren't correct, they tell you what is wrong so that you can screw the balance in the right direction. Someone talking greatly about metaphysics can be an hint that too weak is science right now.
For eternal glory is seized through today's glory. That man only finds purpose with illogical thought, with impurity of thought. Only when selfishly focusing on individual things can one escape from the overarching numbness of mankind's direction.

* Human, All Too Human: Angst and the Middle of the Path :Philosophy:
And that the poet knows how to comfort himself, he that dares not escape, he that willingly stands still, open to the tsunami and the sandstorm of angst. Kierkegaard-like angst, that stops one and makes him fight for action. Fight to make a move just to stay in the game. This is where I've spent some time, but I've come to realize that I need escape from escapism, I have to seize. Either I'll make it or I'll die crazy, and hopefully I'm not already in the process of dying crazy.
We are mostly some bastards who can't help but to stay in their lane. And most of all we can't help but to keep moving. That one grows disdain to his motives does one think about it logically, one ought to roll through life.
"Like cameos, praising them because they cannot love them, and quick to admire them but even quicker to run away." In this state live a lot of famous people in people's minds. Just the admiration, that vague notion, that blush of something, yet just a title. How many great philosophers have created that space in my head, yet not to be read ever? How can one say he admires Nietzsche when everything one has is an impression. A feeling with null measure in time where one got a glimpse, yet was coward enough to let it at that, perhaps in the attempt to not let go of admiring him, not risking losing interest. This way one can throw the ocasional "oh yea Nietzsche that guy is tough man, respect". It is easy though to let them crawl into your head unaffected. It is feeling part of the collective. The collective that talks about something only the individual can know.

* Human, All Too Human: The Super Animal and the Rejection of Morality :Philosophy:
"The super animal. The beast in us wants to be lied to". Says that morality was in a sense created to keep us from suffering from the fact that there is no such thing as morality. Rather it seems to be that we want to lie to our beast, so we don't awaken it. The beast I was cries on the road. I left it there to come be who I am become. Morality, the suggestion presents itself, and this a reason people say Nietzsche is dangerous, morality isn't instinctive, morality is limiting, as we know, but limiting as in not freeing. Morality the sort of thing to leave us at home with the people we know, and not explore the forest from which a beast wanders in the shadows, a beast which we have come to disown, to disinherit, to unknow. "These civilized people, they'll eat each other". And so Nietzsche comes with this concept of an error in our progress, that morality was an error, a dissimulation, just like religion and art, all attempts of the human to justify having caged the beast, all attempts of the human to separate himself from land which he has ceased to touch and feel. He wears the most protective shoes, he only touches that of his kind, an attempt of cutting the roots, an unacceptance of what we came from. A presumption on what we have become. Creating human exclusive things, not to label the different, but to differentiate the label, a discrimination on the surroundings. A delusion of plagued beings, to keep them plaguing.
That the brevity of life makes humans stickier, less variable. On average we take ever more time to be "formed"? Formed as in formados, tipo estudados licenciados prontos a contribuir para a máquina.

* Human, All Too Human: Fake It Till You Make It and Belief :Philosophy:
"How seeming becomes being". Here Nietzsche lays down his account of fake it til you make it.
Says that in all deceptions the deceptor has to develop belief in his lie, and only when that belief overcomes him, I say: when he is not conscient of his belief, that's when he deceives and it's that belief that passes on to the audience. He is being honest, that's the point, the point is that his lie has fooled himself. And that's why you can believe in him easier. And this is great in that it gives you power to lie to yourself on purpose to believe something. But it also means you can be thinking that you don't believe in something around you, but maybe ya do. Maybe the day you saw it first time ya didn't, but now you've learned to believe in it, even if you don't allow yourself to know that.
And this we know, we humans adapt to things pretty fucking smoothly considering things. We think we own much more of our identity than perhaps we'd like to admit. And this is why it is absolutely important, or so they say, to choose wisely who you are around. Maybe you were different, and maybe there's still different inside you, but being with the others perhaps slowed that down, perhaps you think you are not one of them but are in the process of becoming. Know thyself. Keep thyself knowing thyself. Perhaps everytime we hear something we get closer to believing it. Perhaps that's why there are self-assuring mantras and what not. But think about it. Everytime someone says something you get closer to believing said thing.
"Become vocal with it, and it will happen" - Connor McMystic. And I mean the implications of this are huge. Like you really do create your reality. Imagine knowing this and what you could be as a result of faking it. It may not be a joke that I say that this is the most important thing you will hear. I mean this can be life changing. Join this to the "you do what you believe", like that guy that got the wrong SAT score and believed he was great and became a millionaire. Cheat code. Mind washing is real man.

* Human, All Too Human: Confidence, Worship, and the Mass Hallucination :Philosophy:
"For men will believe something is true, if it is evident that others believe in it firmly." It's sort of cause of the law of least action, at the end of the day you hear so much shit you just gotta believe, it isn't practical to go find counterarguments to everything. Confidence as a trait is then so attractive. It's biased. Even if you don't want you end up believing an unshakably confident person, it's actually unfair. Girls say they don't want the cocky sort of type but they can't help but fall for it sometimes, that's why they hate it. The level of absurdity in this world.
"How can you deny him?" Hallucinating into reality. Dreaming into reality. I mean saying it is just a step above dreaming. You do get closer to reaching your dreams by dreaming them. And visualization comes into the picture, makes you more fluent with it. Why does praying work? Makes you more vocal with it. Makes something external created internally, which I call Jesus, believe in you too. Simulate the conversations, hear his confidence in you and surer you will be.
That nobody understands what is being built literally or figuratively. Like the bomb. For the conscience of a man on such a big thing would kill him. So bring thousands of people together, so that their wrongdoing is done together, so that they are dependent on each other, trusting one another with good intentions on their path to hell. For we are seeing only one bone of the beast we create, and thus feel at peace with the final result. Put a man alone and he hardly can escalate malice. Put an infinitude of them and you shall have built a beast.

* Human, All Too Human: Suicide, Power, and the Wrecker :Philosophy:
That suicide may come as more natural, empowering, accountable, just, honorous. Suicide as in deciding to end life, not give up on it. Get out on your own terms. But no Hell no I ain't doing that. But could life get instantly more meaningful if you accepted your power to end it? You could believe you were alive for as long as you wanted, alive for when you wanted.
Each has as much right as his power is worth. This comes with a notion I love, that of the wrecker. Just getting and doing something no one imagined breaking rules conventions, and doing it all in a confident innocence and ignorance. I must become a wrecker.
The dichotomy of the executor and the commander, that one is protected by the lack thereof imagination in what he commands, and that the other is saved by the lack of responsibility in what he realizes. The latter not so judged by contrast, cause people think people have choices, and by contrast his wasn't very there. The former not so judged by committing a mistake, he didn't have reality first hand to help him out of a trap. Society failed him. As for the latter, he failed society. And so is amplified, and at the same time slowed down, made less fluid, more viscous yet more certain, the man who does it all. The man who brings forth a complete idea. Not dependent on other's mistakes he has firm grip on what happens and on what he does. In other words, he has choice. This is the kryptonite of capitalism specialization. That big errors can propagate unseemingly, infiltrating through the halls, like a spider crawling unnoticed. That insanity can go wild, exponential.
When you have the openness to see your idea, the balls to put it into language, then when you fancy do it, you will most likely see the shit you have created. Cause everybody gangsta until they responsible. Everybody has great ideas until their life depends on them.

* Human, All Too Human: Imposing Narratives and the Mystery of Art :Philosophy:
That religious worship is a way of directing nature, enveloping it in a human narrative, while with physics it's about submitting to its order, less those attempts of injecting beauty into the narrative, which get us farther from the reality it has presented us. This fun game is played bouncing between imposing and receiving. We want to impose cause vanity, and receive cause ego. The masters become slaves.
Says that art strives to be felt as an instantaneous self-evident piece of truth, in that it is an imposition. The artist rather have his art seen as a gift of god. This I wrote as art not wanting to be explained, and the more mysterious connotations and untied branches it leaves, the more room for inspiration on the viewer. Love and art and these abstract things we say are human, are in this very sense human cause they are fantasies, fed by the imagination of those who fall in them traps. Letting people put their inventiveness to fit their own selves, so that a masterpiece is multiple, plural. It is felt by everyone as they wish to feel it, which is to say as they are mistaken to feel it. Letting them earn their own art, cause people have egos man. If you just produce something that no one can do, so that a feeling of separation, an eternal gap to the viewer is felt, only an acknowledgment is given, no investment is made, so they are not committed to fight by your side. Rather, if you give them enough trace to follow, and if You WIN them, that means you will convince them of that which they want to be convinced. They did the work and the adjoining of the art with the observer is thus made. A bond is created. If your art lets people as far away from solving the puzzle as they already are, they won't credit your art even if they achieve truth. Make everyone feel like they are great, and that will make you great. They will make you great.
And those elements of mystique, that which people can't know, that's what they want. If you suddenly died after drawing this your last painting, suddenly people will pay due to watch it. If only people thought you would die in 5 minutes, they would hear for once, not listen. If people think they can access your way then they are that much less interested in what you gave out of that way. But if people don't understand how you can do something, even though they simply may not want to, suddenly you are given interest the most when compared to those living the same life as you. Dead authors, you cannot access their time nor them. Chess champions, you cannot access how their greatness arose, so you fantasize about you not being able to and them having this amazing secret. But if only you saw some plays, and learn some theory, then the fog would collapse, for it has zero measure. In the mind it expands the more you are afraid of it, the more you avoid it. It feeds off that in the unconscious realm, but if only you gathered up the balls to look at it dead in the eyes, you would lose this admiration. That's why they say don't meet your heroes, for you will lose hope that you can emancipate yourself from your nature, when you see that those you thought did are as much dirty bastards as you when you started looking up to them, or rather looking down to the ground when talking to them. Heroes are all matter of fantasy.

* Human, All Too Human: The Disappointment of the Known and Leaps of Faith :Philosophy:
That's the fun thing. These guys doing the great stuff are the ones who least know about it. The fans are the ones who know all the stats, and have narrated the greatness of the protagonists, have taken the time to polish their thought on how everything was meant, they elevate the moments, and elevate with them the people who know nothing about it. Like me with Bolt. I know so much better than him why he is so great.
That's why people are even bored if you just stand there admired, they lose interest in you, little fanboy.
People in essence salivate for discontinuity. They mirage the inflexion point in their lives, the take-off zone. Then they are surprised no music started playing when they got there. They still have to eat, shit, like when they thought they were something the less. I mean what did you expect when you put someone so far out there of course they are going to disappoint you, cause there's nowhere to go but down.
And as much as you don't want to admit it, you don't want the path ahead to be predictable, you don't want to win predictably, for I'd argue that would be total waste of time, even if you ended up out there. Without free will, it wouldn't have motive for joy. Cause if it's predictably winnable, then you know you can have it. Wins that matter come when you take that leap of faith, when you only along the way and sometimes only after winning learn that you will win. When you know you will do something you are just doing it for completion. Your mind has wandered away to the next thing. Cause how sad it is to live without testing the limits, without knowing what you could have done. What you already know you can do already is on that list, chasing it won't change you. Things new are the ones where you gain velocity, beforehand you were only moving forward, new things are where you gain acceleration, new thoughts are where you gain jerk. New being is where you gain movement.

* Human, All Too Human: The Rawness of Art and the Era of the Highlight :Life:
That the advantage of art is you only get the state function-valued final product, no evolution is implicit in what is created. You gotta know the evolution to do it, but don't be afraid of rejecting it all when showing it. Leave it raw, live it spicy. Don't put sauce on it, don't mix in other ingredients, just put the plate down with the product. No words can cover it up, when you nailed it, no song can filter it, it just sits as it is, there is no denying what it is. The rest will they do. Journalling by this reasoning should be for yourself, work in the shadows to shine in the light.
Sou um gajo sem noção. Essa é a minha vantagem. O que trago para a mesa é não ter limites, conseguir estar no plano mais acima mais fictício mais infantil, e no entanto ter sempre por detrás da orelha a parte real desta coisa. Não é fácil transitar de uma faixa para a outra, não é fácil mudar o chip instantaneamente, mas é isso que vou conseguir fazer. Go high, I can go low.
Hardly hear each other, how would they give a glimpse of care into a poem you wrote as training, they don't, it's the era of the highlight, whether you want it or not, your life may be registered in the 21st and 22nd century, through your highlights, maybe not what you consider to be your highlights. Yet if you leave it so raw that it smells, that others can't explain, that others can't rationalize but only appreciate, that others can only fantasize about, you have got a place reserved deep inside their mind, my dear friend. And that's what your modern day world genes have wanted. Sometimes shit happens that you don't explain, just roll with it, sometimes what you think is a highlight is a mere entry in the blockchain of this world, one more transaction. Like honestly though. No one understands your motives, they don't understand your jokes, only theirs. In this higher abstraction world we are just using each other, there are very few that you actually got your back protected, there are genuinely very few that you would really fucking help.

* Human, All Too Human: Fantasies, Incompleteness, and Underestimated Evolution :Philosophy:
Fantasies thrive on size, they feed off of it, they are but it. One builds himself that space for travel does one not choose to collapse reality. Nietzsche calls it the incomplete as the effective. Fantasy needs room to work it, so not realized thought, not realized philosophy, not realized action, not realized being, gives it that room to maneuver. Since it isn't closed, one can make a branch out of it, one can build from there. The exercise of completing the phrase, in summary, is the little game that the poet plays when he wants more investment, more attachment, more attention, more interaction, by the reader. Those poets should too take the opportunities they get to make their presence and their thought prohibited, restricted, limited, as to create the effect of "you want what you can't have". Even in books, when I get to those last 10 pages is when I actually start paying diligent attention to what the author has to say.
Nietzsche has amazing thoughts, but it's me who's reading it.
From evolution being underestimated results that you must finish what you started, can't expect others to close it for you, even if you are super close to achieving, others may reject what you have. I'm sure had Einstein left his reasoning for general relativity stall at a certain point, say 1913, no one would have finished it, for it presented such a learning curve, to fully adopt the idea of another human and make it your own when you know it isn't.

* Human, All Too Human: Abstract Art, Moral Stagnation, and Originality :Philosophy:
The phenom of modern abstract art, wherein what is has little place in people's minds, rather what means becomes the subject. The uglier is presented, there is less sensitivity to noise, and distraction, one looks through, one tries to read between lines. In music and paintings points out Nietzsche, says that the ear and the eye have become more intellectual. "The world is uglier than ever, but it means a more beautiful world than ever existed."
That we are convinced by out there morals to applaud the mysterious and reject the objective. People have become so good that now to get better one has to come up with higher abstract standards. Now the purely good is seen as predictable, dull, so to mask the lack of talent one comes up with new measures, but very vague ones, so that one does not risk them not stacking up. One refuges in the new measures to not lose progress, originality. Abstract art because no one gets it. Both the talented artist and the abstract artist have intention, but the former gives arguments too clear for why his should be recognized, whereas the latter tries to instill in the public's eye that his intention far exceeds what is objective, sensible, so over the top that mere painting mere blush, mere physical things can't explain. So one has to travel to a higher sense higher dimension to find the meaning. That's exactly why one doesn't sense that art. Hard to argue it has physical existence. You have doubts to distinguish a human from a philosophical zombie.

* Human, All Too Human: Bound vs Free Spirits and the Pain of Clarity :Philosophy:
That the free spirit devises reasons for his habits, and the reason of the bound spirit is his habits. The latter acquires them, while the former searches, be it for good or bad. One may be bound to tradition, to his country, to his time. When he accepts the imposition on him of what he is he is acting out of faith. Religion is basal for bound spirits, for it gives them all the things to adopt. Not much thinking beyond that required.
Nietzsche writes what I had in my head for a long long long time, or at least it illuminates me the way to this thought. That the free spirit has many more options which makes him less certain, less reliable, less willing to accept his role, cause he has none. He has no limits, hence unbounded. That is an advantage for the bound spirit, for he can know what he must do, he can choose how far he pushes himself, he can choose the finish line, get there, have purpose, achieve it and be happy. He knows himself. He has the courage enough to describe himself, to see his dream as a finite thing, to know the path ahead. To know himself, his strengths, weaknesses. So he is a spirit of action, he has great confidence in doing his thing and no more. He has great confidence to know what he wins and admit defeat where he loses. Very real he is. He can go tryhard for tests, leave his parents happy, and then when it's vacation he can enjoy his vacation too, as if all of a sudden he wasn't a beast at what he does. This confuses the hell out of me, for you see someone who you think also has that vision of greatness yet doing all this other stuff on the side that makes you question their mentality. That's where we are different. Yet one who has the power of this has to act the other way so as to it to make sense in the end. You could waste your entire life thinking greatly and doing pretty much nothing. Being the jack of all traits yet the master of nothing. Being vaguely great. But the potential is diminished, it is first principles vs zero principles.
The bound spirit can win the battle that he knows he can win. The free spirit can win the battle of the unknown, the battle that he doesn't know he is in. And so you see a lot of good people at what they do, but then you realize their ambition doesn't multiply exponentially exceed that, you are surprised that they don't consider going all the way through. They make a plan for themselves and if they do what they gotta do then they can also enjoy the cake at the end of it. They have clarity. They have a ranking of things all with finite points. The free spirit may have one thing with infinite points, one thing he could give everything away for, and so he is not constrained, he can forget the other things, making a lot of things contingent. The middle of the path, Nietzsche calls it.

* Human, All Too Human: The Middle of History and The Loss of Spiritual War :Philosophy:
There is this thing that I haven't considered, maybe geniuses are a transitory thing in society, as in certain phases of our evolution they may not be there, meanwhile I thought it was natural they existed throughout. Things are indeed transitory, there will never again be, or at least there isn't no more, that super hard and dense religious feeling. He then suggests that in this period of middle of history, which I think it always seems, since there will always be so much to figure out, there is this imperfectedness and all this turmoil of feelings, we are at the peak of chaos, disorder through the increase in entropy. Maybe if society pulls through and is sustainable in some form for a long long time, then there may not be material for thought, no drive, no hunger of more knowledge. No purpose. "We are the middlemen of history. Our great war is a spiritual war. Our great depression is our lives" (Fight Club). We don't have this kind of purpose no more, to put our lives on the line on war for our people. We've lost that into the softness of the now. Maybe further advances will make pointless the fight to get more money or whatever. Equilibria will be reached, and what that implies is stagnation of big trajectory altering events. There are no great wars no more. Maybe history will cease to make sense writing, maybe time will cease to make a difference. Everything would just come and go, again and again. Maybe then one could have the freedom to choose to recreate the conditions of a fargone time of history, like once I speculated that we may end, coming back to the trees and the hunts in the caves, but here you could choose between many versions, and feel like you have something to fight for. This also happens at our life's scale.

* Human, All Too Human: Equilibrium, Highs and Lows, and The Search for Truth :Philosophy:
If we reach the equilibrium, as in stability on all fronts, our life is good but doesn't have that spice, and looking back, that chaos was the essence, that which we did take for granted even tho it was the best of life. When you admit made it and live like so, now the job is done, there is some sadness to it, as you don't have those problems no more, don't have that urgency and worry no more, don't have doubt that it will all work out. But when you are living on the limit and it pays off, oh boy will it feel good down the line. I think I'd rather prefer to ever play this game of reaching highs and appreciating that yes, but the next day you wake up and you are back to zero. Once again longevity factors into this, if you are gonna live a long time then quitting from progressing midway feels dumb.
Argues that the pleasure in science is for those who do it, not the ones learning the result, and that going forward. So I say going forward, more results to learn, harder to give new results, and humans, to regain pleasure, will resort to error, illusion, fantasy. Philosophy and poetry will become usual, as one doesn't have such a learning curve.
That the advantage of having that science background is the ability not the knowledge or results, but he "learns how to gain his purpose purposefully". He expands this science pleasure thing further: the results of science then, and still now, give us curiosity for their beauty, but maybe it's because we are in the early stages. Maybe one day this beautiful girl that we chase may become elder, maybe the results will be boring, and then, without the pleasure, how will one find in himself the will to push further, when only truth is left.
And yet truth as the most penetrating of all richnesses. It's rare, it's valuable. Truth has no word to say on the appearance, or the abstract things we made up for ourselves. Truth subsides all that, truth breaks those all barriers. A man in search for truth doesn't move a single hair when confronted with wit, Nietzsche says, or seriousness, or professions.

* Human, All Too Human: The Ugly Soul, Unfiltered Art, and Continuous Becoming :Philosophy:
FROM ANOTHER bus travel:
That one would be limiting art too severely were him to reject the art of the "ugly soul", Nietzsche writes. That only the balanced, morally sound, achieved person doing art is boring, it's not spontaneous enough (wisdom of crowds I here add), it lacks in content, it lacks in authenticity, for it comes from a place of yes-saying, yes-doing, a place of wanting to say the right thing, a place of a lot to lose, and the censures implied by this status transgress into the affected freedom of thought. The erratic, from error, way, the nothing to lose attitude, it gives you the advantage of openness. The attitude that no matter how far you've gone, you are that beginner still, that student still, only transformed in time not in mind, that Poatan mindset that you are recurrently fighting for the championship, even if you already have it.
And in writing, journalling, you understand that you come to think that at present you are a work in progress, and in the past you are shit. This is for the same reason that when you read others' material you find it boring if their points are already solved in your head. The only thought worth thinking is that for which you have to fight for. Same thing happens in dating, you don't want to understand too plainly the other individual, you don't want to feel like they are just a phase of you in the past.
And The ugly part is the one that you remember. The one where you were sailing at rock bottom yet knowing you would fly, nonetheless exposing yourself to that moisty uncomfortable, bed-sleeping, feeling (falling to bed like a baby when you are working hard), which is facilitated by a hanging motion, a giving up action (as I have here and continue to lay out the philosophy of sleep.) The illusion of inspiration, as opposed to iteration of shit, and careful selection and mixing. Letting it out at once because you have been no fapping for long. Then one has the choice, to stay true to his art, even if no one is dancing to its sound. That art proceeds from the ignorance of the interior, and for that reason isn't reserved for physicists and philosophers. The self distancing of the status of genius, so that we don't have to compete within it, don't have to stand up to its height from our bottoms, for it descended from the "stars". "Perfect is admired. What is in evolution is underestimated." The era of the highlight.
I realize that then the "to search" section of my whatever this is called, maybe log of ideas, is actually to search inside me. I know what I want to hear and I just have to let it come through, no need to get approval from others in the internet wherever. Maybe that's why it seems that when you tell people you are evolving in some fashion they are like "Yeah, sure, whatever", it is seen as an excuse. But then when you come anew, when everything you said would do you have done, when the chips have fallen your way, there is a sudden 10-fold boost in respect for you. Don't assume they will just respect just cause you know them for a long time and what not, you gotta show them. That willingness to come back to blank state (estaca zero), to learn once more.

* Human, All Too Human: The Master of All Powers and The Silence of Truth :Philosophy:
That a single individual can hold eye to culture, that by holding, by petting, by controlling all powers the like of a great musician, great artist, great man of science, great athlete, great anything, has one got it in him to quiet the storm, to defuse the explosives coming on all directions, commanding the unleashing power of each of himself, does one become ultimate. One has built the overarching edifice of culture above him.
Here one too, with the wisdom of crowds, can read many books at the same time, and yet if he holds all these thoughts, if he just endures the pushing and pulling from all sides, if he still finds the courage to be one, he thy one be. There's the edifice above him, rising with the more one can stuff inside without coming apart in a blowing of spirit. One can thus reach the next handle, one can thus see the handles that lie high, one now is given the power of the connections on the graph, not just the nodes. Does one have the power to shut it all down, and yet to hear its fullest noises, what can't this man not express himself. In the limit once again, one would reach everything, everywhere all at once. What comes before it however, is the greatest silence ever assimilated by the like subjects. A silence not merely of sound, for one has internal blood rushing and flushing, not even merely of senses it shall be, not merely enough of thought, for one is aware, not just a collapse of space and time, nor of unconsciousness, or guilt, or protruding self-analyzing alternatives of actions, nor the dimming of every last light, nor the shutting down of computation in the meta-knows-everything embedded space of possibility.
Rather a stillness of truth, if one must call it that. A fixing of gaze defying all as of yet conceived. That those who went can't find the way to gather up the words to speak it, for they shoot too low, for they make it unjust, corrupt, inauthentic, the killer of depth. The reluctance to come back, the realization of purpose born out of the go past the point of no return, as much as past the escape velocity, acceleration, and all the infinite therein entails. The readiness to sweat out all the machinery synched into one great punch, the promise of alignment written into one singular output.
Untamed. Undone. The infinite compressed into the unfillable. The intertwining of the void with oneself and beyond. The unrepeatable, the relentlessly fresh, the thing that is to finite what finite is to permanent, the decision boiled down to the flip of a bit, the yes or no game of the all-untimely crudeness that nature presents forward, and presents with the greatest fiber of reality. The physically unrememberable yet omnipresent. What perhaps we forget as we rise into the desert of existence, and what we taste as we prepare to be engulfed into the eternal rest. One smooth movement is all there ever was.

* Human, All Too Human: Fluidity, Letting Go, and Fighting Entropy :Life:
All "Be like water" means is this. To have controlled the beast you've become, and manipulate it at will. To be able to, to expand the tiles of your piano, the buttons of your spacecraft, the colors of your painting, the formulations of phrases. The worlds, and the feelings, and the mindsets you can tap into without ever leaving your couch.
There is this hesitation to letting go, letting yourself go and become yourself. When it's about letting in, we let in more than we need, more than we wanted. Yet letting go with all its consequences, letting this shitty world forever for a better one... We are very hesitant to jump, afraid of falling. Confronted with whatever metaphor works best for you, maybe abyss, maybe handle to reach, we are slow in taking that leap of faith. And we know all about it. We don't allow ourselves to aim the highest at every moment because we have memory of the past, and not of the future, the same memory that allows us to build the scope of our aim at present. We are fearful of letting go friends that at present aren't in our best interests to be friends, places, in essence everything to which we have invested some part of our lives. Fight we must, this tendency of being tied to that to which we gave rope. Be careful the places you tie your rope to, be careful where you touch. There's a big big difference between being attached to your surroundings in the abstract wide high dimensionality space of things and not being so. Yet the line between them both is so fine one is reduced to a coin toss to decide it. Have then this option of shutting off that which you are part of, have the ability to forget every part of the universe causally connected to you. Fight entropy, gain the right to use your memory however you desire. Conjure up in yourself that which keeps making alive the life.
Fighting entropy in many ways. Relationships, your body, your mind, the company. You fear more the man you haven't solved, haven't been able to put into words what you think he wants. Allow yourself the liberty of not being fully known, don't put all you have in the light. Let yourself be the sole owner of everything you have, even if everything you have is also someone else's.

* Human, All Too Human: Marching Forward, Perpendicular Growth, and Independence :Life:
Why marching forward is of importance. Growth requires change they say. Ideally you would be outgrowing yourself constantly to become the highest form of yourself. Going beyond each phase you milk the shit out of, to understand them more fully. The things that trap us are the ones we don't understand, we only have of them the sight of the interior. We lack angles. Travelling places anew gives you more angles. Think of it as you wake up suddenly in a black space, and you want to know the space as well as possible. Well you must be constantly on the move, looking for new sights and crossings, both to expand your horizon as to clarify even more where you've already been through. In fact if you are on the boat to experience everything, in a well behaved space, you would want to go perpendicular to the space that you have already covered. This elicits the greatest stimulus, to go in totally independent directions. If you are however in this for other reasons, as almost everyone is, to the extent that it is also hard to go for the other option, then it is valuable to first search the space around you for better possibilities before you settle to build your house.
There's a great perk to exploding all at once in your utmost vigour and display of color, like suddenly putting yourself out there go on this rampage of conquering. Nietzsche poses this strong constraint to be free, that you have at least 2 thirds of the day for yourself. Interesting how much discrepancy there is between this standard and what has become usual nowadays.
That wisdom walks along age so you accept when your time has come. It as just a trick to make you more pleasurous. This another reason why natural, that meaning, comes from nature, which is no trivial of a fact.

* Human, All Too Human: Winning People, Adapting, and the Ego of the Reader :Social:
That there is always this partisan in a party that everyone inside the party demises. This shows your flaws in your presentation, for some people took it in ways they shouldn't have, and now he is a force against your partisan, for when he speaks he hammers the party's ideas. This force serves as a way to see how the fundamentalists of that party will be like. Most hilarious is that, a la Incredibles with Incrediboy, mostly these guys that end up screwing this up are the pedestalizers, the ones that idolized so much became so much fans that they became blind to other ways. They seem to be the perfect candidate, for they were born and grown around the party, and yet they come to love in such a way that it is even suspicious even to the very creators for they don't think they have found the ultimate recipe.
Winning people over is also about appealing to what they want. Persuading a brave person to do something means placing greater emphasis on the danger and risk associated with said action, if it's a person of altruism, bring that up, if it's a person moved by a fight against injustice, activate those buttons inside their mind by ringing up the volume of the correspondent knots. Since you have all these ways of adapting your personality, conduct, to the situation you are in, you then get stuck some but to that, so when you come back to the places, even tho you got way beyond that, it's harder to bring that new you to the old places.
A paragraph that could be added: 1345 - Ego of the reader. There exist brave enough writers to want their points understood. Some readers find it, however, an insult to their time and their finer intellect.

* Human, All Too Human: Imposing Thoughts, Partying, and Artificial Behaviors :Life:
When you have to expose that which you consume and impose that which you create you are closer to truth. Reading aloud is how you expose what you consume and writing down your thought is how you impose what you create. Reading aloud thus allows you to learn about your own character, says Nietzsche. In the first sense I laid out that is the case. But in a more literal sense too, for it makes you play the characters of the story, it makes you see more clearly what kind of character you are yourself, to which do you find natural saying their passages, to which do you adopt the words and not just read them. To which you believe what you are saying. Intention is very hardly hidden from plain sight when you speak aloud.
That partying is about pretending you belong there. This first stems from an adoption of what we consider fun from Hollywood, from pop culture. It is societal standard that at a certain age you start to enjoy going out getting hammered dancing getting chicks. It's made up, it's not natural. Few people at the start enjoy alcohol, just like few at the start enjoy only getting home at 5 am and feeling shit the next day, or somehow enjoy listening 3 hours in a row music at super high volumes with epileptic type lights and everyone banging each other in their own world because they literally can't talk to each other, either in their own world of thoughts observing the absurdity of the beast or trying to forget themselves in the sound of the music. This Hollywood thing makes expectations high, like there's epic scenes in movies about partying, and here I'm referring to this type of partying which I guess evolved from more natural types of partying, in an attempt to industrialize it perhaps. Like when people say oh my god this is going to be the most epic night ever. Do they think that is going to happen by drinking and going to the disco? I doubt it. If it's the best night ever it's just cause you surpassed yourself, either by letting go harder than before or doing great in social settings or with girls. Notice how fast some people go from all out banging music and rocking side to side, wilding and tearing, to complete normal in about 5 seconds as they get out of the club. Not genuine. Almost like they got paid to be an extra.

* Human, All Too Human: Judgement, Egos, and the Illusion of Mastery :Philosophy:
On judgement. The effects of judgment ought to be thought as the manifestations of our big egos. We usually don't like the judgement of others, cause they don't know us completely, even if they know things that we don't know about ourselves. Because they don't know us completely we feel like their judgement is too down to earth. Our egos make us think that others will have moderate success while we will have tremendous success. Each of us lives in this bubble of self confidence and faith that we are the chosen ones for indeed our experience shows us we are for we are the only ones we can guarantee even exist. So throw down the results. We also like to convince ourselves that they haven't us figured out, that we still have something up our sleeve, that they miss the point, that we don't say nothing because we don't stop our enemy while he is making a mistake. Enemies however usually know something about us that the closer ones don't, and we are annoyed, as Nietzsche puts it, if they disclose to the world this that they have seen from the other angle. We are likewise annoyed if someone enhances greatly one of our qualities, for we feel like they are neglecting the others. Even if someone says what we like to hear about us, which is usually far from what they say, for we have this ego-inflated vision about us, we are suddenly demanded to behave like that, we have this extra pressure. For people who don't have contact with us, and thus are much less biased in their assertions, we find them annoying for their description feels reductive, all too objective. We also, because again of our ego, hate when we are said to be alike someone else. We ask who is it that we look like, and we always disagree.
This realization is insane. That those that become masters at one subject probably are amateurs at all others, and yet are perceived as the reverse. Wow. The specialization of modern human is thus incentivized, range is not appreciated if not as a single thing in itself. The champions are these that mastered the subject. We think that because they can become great at something that they are great in general. This gives an advantage in low profiling on that which is becoming the masters of many things, if you can see through it and not give a fuck about this perception. The era of the highlight.
Who you like and who you dislike, your acquaintances, distract the conversation from ever finding ground to connect rightly. No, don't make friends with someone because of other people, some you've never even known. Winning people in conversation through putting the ball up there to be dunked. Allow them the opportunity to be funny and great, and they will follow you.

* Human, All Too Human: Socialism, Justice, and the Power of the Oppressed :Philosophy:
That socialism doesn't pose a problem of justice, but only of power. A problem of redistribution of power. We have nothing to say about the justice of the rise of the oppressed against their oppressors, which have made a business out of putting them down for quite some time in most cases. This rise however puts the first injustice part in question when the oppressed find a means of supercompensation to release their long-held anger onto the world, when they believe they now have the right to be put on top for some time as dividend of their downward ways. When the war they are fighting transgresses the oppressors. When they believe themselves as victims of the world, as fighting for their freedom, even if there are no oppressors to be seen. These figure the collective psychological consequences of poor treatment in stages of development, and can manifest as individual issues. This expansion of the war they fight into the neutral territories backfire, for a well-deserved hand was given but they are trying to take the whole arm. To blame far descendants of the oppressors is unjust. Justice has no past, no record of accumulating credit. Justice is about the now. There is no place for vengeance in a just present. Most importantly, we gotta move on to bigger issues. It's shameful to lose precious time on air, at home, at dinner, talking about woke, discussing these things that shouldn't be a thing in the first place. It's a problem that we created for ourselves, and how far can we really get by "solving" it?

* Human, All Too Human: Selfless Revolutionaries and Reverse Psychology :Social:
That selfless revolutionaries are more dangerous than selfish ones. In my opinion, the first ones have faith, and so their belief is much more fixed and independent of criteria, unnegotiable. For the latter however, no matter how deeply rooted they are, there is always a good enough condition that will make them step over their belief.
To use reverse psychology with the masses, which Nietzsche calls getting benefit from the counterpassion that is awakened by you emphasizing a certain passion. Gives the example that if you didn't wanted France to be more catholic as a German then you would hate Catholicism in Germany. Give them a stance so they can counter, because at the end of the day the business of war relies in finding reasons to explain the reciprocal hate, in the minds of the naive.
That the recipe for the great man of the masses is simple, he just has to be relatable and that in order to achieve something pleasant he uses an extraordinary will. So he has to achieve something pleasant and seem to have a great will. He then says, and this is the real hot sauce for which I wrote the last paragraph, that everyone admires will because no one has it, and everybody tells themselves that if they had it there would be no limits for them.

* Human, All Too Human: Worshipping Genius and The Annoyance of Normality :Philosophy:
He points out that the mechanism of worshipping a genius is much similar to that of Gods, and that when one does it, one runs into the risk of seeing the basic masses as more inferior than he should. This worshipping has at its core that the worshipper doesn't understand the possibility of the genius/God. He is left with only faith as the resource for fathoming, commensurating with what is understood this beast of the thing that remains elusive to our take on the world. In fact there is also this effect of dismissing the genius when you know what he does. If you think you could be doing what is doing, if by another token he is similar to you in that he plays at your level, be it through leisure conversation, or watching a movie, then you feel he is normal, you feel he doesn't fit the image of the machine you have in your head that could be yourself. This image is what you perceive as the great man of the masses. What you know you could do had you just given everything. Had you acted at the limit of what you know you can act. The omnipresence of this image of the will gives rise to the admiration of the great man, as you think he has that will, even tho no one does, for he himself finds in others the genius that he isn't. So obviously if you spend all time with one person, and you see he doesn't meet your expectations of the genius, you dismiss him as "yeah he studies a lot but he isn't a genius", for example. There must be room for fantasy, for you to put your part into the story of the genius that lives in your head, his perverse body just a confirmation of his existence.
By believing in the possibility of the genius, which is because you worship, you are disconsidering the masses who don't fit. You find them dull, you almost come to halt your belief that they are people equal to yourself. When you are so sure of the higher presence, normality becomes annoying, unrespectable, unforgivable. And here in all practicality you must maintain your gratitude and appreciation.

* Human, All Too Human: Happiness, Globalization, and Inner Strengths :Philosophy:
That there isn't such a thing as happy eras. That happiness is property of the moment, and that it isn't extensible. However miserable you once were, no matter its duration, you are condemned to expect the present, and therefore not continue that sudden happiness. Happiness is elusive and has no regard for the past. When you imagine that you will be "happy forever" after doing something, like in fairy tales, you started with the wrong foot. If animated movies had a continuation, then after the narrator says "and they were happy ever since" we would see a zooming out and after a while, someone says I'm hungry. And the moment collapses on itself.
There is this nationalistic way of wanting to preserve its people as representing the country, its boundaries being the colors or façons of their people. So avoiding mixing of races. If this is the view of some people, they find it disconcerting the consequences of globalization. The process of mixing happens slowly, but one day we may be one world, in fact it doesn't take that many generations for, with great probability, each of us being an equidistribution of the people's genes on earth. That will build the best humans. If these nationalistic gurus want to keep their sanity alive and well they will have to find other ways beyond language, or the façons of the people, to differentiate the citizens of the world. Perhaps one day there will be so much fluctuations that the approximation of a commensurable criteria that mostly separates the countrymen from the foreigners will fade, we will be left with the higher entropy state of everyone having a bit of everything.
That war rages inner strengths the like we will only approximate with the surrogate activities we found enchanting of this very power. War makes you feel alive. For that reason it caught even the most exhausted ones with some will to push on. Most people are alive and can't tell it, and that, as now wars have halted for our good sake, will be inevitable don't we find our battles to fight. So war is about territory inasmuch as it is about satisfying our desire. War is as much a nightmare as a ritual for our soul. Put a group of humans together and they will find some means to fight, are they still ashamed of this our nature. Else they'll do it like one has sex.
Another face of the ego. That we allocate more to criticizing that with which we don't agree than that to which we agree, the latter much more dangerous. We act in the way that maintains this view of our goodwill.

* Human, All Too Human: The Depths of Evil and The Matrix of Suppression :Philosophy:
That diving deep enough into something brings to light the bad in it. Thus it is hard to stay digging the same hole. Thus the deeper you go not only the less there are but also the more are coming back. Like a marriage, you may be advised to think twice before you know everything there is to know. In an unfair world like this at present there are some things you don't wanna know for your sake. In fact even those that we come across get sunk into our unconsciousness without ever seeing the light of the day. Things can get very disturbing out there. Staying on your lane is one of the few things we have learned in our walks. I bet there are things that would break your heart, break your youthful hopeful spirit. They would fill you with horror and disgust for what before found home. Going deeper may change you in ways you didn't want to. Maybe it's as harmless as about knowing the truth behind events in your life, gaining a new perspective on things that keeps you awake at night. Cause there is a lot that goes under this constructed surface. And the surface was made to protect you, not with the intention of lying to you. The collective being has repressed some of its wildest characters and forms. The rabbit hole goes deep, that I'm sure.
Something tells me that as much work as we put into building this surface is what we put into closing the evils of this world in the bottom. Cause you need to go to sleep at night. All do. But there have been billions walking on this earth, each compounding on a giant evil we have walked along ourselves. Perhaps not some evil you want to fathom alone, this evil that was built by millions upon millions. You are in such a safe space you don't even conceive of the wild stuff that is happening out there. Even if you wanted to escape the matrix, maybe you don't really, when you see what's out there. There are levels to this shit. There is a lot of sickness in nature. Like sickness you don't wanna know. Part of maturing is getting further in this awareness. Disconcerting.
This is another way in which the matrix is real. But we built it for ourselves. Again no creators, it was the development of the collective awareness that most people don't wish to go down there, most don't come back. It is one of the undercover genius inventions of our kind. It serves its purpose and we shall thank for not having to worry about the depths where the light can't reach choose we not to.

* Human, All Too Human: Paths, Annoyance, and The Boring Truth :Philosophy:
That it is the path that is taken that offers the most inertia instead of the destination. People are sure where they are going but too thinly know where they are headed. Don't underestimate the hardness of keeping a destination in mind through the paths. The hard part is not following through, but to keep in mind the end goal while doing so.
Annoyance as a physical illness. Yep annoyance mostly boils down to the state of your mind at the moment. We however usually find a justification for it. Is our demand satisfied we find another one. The problem is in not realizing this independency, and so giving in to your momentary desires.
Truth stays low not the most when it's dangerous, but rather when it's boring, Nietzsche says. Truth has this ruthless impartiality to it. It is or it isn't. Its search presents the least of guarantees to anything desirable. When truth is boring fewer want to tell its story. Its story gets lost in the ways of the imaginary things. People are there that given the truth of the world would never get out of the first stage - denial. It can get worse than there not being any truth, it can be boring to the point one can't find words or descriptions to elevate its status. The dragon scroll.
It is thus required a harsher sense of direction. It is harder to stick to such an ending of possible disappointment. It is not rewarding in the usual sense. Opting out midway is in all likelihood the smartest choice. It requires a selfless soul of the kind that gives away his life to reach the untamed waters.

* Human, All Too Human: Nature, Comfort, and Questioning Reality :Philosophy:
We say that we like nature because it doesn't have an opinion about us, says Nietzsche. For one, you definitely see this behaviour when people steal nature for themselves, talking to it and responding back in an attempt to believe what they want to. Such is summed up in the phrase "only you understand me", when talking to flowers or something. Thinking about it really points out how ridiculous it is that we do this routinely. We have to put some voice into something to hear what we wanna hear.
May truth turn us into animals again, Nietzsche asks. May irrationality hold the truest way. Fight club.
That we need consolation more so for that we were consoled easily than for the pain of death of a loved one. Nietzsche's appeal to some portion of the masses, is that he has these dangerous if followed statements. He shows us a very bad reality of what human beings do. By coming one with his words you perhaps satisfy your need to challenge the system. This is his way of covering truth, through the danger it represents. Much less popular would it be if he left it plainly and boringly. So he makes this trade, how raw does he really want the truth that composes his symphony be? And how many will dance to his music? What he then most likely hopes is that I see through the chords and into the fundamental melody, even if dissonant, that underlies it all.
We adhere to opinions in the name of our ego too, sometimes.
The first thought of the day, Nietzsche suggests should be whether we can give pleasure to at least one person in this day, instead of the usual prayer. The first thought of the day I believe should set the rest going. Win it and your day will become more meaningful. Win the first thoughts.

* Human, All Too Human: Deep Thoughts vs Popular Answers, and Finding Love in Actions :Life:
That when answering questions of opinion we don't give our opinion less we have given it great thought and let the deep rise to the surface, where we can fish it into our message. Mostly however, we are most worried about piecing the phrase together, and so, with this added pressure, we default back to giving a popular feels-right type of answer. That's why this space of writing lets you make your thoughts. There is no pressure. No time limit. No social expectancies hindering the way of pure thought thereof.
That we sometimes are annoyed at the world or others because we are annoyed at us. One must look out for this type of mistake. Maybe my annoyance at always going out was that I was annoyed that I wasn't doing much at home and then projecting onto the fault that it was because I was with others.
That in wanting to know something it would do us good to find our way into loving it. To assume its greatness as we dive into it, to give it such a headstart. This was for me the most useful in school, to do something very well you wanna love its cause, or learn to love it, so that you align yourself with it, you allow its collaboration. When I had to do a presentation on a book, this is exactly what is the most useful to deliver a great presentation about the book. If you say you don't enjoy the book, it's kinda hard to elaborate and have will to even write about it. However if you find the points that to you make it the best book you ever read then of course you will be in a better position to find greatness in it. This applies mostly to those places where you are given what to do. Then, complaining about not doing this or that won't lend you much far, that is, if you are really stuck to it.
