Then maximizing it is the proper goal. In the real world of possibility however, this allows you to choose what you want to do, by making you love the things you a priori didn't. It allows you to intentionally morph yourself. 

* Human, All Too Human: Cold Sobriety and Changing Convictions :Philosophy:
To have to carry out in the coldness and sobriety what you decided in the midst of passion, says Nietzsche, is one of the biggest burdens. This coldness and sobriety seems to me an inevitable non-zero measure interval of your experience. And in that you are not anymore backed by the naivity and relentlessness of the passion. Had you made too much ground when on your high, then perhaps you may find yourself bound when sobriety hits like a brick. Whether that means promising too much to others, in essence talking too much, lending yourself in fight for coherence over the trap you've made for yourself. Nietzsche argues that it shouldn't be a shame to change convictions midway, he wants us to furthermore become routine traitors of the convictions, so that we do not suffer unnecessarily from the burden of having to keep up with your past self's aspirations. I find however for me a lack of commitment to these past decisions. I have to much more respect these decisions and be bound to them, for the lack of pressure by rejecting them causes an implosion of the soul the like only cause of slowing down of the day.
That an enemy of he is, he who pursues convictions with no method. A form of fanatical thinker that gets excited with the first explanation of the unexplained that he comes up with. He declares it his own and runs with it. Very many died for their convictions thinking they were sacrificing for truth.

* Human, All Too Human: Truth, Ego, and the Ignorance of Dying for a Cause :Philosophy:
The human all too human enjoyer of life wants to have truth as long as it is sound with him, as long as he is the one reaching it. It takes humbleness in the devotion of truth, in that all you ever produced may suddenly have to be thrown away for something that another human did. You must then not be too bound to your works in the name of truth. I myself still do this quite a bit in philosophy, you get inspired and spit some great hypothesis but then you are hesitant in passing them through a rough filter in fear they may not come out as fit and worthy of truth. Truth can make your ego play limbo.
"Out of passions grow opinions."
That the limit of one who holds no convictions, has performed the infinite resignation movement, is to have only carefully calculated probabilities for each assertion. How could one then die for something? Dying for it implies the leap of faith that that which we are dying for is absolute truth. He who holds no more convictions cannot die for anything without acknowledging his mistake. Perhaps that is once more the bliss of the ignorant, that they can in all their right and innocence of mind actually believe they are dying for something bigger, that they are doing their part. They are by this means more likely to die.
* The Blind Watchmaker: Telos and Human Perception :Philosophy:
"As palavras são nossos servidores, não nossos senhores."
Acho que a finalidade das coisas (telos) não é parte da natureza, mas sim da nossa perceção. O telos faz parte do filme que fazemos do mundo. Nós buscamos o significado nas coisas, o porquê. O significado das coisas, tautologicamente, é o que dá significado às coisas.
* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude, the Highlight Era, and the Overhuman :Philosophy:
The era of the highlight, that today things amount to nothing unless a show is made out of them, Nietzsche says. Image becomes an ever more important commodity, a necessary and sufficient medium of truth, of belief, of idea. Truly ever more things you did in your life are more irrelevant if they weren't filmed. And yet we that in this generation passed through both scenarios have still this privacy to us, that no one knows what we've been doing all our lives, it gives so much space to achieve so much without anyone knowing.
I think we shouldn't neglect the brain drain that happens right inside our homes, with little children future owners of mankind watching tv shows that at times bless nothing onto them. Even worse for old people, that are allowed watching nothing bunched shows that drain life away. They in reality show that one doesn't need meaning to live, as the discussion of Camus made me think. One can just pass along not bothering much. When death becomes widely recognized as more avoidable I wonder what will be done to these people. Or to these shows rather.
Nietzsche says the way of the world is that it revolves around the creators of new values, but people revolve around the play actors and the show.
That which we recognize in someone else, we also inflame in that person.
There is a real sense in which the dilution of authority, responsibility, into the collective being, brings about a certain confusion as to the problems you face, and honorous and more generally value-driven people would abstain from playing this game. They'd rather die in battle than live with the mess of modern problems of which they have no clarity. They don't want to live for free if that means giving away their individual values. Thus one sacrifices even his life, thus one seeks the clear rules of "into the wild" a la Kaczynski.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Natural, After Taming :Life:
Being a natural, and following nature are worthy of admiration. The perfect innocence of senses, after they were dead-on and taken into your control, and then by immense control did you give yourself to free them. After taming all conquers of yours, letting them flow their new yours-given way.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Climb Past, Then Fly :Life:
This thing that adults and cooler teenagers who are living their lives to the fullest get to stay up till late, doing cool people stuff, this wave is now through me, and I am reaching the end of the ride. I confessed not to become this, yet see myself become the thing I sought to destroy, behold, now I present to you the overhuman, the overme. Coming back to truths stronger.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Love and the Creation Beyond the Self :Philosophy:
For the solitary one person around is too many, Nietzsche says, that it prevents the two of the solitary, him and his mind, to take the moment into greater depths. Thus I say company is enemy of truth. If right now what you wish is truth, all it takes and it takes all, is you alone. To be able to journey within into depths you are afraid to show another. There is no single one that knows you beyond the depths you are willing to go if you are alone, or rather with anyone you are not willing to go beyond certain depths. Truly being alone, that means being with nobody, not talking to Nietzsche, or who you are talking to in some of your free time, not having the company of society's mass produced memes. Truly yourself. Here can you search the big questions, and can you be free to do so. How much can you do that you can't do while with others? How many conversations there are no ears for in this world except your mind. How many places can you go only you comprehend. In essence how many limits the world places on your mind, that is what can be overcome as you stand with yourself. How much can you discover beyond the usual field of play you've been bound to. What would you actually do were you handed solitude for some amount of time? Those are some interesting questions. What voices are being muted by each bounding factor. What thoughts are better vocalized and more eloquently expressed when you are by yourself? This one particularly hits home for me. I'm much better able to produce ideas by writing or thinking by myself than when asked from people with people.

* On Silence, Frame Control, and True Questions :Philosophy:
At least be my enemy if you can't be my friend, says Nietzsche in somewhat these words. If no one is your enemy then you perhaps don't really matter, or don't do something big. Heard this too from Andrew Tate, and from polarizability in general, people want to see your action, whether they are friends and want you see you rise or enemies and want to see you crumble. There is of course bullshit and stupidity in Andrew Tate, but there is also things to be taken, value to be taken by going where others don't dare perhaps for the depreciation of those around them. Perhaps being dangerous and choosing to work as a team is a build of solid friendship. Making your friendship valuable through mutual knowing of each other's capabilities to end it.
Arrow and a yearning for the
Nietzsche here perhaps insinuating that in your friends you see you that must be overcome. Your friends show you how you must improve, for you see in them what you look like and what you have to improve.
Nietzsche seems to imply that everyone's good and evil tells us about their overcomings and their will to power.
Perhaps obvious but never by me said that this tablet of good and evil in every person which dress their will to power is the single most valuable thing in understanding someone and predict them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment :Life:
Still in preface: that to become immortal one has to die first some times in this lifetime. To push oneself to living the boundaries where trash and the unalive meet. To live everything everywhere all at once. To carry the wear and tear that only you know what, and only you will ever know unfortunately.
To host in oneself all that the immense machinery of control comes with. To be able to explode and take the explosions, to be able to look all the light in the eyes without becoming blind. Earning each and every one of the controls on your machine means pushing each to the limit. Knowing this limit. To know it you must pass it. This is a truth people hardly take time to understand. They are afraid of trespassing limits that may not even exist. How sad to live oneself blocked by others' limits. You gotta be comfortable with every single dial on your toolbox. Win yourself. Push the boundaries further.

* Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment II :Life:
That the rejection of this first world is of a nihilistic nature. The ubermensch is made to go through the human all too human. The neglect for this first occurrence as the disease of the unholy. The wholly mistaken. The finding of the ubermensch through the true respect for our nature, that which abides the rule of selfism. Not looking up but looking at ourselves to surpass ourselves. Not finding proxys to our problems, but fixing us. That our meat is the evidence of the existence, and our blood of the alive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- AI, Memory, and the Death of the Internet :AI:
Will to power perhaps manifested as this playing of the game for more control. But perhaps still just a particular case. Will to power as will to Schopenhauer as the world.
That the living value much more many things than life all they have. Our will to power means nor dominate the upper visible world. Has more power who shoots lower and hits the more fundamental things. Like changing the state of mind of people. Or further along that axis changing their inner beliefs. That is something that no war or event will win over. Further comes changing their world view. As in they can't see the world another way. Imposing on them your will, a sort of second occurrence of the world in their lives. Humans at the in so far seen peak of the expression of nature. We are nature's best attempts to understand itself, to assert itself. Our will to power the most explanatory of the life forces of the force of nature.
An infinite movement of affirmation, self replicating at all scales. Self similarity in everything all together.
At the level of our lives, affirming our nature is the will to power. This solves the non existing riddle of existence.
Our ignorance decaying as probing our teaching. "We teach the teaching". We have to solve this self recurring puzzle that we are solving and making all at once.

* Quotes and Questions: High Bandwidth, Searching Vast Spaces, and Long Term Vision :Technology:
Two types of people emerge when the demon of eternal recurrence casted his spell. Those that would get infinitely annoyed and beg to forget the existence of that almighty weight question. And those that would thank equally infinitely the daemon. For some it reduces their life. It makes their life rescuable. For others, it makes them see the shape of their misery, and make their life unrescuable. No salvation can be attained doesn't one want to save himself. Hesitation behold the daemon shows lack of commitment to life. I'M ALL IN
This oxford book with so many distractions, so much fluff. Chronologies and rumbling about nonsense. Mate, it seems attractive at the time to read these things. But just think, do you rather read the introduction to the work by a random oxford scholar or instead of that read another great book that you haven't read. Cause there will be loads of books like the latter that you won't be able to read. Assume it, know it. It's then about increasing the average quality of your time. Making every bit earn its way onto your thought.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Elevation and the Godlike Will :Philosophy:
The new god as the body as the sense of the earth. To not stay true to our real mother earth as the new sacrilege. The body reveals about the soul, it commands it, not the other way around. Thus let the body speak. The body as the temple. The life as God. Nietzsche let the body speak, through all his intimacies of the body and mind, of illness, of hunger, even his collapse. One ought to align body and mind, after all one is restricted to the body, as of now. God's domain ends where the body ends. It is possible that the body will be the hardest thing to replace and automate. Perhaps not, perhaps in 50 years we can upload our mind to digital systems. But there is a chance that the body is the last thing to go away. The last human piece one can identify in the world. The last refugee of hope for the anti-progress and anti-technology.

* Quotes and Questions: The Champion Has a Name :Life:
The body is still the best piece of engineering and architecture, the best autonomic system ever conceived and built, the most expensive work of art, the most prolific machine. This truth can easily fly over one's head. One can search himself everywhere but in the body, where all that he is lies astill. We do not want to admit the greatness that our body presents. We are now evolving into selfism. To play the game one has to play along the most reliable system, that which runs ourselves. It is thus wisest to cultivate the body high up, for you don't know if there will be another such of controller of your player. Lose contact with the body and you risk becoming NPC. Perhaps here is too the greatest freedom. To free ourselves from our controllers. But it's a Pascal's wager, and all humans as of today have lost that bet. All of humans till now have had to live in their own body. Furthermore paramount is the importace of being in charge of the controller, so as not to get played. If you can't get rid of it, you might as well own it. Each one has Jesus inside him, the on-earth god's presence. It's his body, and even for the unfaithful ones, those that are only content with a higher presence, that higher presence has only existed in the depths of his earthly body. Those that don't trust enough in the power of their fiber. If you can't beat it, join it. Maybe the body, the so previously despised piece of unpure meat, has everything codified in him. Has all the marks of creation. The search within could be our ticket to paradise. Maybe our body plays a part in consciousness, we are just too weary of associating the two, one cruel and old, the other transforming and fundamental. We have been lied to, people. We lied to ourselves. The first stage is denial, denial that we are stuck to this thing, this random body of ours. This being a stunning realization dates back. We've been through it all, now beyond accepting it we must leverage it. We like to think our minds play dimensions above our bodies. But again, we are the controllers as much as the controlled. A brain alone isn't able to reproduce, to survive. Long ago this the most decisive of our species's treaties was signed. The symbiosis of mind and body.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero's Last Self-Deception :Philosophy:
To be clean with the dirt rather than dirty with the clean.
The greatest experience not that programmed experience, but the greater unprogrammed escape from all programs.
Not you not doing the bad, but you not trying, is the real sin behold the heaven of life. You owe to the gift of your life, the gift that your life, not god, gave you, to get the very best of it. Your life has eternal love for you. It could have been handed to you a perfect life, but life wanted you to have free will, it wanted you to be yourself, it gave you the chance to escape every closure, to be once free, to feel at once alive. The pressure could be immense, one might imagine, the angst unfathomable, from this unmountly weight that rests on your will. But rather there is zero pressure. Life set you free to set you free, not to expect of you something. What if not life has the power to create everything, your everything, people underestimate massively the value of their life, of their experience, of their consciousness. This brings us unite to the best thing that ever happened to anyone of us. Thus don't die, paired with live, the best way to pray and honor our gods. Suicide as the ultimate sin, but not really, just a way of living. Again everyone of us has that transistor in us, most of us don't choose to activate it. But don't think you shouldn't have it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Every Hero Must Go Through Their Own Journey :Life:
What is ironic and curious about the role of the Zarathustra is how he needs to "go under" - Nietzsche writes, like the sun. In essence a movement of coming down to the level of those who are looking so high up. Looking so high up that they can't find the next step up. The grounded ones after having risen have to descend from the place where the bounded are stuck, to the place they occupy, unknowingly too. Accepting that they can conceive the building, trusting that all they have is all they need, and not all they want. It is those that conquer the going under that can one day go over and across. Those who can sit comfortably with the lows, that end up reaching the highs. Those that fear not the ground, and so don't hesitate risking the jump further. Those that can look at their great height and remain unaffected. The affected ones will see too big of a loss coming down. So they mostly stay. Those that don't require a higher cause to go lower, to go under. Those that can go down, not knowing if ahead lies the path up. The ones who can't accept going under are condemned to be confined in the gradient ascent, be stuck at local minima.
He wills his going under, having no guarantees on crossing the bridge. This unselfish nature of the movement, this fluidity of movement within the arena, no hesitation to go above or below, a mobility only the free spirit possesses.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Desiring Both Coherence and Self-Actualization :Philosophy:
I literally cannot believe this that just happened. UNBELIEVABLE. I was editing the most fluent melody of my life. As it should be, time should above all make you more fluent in your thought. Then I lost everything I wrote for the until then most inspired 20 minutes of my life. All that I wrote made this loss much more valuable, I became an accomplice of myself. Only I know what was written there. Only I have the formula. Only I can get there. And so HERE I sit sacrificing my sleep to salvage what was taken away from me. I reconstituted my thought, still adding more, in the then most inspired minutes again of my life. AND as I had reached most of the points, in my perception, I deleted again everything accidently. It is hard to know so much technically was lost. It was 1 hour of writing, that I hope to reconstitute the stronger in one hour more. One shouldn't be searching for the words I found, but for what they point to rather. One is unaffected to lose one's words, but it becomes harder to maintain lassitude when one loses one's thoughts. And still I'm grateful for being given this environment where I'm submitted into learning the hard way not to be attached to thoughts. And even the more reconstitute them. For some reason, even though making is harder than remaking, remaking feels much more strenuous. Much more slave work. You are confronted by the greatness of our creation, and so come to fear it, fear yourself, that you are only as persistent in time as you allow yourself to. You narrate yourself the same tho changed person through time, and still doubt your capabilities at present at times to supervene those of your past. And yet again this obligation to start ground zero. It drops your ego, it forces you to remember, oh such misery of the modern! It polishes thoughts in no way that an unselfish nature could come himself to do. I am very attached to my thoughts, I am annoyed if I forget some bit. I'm not courageous enough to erase thoughts, to be able to give away your product. Selfish with it that you want all of it for yourself. This comes about FROM the notion of going under, the movement of going under and yet not be tempted to go over others remembering them you are going under.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- From Smallest to Greatest: Same Flesh :Philosophy:
A silent going under. Not complaining for the suffering you impose on yourself. Not being loud, neither through the high nor the low. Being able to keep more than what is promised. Being better than what is required, better than what is recognized. There is no outside force nor pressure. All that you do comes from within and within solely. There being no question to answer as to why you do what you do. If people asked less why they should do this or that they would get more done. Cause again the exposure to all options make you much more selective, much more exigent of what comes in. And that should be, but it should be after you conquered your will, after you are able to go with everything at something at any time. Finding the end in things themselves brings about a peace the alike are missing. At times people start with means in mind, they start impure so that they can do what leads them to purer ways. Like starting to workout to get more girls. Or studying physicists to think of oneself that one is smart. But then you gain a seriety, a peace, a control, a freedom. The way of the flow. It becomes second nature, and most, it becomes natural. Natural as leaves falling in the autumn time. Natural as incorporated fully into your soul. One thing about reconstitutions, looking for words makes them much worse. One forgets to think and tries only to complete his words instead of completing his thoughts. We are so attached to thought that we are annoyed to lose them, even though thoughts are not forgotten, least temporarily. They will come back. But only when you accept their loss. Only when you become unidentified with them. Only when you don't try any longer to replace them or find them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- When Reading Yields Nothing :Writing:
Only when you go look better. Going under unnoticed purifies the soul. It makes all narrations unnecessary. Justifications only weaken the cause. They usually strengthen, in the everyday world, but that is reserved for those beings that are still not sure of their cause. Those that still have room for doubts. To those to whom the movement doesn't come off naturally. Those who still haven't entered the continuous stream of flow. A floating motion in a straight line. All else is stopped. It brings you to reality, or reality comes to the surface, you tell me. Truly, only here can you see that all back there in your last life was not. Yes, you abandoned that life the moment you entered flow. We only find it real because the bound perhaps find jealous in the unbound, and so try that anyone can't make the movement they couldn't.
You can still allow parts of yourself to pretend to believe that what was back there was any bit real.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- When Reading Yields Nothing II :Writing:
Above all it's the silence. The stress that existed is no longer. Anything that worried you shows its real appearance. The monster that kept building and building while you didn't interrupt that past gone simulation is now shrinked from this perspective. Its deceptive nature is no longer. You are still. You are alive. You know better. When you entered flow the clock stopped ticking. That world stopped. All mouths stopped moving, all hands stopped shaking. It required you, to puxar a corda do carrinho de corda, dar à manivela so that it kept moving. It needed your commitment to fuel its reality, its alivedness. It needed your attention, our consideration. You have escaped. Now you can't look at it with the same eyes. The conspiracy of the bound. And they are many. There is so much bound simply because people take this whole, or hole, too seriously. Rarely do you see someone who plays back with the same level of absurdity and irony to this Universe of ours. Rarely see someone that is courageous enough to believe the fragility of it all. The monster is all screams and amplification.
I like this test of character I gave myself with rewriting the same 3 whole times, like the Romans won wars, simply by coming back the same. Unaffected. Still. Not any longer sensitive to the frequencies of the unreal. All the noise has shut down. Nobody realizes the traps they set themselves in by believing in the reality of this world. It's not that deep. Yes you are free.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Masses' Inherited Power :Philosophy:
Tired.
Being tired is another such illusion of those who haven't found the way. Tiredness is reserved only to those who are doing something while thinking. It's all a misconfusion of acting and thinking. Being in the flow doesn't let outside thoughts creep in, doesn't let things of the past life. It is what I can only describe as the vacuum. No sound can any longer lead you astray. You are the master of your music at your every symphony. There are two types, when faced with the vacuum. Those that become surdos, and those that become composers. Being tired is a disease for the unconquered ones. Those that can't conquer because they haven't conquered themselves. Proxy of truth is a bitch. You can see when something was looked up and searched up, and when something came from within. I can be here writing forever, I believe. To stop is only out of shame. For wanting to come back. Still the point lies still. No sleep or to do can play with the strength of the flow. People should tune better their frequency range, not to take in some magic frequency there over the top, not the wholly holy great to be found there, but instead in the frequency of silence, the most unused of sounds in music. The most unheard sound. There in the depths where here meets now, is where people can't gather themselves to be. It's a fuckery to pretend one doesn't see reality, and perhaps even forget of this pretending.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Those Who Want to Punish Cannot Live in the Present :Philosophy:
A self sufficient acting. It needs no dressing, you learn rather to have a naked will, without reflection. Nothing external can deny its fiber. This going under of the likes of acceptance that others confound you as bound, for you walk among them.
He who wills his perishing, devoid of ego or self to latch on to. He who knows he can handle more, and even gets frustrated when his demands of perishing aren't met. If you want to lose as much as much as you can take, then this is for those whose losses in this world aren't enough to take them down. Beings so sure that all the evil of the world can't face front to them.
Being able to perish even in the smallest thing, thus being able to improve even from the smallest thing. If you had only access to little things then you'd want to milk the hell out of them, you'd be able to walk on all levels. In fact the simplicity and ease of that which you are presented with allows you more room to go over by going under, as your resources would still be full for it. It takes more will tho, more intention and more bravery of going under, as there is no obligation. We from young associate will with obligation, with surmounting the obstacle we see right of us. Quickly should we realize that all those obstacles were made by other for us. The quicker we start making the obstacles for us the quicker we become autsufficient in our improvement. AGI take notes. A priori. In escaping your environment. Now you are empowered, you can become a warrior without ever leaving your home. Your mind takes over the world as the fountain of resources, experiences. Selfism. Yeah well guess what your imagination outpowers this world, it is as great as you want it to be. All problems are crafted. So create the problems for yourself to improve. Thus you have to know yourself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming Builds the Height to Fly :Life:
Now this thing of writing down on kindle is accompanied by this ritual after reading and writing of collecting our writings into One note. It allows for some awareness on how much was done. Before of course that was also a thing, in fact it was such a thing that it could even prevent us from going further, we were frequently checking how big the paragraph was, and that at the subconscious level may play the role of hindering us, by either conceding that we had enough or by letting the image of ourselves dictate when it was time to stop. For those occasions you wanna again have the greatest self image possible, but now I can give myself the luxury of losing myself in the moment. Kindle also doesn't have Time clock, it's the perfect recipe for some natural flow. The human as the one able to hold contradictory thoughts. That's why you can sleep, that's why you can live the day with no worries. We are the owners of experience. That makes us the lords of ourselves. We really choose what we experience, and taking this further, I wonder if everything you've ever done, everyday you've ever had, has been previously imagined by you. From the simplest to the most sophisticated. Having the idea, having the belief is the greatest boundary to our capabilities.
Your standards at many times make your movement more viscous. If you have such a high standard for writing, then you may never start, same for talking to girls. Chillness is found in lowering your standards.
And in reading these works I lower the standard of what I understand on what Nietzsche was really trying to say. I give way to what I'm trying to say. Cause that I at least know is something.
Much of the work we do is for convincing ourselves we are worth of this or that. To build the self image we can have by being more direct and looking inwards. We sort of distract ourselves from ourselves.

* On Talent, Equality, and Winning the Moment :Life:
You know what's more than a believer? A team of believers. The inner circle thing in MMA, where you build each other up. The collective intelligence arising from this allows for some great endeavours. Hence why companies are teams. You can make things into reality which were previously inaccessible, the collective imagination brings on wider fruits.
And now Zarathustra urges for the human to have a goal for itself. To plant the seed of highest hope he says. I say to carve into reality the highest you can imagine, conceive. For what's already not carved into reality is hardly ever grown from it. We shall not wait for chaos to go away, for stability. For there so small will be the fluctuations that change is lost, and what has been built beforehand is what stays to be. Take advantage of the current all inducing all erasing chaos to not be at peace, to not be at equilibrium, to go further, to go where you'll no longer can go when stability hits you like a wall, when all the entanglement and spreading of you in this world has made you so defined so as not to risk disaligning with this definition. Regret minimization framework. It is of the essence of nature, namely 2nd law, that this equilibrium comes after the chaos. And so the urgency is now. As I said, every here moment is the most defining moment of your life, there is no rest in the middle, only at the end a la Kobe. This again sets the pace so high that it is now a challenge to keep up. Most use their ability to hold contradictory thoughts simultaneously in order to silence this thought.
This is inasmuch for the human as it is for the man, that's where I was going. You are now old enough to do something but young enough not to know yourself. You are the god of this life if ever there was any. You've explored enough to know what's up, but you still have speed enough so that you can build the highest tower. This life you start off at infinite speed, and decrease exponentially your speed as you explore. So be quick to escape the matrix, and then quick to do what you want to do. This life doesn't allow for much more than half a dozen doings. Think like that and you shall produce.
So you should love what's urgent, we like what gives us more time to decide, even tho it only takes away our time. Makes us slower for the weight it carries in our memory.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Human Sees the Power of Spirit :Philosophy:
The power of not knowing is not here for very long. Naivete Is a scarce resource. It's much the opposite of what we usually think. Knowledge is power. But not knowing is a greater even power. And not knowing is different from knowing not. While you still know little take advantage. You will never know less than you do now. That's the gift and the elusiveness of the moment. Change while you still can. Change big while you still can change big.
I say fight the second law, I say that's not giving in, I say that makes a life full of memories, and memories honestly are one of the best things when the rest comes, in the end, not in the middle. Be grateful for the specialty of this moment. If you were to wake up in a random time of your life, almost surely there would be less power for you than there is now. Less speed. Less fluidity. Less change. Less imagination. Man, the more you are exposed to the world, the more you believe its reality, so it's harder to escape it, and the less you believe in other worldly things. Induction is a bitch. You will without you even noticing start to believe the sun has to go up everyday. The more you see something, the more you believe it, same with the world. The more you see, the more you have to see to believe. Believe that there is but perhaps a tiny interval in your life where you will have the greatest producing power. And if that interval is right now, do you regret the heights you reached. Your vision will deteriorate into clouds, there will be an opening you've seen enough and see clearly enough that you can make some explosions happen. You can leave your trace in the world. You can make the world see a bit into your world.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as Tool, World as Experience Beyond It :Philosophy:
The last human. The last human is one for whom it is most clear that he is alone in the world. The previous still believed otherwise. But he can see it, and he will have no reason to do anything but for himself. What will he do. Will he still leave a trace, he that knows that trace is regardless untraceable, will he talk, he that he knows no ears will listen. Will he dare to imagine. What will the last human do, he that has such an opportunity yet only one life to carry it. Taking in all mankind, what would he exhale?
What will the last human do, he that is standing on the shoulders of giants, but can only build on top for him to stand on his shoulder. And at last isn't mankind the last mankind?
Interesting how it's in the middle that the proposal to do something is born. I always had this thing of all day reading day, never coming to be. But 90 minutes after starting it becomes an option. A very rational one. Aligning with your thoughts.
The last human will be confronted with statics. Some kind of silence of all kinds (actual sound, movement, distractions, ) that we as of yet are unable to hear.
Still it's interesting to do this one day reading and writing, so as to value the day, see how much was done and produce in the units of reading and writing, so as to compare all others with this one. So as to have a standard. Well the conditions are met. It's a rave then. It embodies these principles that I have been referring to, like now is the most decisive moment. It's like saying, let's turn it up today, let's put the bar of where we are so high so that we can only go up from that. Give Everything now, more later.
Maybe this is how urgency starts. Maybe how taking in all we have and exhaling it at every moment. Manifestation.
You are limiting yourself if you just play with non contradictory thoughts. Your machine should have all the buttons.
The last human lives the longest, he may come to live the slowest, for he has no speed to compare to. Mankind's last thoughts may span years apart. He lives in the silence. When he sleeps, mankind raises its arms ready to celebrate its comings. With every step they celebrate more their extension. His world becomes a play of imaginations. The last human hardly has a grasp on reality any longer.
The last human is divided in being either the most important human ever, and being watched by everyone, and being the least important human ever, and being watched by no one. Perhaps the easiest way of being the last human is to pretend you're not, to live like you're not.
I guess the last human is much like a cat at home alone. He goes by instinct, he is free to flow into the nowhere he seeks. Then he sleeps.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- First Hear, Then Resonate :Philosophy:
Being tired is a decision. I don't get tired. It's a consequence, not a cause.
Parents have a big role in printing self belief onto the kid, loving him like he is special for him to understand he is special.
I am not in a hurry to have a car. I am not in a hurry to have a watch, I am not in a hurry to have all that I know I'll have. I am in a hurry to increase all that I'll know I have. I am in a hurry to build what can't escape me, and what I can't escape, so as to live happy whether I have what I can not have or not. One shouldn't hurry himself out of being a child. One should enjoy and prolong to the maximum what will be taken from him. I will have tremendous amounts of time to be an adult, to be an old person, to find all the materials I want. This is what is frugal, this is what is valuable, don't throw it away because you want to tell others you've made it. Make it big.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Brings the Moment to Life :Philosophy:
This decision possible to be made of stopping your intellectual journey at something, this guy I heard saying Nietzsche is where he stops, he wants no more, he will absorb and put out. Cause perhaps even involuntarily people stop their progress in areas at some point, content with what they've found and move on, that is natural but still striking, as in to reject the possibility of going further. Attempting an ultimate analysis, a perfect finish. This gives you an urgency tho that's for sure. It's now or never. All the questions you have about this, either you answer them now or you'll never know. We play a lot on these secret little hopes that we are not brave enough to shout out loud for we are not sure or don't wanna confront the fact that what is done is done and that there is no coming back. Freed from desire.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Overcoming :Philosophy:
The last man, that which contempts with superficialities. That which sees no problem in being fine with a superficial explanation.
That which it's the same, he sleeps the same way at night knowing the truth or not. The conforming spirit, the spirit that could be thrown into any world and remain indifferent.
Last man because after this there is no further evolution. It marks the end of change. The last evolution. Again the unawareness of this state of things is what makes it lethal, that no one will question it. It will be the last piece in the heart of mankind's machine, matter of time till all the livelihood is gone.
Zarathustra not being understood, being stuck in a space and an age where he couldn't be understood. He thus went under, doing so for the hopes that somewhere out there someone would receive his message, hoping that the ubermensch was to be born. The next evolution of mankind, the dawn of the overhuman.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willing the Thinkability of All Beings :Philosophy:
The first step of the three transformations of spirit, to be able to bear the weight of weighting down the ego. To become independent of one's ego. To carry in going under all the wrong this world puts on to you, to reach great heights inside the world. To play the game and win. To play the game everyone knows and win. To press on. To become unmoving to the low blows, to conquer one's mind. To thus become oneself by not fooling oneself. Then the second transformation comes, the spirit after gaming the game and ending the game's game, breaks the game. Revolts against what he very much needed previously. He becomes independent of his mind in the game, and then becomes independent of his game. It surpasses the master by not just being who he is supposed to, but becoming himself. He no longer only conquers his mind, he creates, he adds onto it.
Sort of like the thesis, the antithesis and the synthesis. You throwing away your whole game, to conquer exactly the opposite you were before, and make perhaps the even harder movement of coming back to your nature, after having neglected it. First he does as the will of another, then he wills what to do, then he wills his own will.
At sleep you must reconcile yourself, at day you must overcome yourself. Ten truths you must discover during the day, spoke Zarathustra, so as to not leave the soul hungry. If the day you don't overcome yourself the night will be hard. You will perish, for will will come to you to do something, now the most that you have to give away, now that you have to declare the day a day. Now that you must face with yourself this day that you just processed. Regret is the enemy of sleep. I sometimes used to deviate this will of becoming to listening to music, diverting the regret into assuredness about the tomorrow's truth discovering journeys. He preaches one also must get in his dose of laughter. In general what you want for life you'd want to do in a day. What makes you lay rest at the end of life, the same things which are necessary for you to be at peace, for your soul to be full, death the biggest reconciliation, what of life if not the greatest overcoming, for here one must accept that that's his life, the more painful and reluctant the moment the more he feels he didn't live. The less he overcame.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will the Greatness of the Universe :Philosophy:
And I should value more the time with my parents. I am out here substituting that for my overcoming, but I will be here for a long time, I will have time to do this and much more while they are not here, most people regret not spending time with those they love, so I must win. I must go and win, for then at least the sacrifices are somewhat justified. A more riskier way of living, incorporating your family too.
Immortality of the self is one great illusion of this life. One thinks he is chosen not to cross paths with evil. One takes for granted his health and well being. Nature is cruel tho, it takes out the greatest and the poorest of soul the same way. One should keep humble about being alive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming the Creator of Worlds :Philosophy:
You are a champion before people recognize it, you become a genius way before anyone puts that title onto you. You are rich way before having billions. You are the best man before having a girlfriend. Become first what you want to be to be that. This aspect that it is only a surprise for the people in the outside. You know your worth and if you say it out loud people would laugh. Overcome. Prove that your frame is stronger, that your world which you are about to share has more will and representation than their world.
It's this motherfucking thing where the system tires those that wanted to do something, tires them so as to submit them away from their ideas, that they forget the great things they set out to do. They then spend their extra time doing non sense, instead of going in for the real things. This I thank my college for showing me, and also that you have to act immediately on these things you want to do.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Humans as Masters of Making Things Understandable :Philosophy:
To sleep soundly one must be at peace with god and with his neighbor, and also devil, says Nietzsche. In essence one should be at peace with the immediate, with the above, with the below, with one's aspirations, with one's failures and successes, with one's being, with one's not being.
A fair part of this world is first impressions. As I read with a weak mindset, as I continue reading Nietzsche, if I read and don't find the impulse, the spark, then reading again these portions won't cut it. The spark was deaden, the wave function collapsed, ain't much to do about it. And it's unfair that with people first impressions are so vital. That they sort of forget the rest if it doesn't align with that first impression. Of course they notice change, totally, but first impression still carries a heavy weight.
And this aspect of definedness entails to an inertia in personal life for example. The more you are out there the greatest pressure to maintain what you defined. So if you know a lot of people at a particular place, the more the image that you have built weighs, the harder it is to change it.

* On Life Affirmation as Practical Course of Action :Life:
Again the idea to my liking admittedly that the voice coming out of the healthy body exudes more truth, that the healthy body is more embracing of a clear mind. The mind body symbiosis, having alignment in spirit. The hard wired codification of the soul in the body, that the body holds the information of all things we deem superior, be it soul, god, transcendent experience, thought, consciousness. People are again afraid to believe that this unpure piece of meat holds it all. They underestimate, they want something more, they deny that you are what's inside the dragon scroll. Selfism. "There is more reason in your body than in your finest wisdom". There is more truth, more practicality, more coherence, more sense, more direction, more meaning, in your body than in any of its over the head inventions. The body is still the greatest technology ever put together in this world. All kinds of improvements can be made, but the substrate of the body still reigns the greatest success, the greatest love story, and it has been here for a long time. These people way back had access to this beautiful machine of ours. They did. Thank the body thank the self be grateful for it.
The last man mindset and the cause of the last man society. There won't come a time where man loses its sense for change unless that thought is already implanted into us. Zarathustra finds that his hell of the last man is the heaven of many, that they aspire to be this last man.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Fortifying Beliefs :Philosophy:
Is it me or we have ourselves the illusions that we are living collectively? Sure there are degrees to sociability but most of the people you talk to you didn't plan on talking, it simply happened that you both crossed ways. Like you didn't meet with them on purpose, rather you saw them and rolled with it. There is a certain shame in going with someone even tho you didn't meet, but I guess it would be too burdensome to invite so many people to this or that.
Do not doubt the messages your body sends you. Try to hear your thoughts through your pains and things to "coçar", your appetite sounds and your "bocejos". Do not underestimate the information preserved in the chills you feel.
That your virtue, and again it is better to have one virtue than two, one ought not to reduce it to other's common name virtues. Taking your virtue as a whole, unique and whole and raw. Not trying to decompose it, to project it onto the written names, that makes it lose its unicity, its impossibility of being cloned, its value. Make your virtue inhabit your body, embody it, make it the forthcoming of a likewise unique and unreplicable body of yours that you own and name.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drives That Are Contrarian and Inaccessible :Life:
Passions turned into virtues. You going from feeding on these bodily sensations, in reality they were signs that there was virtue to be attained. Your body smelled there was something it could say, something it could add, something it could be. It made you follow the passion until you then understood you had virtue for it. Each virtue demands all of you.
The I is for me something to be overcome.
The interplay between your thought, your action and the image you have of yourself, your ego. How strong is each, how flexible is each. Which commands which? Does the image of yourself dictate everything you think and do? Are you however in control of what the image of yourself implies? Having a big ego is vgood if you want the results of what that ego demands of your thinking and actions, it's bad if it controls you, if it demands things of your thinking and acting that you didn't want. Manifesting is really just tapping into these higher forms of having control on yourself. You can have control on your actions, but if your thinking controls you then you are still a slave. You can control your thinking, but if your beliefs control you then still a slave you are. What's the next step?

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Choose With Whom You Cross the River :Life:
And the burden of the inconsistency between beliefs and thoughts and actions, is a hell of a weight for one to carry. So at times we may deny ourselves to preserve consistency, rather than change ourselves to achieve a new balance of self. We may have a bodily sensation that outrights conflicts with a thought we have, and with thoughts others have, and so deny this madness in its pure form, perhaps adding some bits taking some other bits so as for it to make sense in our head. If you had the will of killing someone, says Nietzsche, you may have now the pressure to rob them, for it would be madness in light of the experience of this world to kill for no other reason. Or the all present abstractions of love for example, that you may think you are in love just because you had some sex drive towards somebody. All abstractions to preserve the image of good people we have ourselves. Or to preserve the idea of a fair civilized world, or a world that makes sense. A causality that makes sense for that matter. Reinterpreting events so as not to accept you have changed, so as to keep at peace with who you believe you are. It may take a while then to understand through the new lens of virtue that you have eventually accepted and owned, the impulse of previous events that were the very signs of this virtue you as of then had yet to conceive.
And don't underestimate the power of changing the mind to change the body. The power of changing beliefs to change thoughts to change actions to change being. Perhaps the body is the platform of expression of actions, the brain that of thoughts and the soul that of beliefs. Or consciousness the self that of inner beliefs, so as not to get too spiritual and exotic here.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Becoming or Someone Else's Being :Philosophy:
"I should only believe in a God who knew how to dance". The idea that one shouldn't worship one that isn't the like, one that didn't pass blood and meat. More relatable and admirer worthy is him who started so humble, him who had what you have and was able to overcome it, was turned into the Overhuman.
Zarathustra says that just like it is the wind which whirls the most the serene tree, it is invisible hands that bend us and torture us the most. For these invisible forces have no opposition, you are their subject and don't even know it. Now apart from the outside forces that control you without knowing, there is also the inside part. One more facet of gaining control. If all you have inside, all actions, all thoughts, all beliefs, aren't explicitly put on the ground, and for that it doesn't suffice just having them, but more so revisiting them and seeing them whole, rather you have to gain awareness of them, shining light even to the darkest corners, then there is room for the monster growing while you don't look at it out of fear, there is room for there to grow an unknown beast inside your house, one that affects your life. Shame on us to let us fall prey, to withdraw control to some subconscious tree whose roots we ignored. Whose roots we could've killed right away had we seen them, now intertwined with a greater part of our being, and so much harder to get off, for its traces are everywhere. That is the nature of entanglement, of the water that you drink there are parts of the body of Napoleon, if Napoleon is its constituents then you cannot remove him from this world, he is everywhere and nowhere, that is the highest entropy state, but Napoleon is harder to be removed not just because of its constituents, for those all man possess too, and much more than his "pegada" on this earth, cause he was in many places and knew many people, it's his information being in history, culture, people's minds, people's imaginations, that makes him immortal, even if we tried to forget it, we'd still get caught with the invisible hands of his influence that still mark what to day is, butterfly effect. So what you don't intentionally inspect, you let in permanently and with the risk of the invisible hands. Whether it be friends, experiences, thoughts.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Always Win: The Meta-Belief :Life:
For Negative thoughts this mechanism is well known, instead of shying away from them, for example Topuria says he confronts it, I say you confront it and kill it right away, substitute it for one that aligns with the vision of the self. Catch it right away and whole, or let it scatter and it overcome you. What you don't reject becomes in you the way it entered. The power of the mind is in much being able to change how things enter, what they are when they enter your mind. The power of the mind is that through will you can transcend whatever is around you. You have supremacy over the outside of your mind. But you can only control the outside of your mind if you already control your mind. We perhaps should play more at this level of what we allow in our minds, what thoughts and beliefs we allow to get in uninspected. And also while things enter you can know them, then if you let them mix with yourself you won't be able to trace them, and thus they are invisible hands. So to gain control every thought and every action and every belief must pass through the test of the image you have of yourself, this is the interaction that I had mentioned earlier without knowing what I know now. This makes the image of yourself more clear, less dubious, and more unique, and to that I now speak. When you let in new such thoughts, actions beliefs, your being mixes more, its average becomes more like the thing that just entered, whether it is good or bad, whether it aligned with your previous image or not. Eventually it gets so mixed that it gets like the others, just a black mix. But maybe unique is not quite the word, since if you incorporate more things to the self, the entropy increases, it is not unique in that there is many that are similar to it, however of course it is unique due to the principle of injectivity and in fact the more things it is composed out of the more uniqueness, the more information needed to specify. If it was a simple color, perfectly aligned, low entropy then of course it would be easily specifiable but still unique and far away from what the majority holds. In fact specifiable means that you can then know your self, or yourself, however you want, you can know each corner of it left and right. So, to not know oneself one first doesn't have a sense of self.

* Quotes and Questions: Information Quality and the Expert's Presence :Technology:
Now these things that you do or think, you don't even have to label them as they get in, just analyse them and see if they are alright or not, some lose their power of virtue and uniqueness if you let yourself lose and start labeling them. For the good and for the bad, things grow the more you don't interfere with them. If you resist labeling now, you can see what it becomes in the future, perhaps something you couldn't conceived back then. But of course if you had let the temptation of naming it win right away, you could only aspire to see it be that thing which you named, or at least its growth was hindered and limited by the act of naming. This is also how you expand horizons, by not jumping quickly into putting what you see into what you already have seen and known. You thus let open the possibility that it is something more. Manifestation really is a force of invisible hands that you however have control over. You decide what is that those invisible hands push towards, and so you have control.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Play and Internal Evolution :Technology:
Zarathustra says of this man he encounters that he isn't yet free, still seeking freedom, a prisoner still. Now before the prisoner escapes the prison he is in he must escape his prison. He must free himself before becoming free. But how could he be free if he still has in him those parts that rather be closed in prison, perhaps because there they are something? This is not a trivial thing at all. If you are in prison you have an identity, not one under your control but still a coherent one. You have a role you can follow, name, people. When you get out perhaps you find it all taken away in a split second. You find you will have to do something to be someone. You find all the meaning stripped away from you the second you put your foot off the prison. Believe it or not, if you have a whole life inside prison, it takes some fucking courage to go outside. Most that are there for a long time, if the doors were to open, I'm not sure they would run for freedom. The funny thing is when they entered they were sure they would get out at the first opportunity, but then as they started losing this belief they still hold their image of the belief up there, and so are sad because they couldn't maintain their belief, because they become pretenders, sad of themselves that they've become numb, desensitized, they are not mad on themselves because they are in prison, but rather because they don't see themselves anywhere else. Just like some are mad not only because their relatives died, but because they got over it so fast.

Confidence naturally divides itself into many facets and assets. There are levels of depth to confidence. There is the deep confidence of the winner, that assuredness that no matter what you are a winner, that whatever goes can't change the essence of what's yours. But this is no easy feat. You tend to tie yourself to what happens, you tend to thus dissolve all your might in them. It's little things sometimes that drain your soul. They may not break it, but make it lose its fullness, its vivancy. I notice this, thus I can prevent it, thus I can fight it. It is not one bit demeaning to be unfit for something others want you to fit. You know you could fit if you so wanted. This is the most all what deeper confidence entails. Knowing you can do anything. Absolutely anything. I know that if I wanted I could be better than anyone at anything they do. Multiple. There is cruelty to this however. You know you can have it all, and so you are burdened with choice. Power can make one anxious. Not everyone would want to live with power. * Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Fairness of the Lived Moment :Philosophy:
Perhaps that the abstraction of the state allows for further development of abstractions, more space for imagination and all of a sudden lesser real goals take place, all of a sudden power over the confused and spread state is what People drive for. That there is only mud on the throne and the throne sits on top of mud, Nietzsche says.
That in solitude and from the exterior of the state may the overhuman rise and take over the mere human.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom in Present-Moment Adaptability :Life:
The era of the highlight, that today things amount to nothing unless a show is made out of them, Nietzsche says. Image becomes an ever more important commodity, a necessary and sufficient medium of truth, of belief, of idea. Truly ever more things you did in your life are more irrelevant if they weren't filmed. And yet we that in this generation passed through both scenarios have still this privacy to us, that no one knows what we've been doing all our lives, it gives so much space to achieve so much without anyone knowing.
I think we shouldn't neglect the brain drain that happens right inside our homes, with little children future owners of mankind watching tv shows that at times bless nothing onto them. Even worse for old people, that are allowed watching nothing bunched shows that drain life away. They in reality show that one doesn't need meaning to live, as the discussion of Camus made me think. One can just pass along not bothering much. When death becomes widely recognized as more avoidable I wonder what will be done to these people. Or to these shows rather.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being Active with Your Investments of Time :Life:
Nietzsche says the way of the world is that it revolves around the creators of new values, but people revolve around the play actors and the show.
That which we recognize in someone else, we also inflame in that person.
There is a real sense in which the dilution of authority, responsibility, into the collective being, brings about a certain confusion as to the problems you face, and honorous and more generally value driven people would abstain from playing this game. They'd rather die in battle than live with the mess of modern problems of which they have no clarity. They don't want to live for free if that means giving away their individual values. Thus one sacrifices even his life, thus one seeks the clear rules of into the wild a la Kaczynski.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Things Move On Without You: Freedom :Life:
Being a natural, and following nature are worthy of admiration. The perfect innocence of senses, after they were deadon and taken into your control, and then by immense control did you give yourself to free them. After taming all conquers of yours, letting them flow their new yours given way.
This thing that adults and cooler teenagers who are living their lifes to the fullest get to stay up till late, doing cool people stuff, this wave is now through me, and I am reaching the end of the ride. I confessed not to become this, yet see my self become the thing I sought to destroy, behold, now I present to you the over human, the overme. Coming back to truths stronger.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All This World Is a Dispute of Trying :Philosophy:
For the solitary one person around is too many, Nietzsche says, that it prevents the two of the solitary, him and his mind, to take the moment into greater depths. Thus I say company is enemy of truth. If right now what you wish is truth, all it takes and it takes all, is you alone. To be able to journey within into depths you are afraid to show another. There is no single one that knows you beyond the depths you are willing to go if you are alone, or rather with anyone you are not willing to go beyond certain depths. Truly being alone, that means being with nobody, not talking to Nietzsche, or who you are talking to in some of your free time, not having the company of society's mass produced memes. Truly yourself. Here can you search the big questions, and can you be free to do so. How much can you do that you can't do while with others? How many conversations there are no ears for in this world except your mind. How many places can you go only you comprehend. In essence how many limits the world places on your mind, that is what can be overcome as you stand with yourself. How much can you discover beyond the usual field of play you've been bound to. What would you actually do were you handed solitude for some amount of time? Those are some interesting questions. What voices are being muted by each bounding factor. What thoughts are better vocalized and more eloquently expressed when you are by yourself? This one particularly hits home for me. I'm much better able to produce ideas by writing or thinking by myself than when asked from people with people.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power Is What Gives Graciousness Its Rarity :Philosophy:
At least be my enemy if you can't be my friend, says Nietzsche in somewhat these words. If no one is your enemy then you perhaps don't really matter, or don't do something big. Heard this too from Andrew Tate, and from polarizability in general, people want to see your action, whether they are friends and want you see you rise or enemies and want to see you crumble. There is of course bullshit and stupidity in Andrew Tate, but there is also things to be taken, value to be taken by going where others don't dare perhaps for the depreciation of those around them. Perhaps being dangerous and choosing to work as a team is a build of solid friendship. Making your friendship valuable through mutual knowing of each others capabilities to end it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Why People Find Beautiful Things Harmless :Philosophy:
Arrow and a yearning for the
Nietzsche here perhaps insinuating that in your friends you see you that must be overcome. Your friends show you how you must improve, for you see in them what you look like and what you have to improve.
Nietzsche seems to imply that everyone's good and evil tells us about their overcomings and their will to power.
Perhaps obvious but never by me said that this tablet of good and evil in every person which dress their will to power is the single most valuable thing in understanding someone and predict them.
* The Blind Watchmaker: Death as the Surrender to Entropy :Philosophy:
"Seja qual for a quantidade de formas de estar vivo que haja, é absolutamente certo que há uma quantidade amplamente maior de formas de estar morto, ou antes, não vivo."
"Se as coisas vivas não trabalharem ativamente, fundir-se-iam com a sua envolvente e deixariam de existir como seres autónomos." Ou seja, a morte é a dissolução, é a desistência de lutar contra o avanço da simetria e entropia. 
My quote: It's not that they break the laws of physics, is that they know how to use them.
* The Unabomber Manifesto: The Industrial System and Surrogate Activities :Philosophy:
Ted talks about the system, the industrial system (I'm reading this book in parallel to reading Simulacra and Simulation). Ted wants a revolution against tech and the economic basis, not a political affair. He thinks we do things for tech, maybe that's the being that is feeding off of us, like what I hypothesized in Simulacra and Simulation.
It seems that the way he is going with this is that life was meant to be life in a more primitive way, where what he calls the "power process" (having goals that require effort and achieving them) gives meaning to our lives, and was more present. Because when now you are part of a group so large that you contribute so little, it's not as fulfilling. So the collective advancement and collaboration among humans takes away from our fulfillment. I mean, it seems that he is saying that living primitively/biologically was better. And yeah, if some people want to engage in that, like restarting, it must be hella fun, almost like starting a new game with no resources and only themselves. Yeah that would be cool, but reductive of the potential of humans. On an individual basis one could say that is the way to go, but look at tech, it's amazing what we can do. Yea, we are not free, we are more contributing to the being that feeds off our growth than to ourselves, but it has been the decision of the majority of people up until now, whether they were aware or not. Where I'm failing to see is what the big problem is with this, why is it more than just a choice, why does Ted call for a revolution? Is mental health gonna crash at some point, are we gonna become ever more slaves?

* The Unabomber Manifesto: The Power Process and Artificial Goals :Philosophy:
He says that the natural power process has been disrupted in modern society, and explains that this society is so mentally down and depressed because whereas before it felt like you produced change, now it seems it is imposed upon us. I honestly think this is not my fucking problem, I don't understand that people have these states of mind, don't get why, yeah just not my problem. 
Says that the drives today of which victory is more tasty, those which take hard work, are artificial goals, "surrogate activities". Well yeah we evolved, we are not living according to our biology, yeah whatever let's escape free of our natural instinct. What I learned from this book till now is that actually maybe the cavemen were among the happiest, or let's say, the lives that you'd wanna live if you had to choose. Especially for old people in the mind: calm, same things over and over, simple, live/die, no attachment to dreams, no simulation, no hyperreality, just the food on the plate and raising the children. Maybe what it means is that whilst the victors get an increasingly better life, for the majority of people, the average people, the quality of life in this sense goes down. And yeah the artificialities of today are very real. Ted says that ads make us wanna buy things we don't like, and yeah today we don't do what we actually want, we enter into more and more nebulous and dubious and complex systems of values, we do things such that others like us... yeah eventually people realize that they were slaves to the system, and that what really mattered all along was family, for example. Fight Club is exactly this, making of a small group which engages in this primitive way of living and they seem to enjoy it, they are fulfilled.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: Modern Helplessness vs Universal Control :Philosophy:
Searched up on Google to see if there are some inspirations and a lot of people noticed this too. Says that today's man is much more helpless than the primitive. Hmm I'm not sure about that. Sure in the sense that we described before that is the case. But the path of today's man leads him to be the master of the world, so he controls everything. Primitive man has no idea how the sun goes up and down, no idea of weather, animal behavior, so in a sense modern man makes nature. Maybe there is an issue with this reasoning, maybe it's that the man representative of the collective gets more and more control, but each individual along the way to build this overman has less control.
Ted then errs on the side of psychological security, psychological health. Psychological well being is the best thing to have, instead of living 200 years healthy but living a natural (but worse in this aspect) life. It comes back to "ignorance is bliss". If cavemen were unaware of how insignificant they were in the Universe, thinking that their 10x10 mile square of land is the world, sure they would feel more like gods than us. I mean what would you rather live like?
The workaround that Ted does is "oh but man could accept the things that he couldn't control stoically", whereas today they are man-made.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: Science as a Surrogate Activity :Science:
Under this paradigm, says Ted, scientists are not there for curiosity, or to benefit humanity, it's their surrogate activity, it's the way they find of going through their power process. Yep that is a factor, but how do they choose to work on a specific thing? Following this reasoning it seems that what they develop in their head as the best thing is the one which gives them fulfillment, and so they go for it. Usually the one which is the best is the one that is scarce, and therefore, usually hard or inutile. So hard things give more fulfillment to scientists and others of course.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: The System as a Self-Preserving Entity :Philosophy:
So he goes on to make these principles for how history works, and how reforms won't be sufficient for the system to stop its momentum; it would take a Thanos-like courage to restart where it has gone wrong, and accept that there might be unpredictable radical consequences. He points out problems of the system, what I want to see is how he proposes to solve them with his revolution. He talks about the system as a being, as an entity that keeps itself alive for example with propaganda, interesting.
"If you think that big government interferes in your life too much, just wait till the government starts regulating the genetic constitution of your children." He seems to make some predictions which might very well turn out to be true; the question is whether that should be or shouldn't.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: Behavior Control and the Loss of Free Will :Technology:
Ted says that a revolution is easier than a reform. Even tho theoretically the argument that "oh but revolution gives purpose to people" works, I mean it's hard enough to convince a lot of people, let alone change the way they live their lives. If people have trouble reaching their goals, this goal is not going to be achieved at all easily. 
Then he talks about the system controlling behavior, and this part is haunting, that the system feeds antidepressants so essentially changing people to keep playing the game. Very Silo-like. A system which has no center, no dimension, no owner, created for and by humans, selfishly evolves on its own, and humans are too dumb to realize their creation and their destruction. Very haunting and escaping the matrix-like to think of things in these terms (surveillance, data control). Yeah you can go down that rabbit hole and become a conspiracy theorist against the system. The problem that Ted points out more or less could be damaging is if the system starts having holes, and then stopping one opens another, like in a water tank in movies. And the rich getting richer is another thing that tech foments. But this whole perspective on the events of the world is a dual of the one where you see yourself as the benefited by antidepressants for example. Interesting this duality.
And it's really a gamble whether biotech will be able to reach our thoughts. When our thoughts start being monitored, one can really think whether they have freedom or not. When or if a machine can predict our thoughts and actions then things will get hairy. If we don't have free will and it gets proven this way, or rather the hypothesis gets stronger, holy...
One thing this reasoning of Ted allows space for is to say that a meaningful way to live life, a great way, would be for me to now get dropped in a random country, start blank slate, and survive as my mission.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: The Thanos Genocide and Reversing Evolution :Technology:
Says that the logical conclusion of technology is complete control over earth including humans, which is questionable. I mean maybe it will allow machines to do the work and we can be free again as cavemen. But having complete control, actually, that is a possibility of a timeline usually explored in some games. From what I have seen, after you conquer everything, you have full power, you go back to the start, but with all that you learned along the way. Peace. "Nobody said it was easy, no one ever said it would be this hard, I'm going back to the start" - Coldplay, The Scientist.
Now human ambition is the barrier to this, in fact this whole thing Ted is saying relies on humans being our worst enemies of ourselves. If we cannot control tech evolution into behavior control and such, which Ted takes as an absolute given, then what he describes might happen. It's sort of a lack of confidence in humans that Ted has. I mean yeah we are pretty dumb in these aspects sometimes, and the way he wants is the easy way, the safest way; we are going down the hard path. Still would be hard to go his way, because what he then says is that factories should be shut down, technical books burned. I mean that's WILD. An essential Thanos genocide, this is very Thanos-like in fact: freedom/peace in the backyard, not many humans fighting for resources. This relation apparently no one in the internet has noticed.
Perhaps those that would rather have the primitive life instead of living longer could separate themselves from us, perhaps we should reserve some land for them. He outlined possible futures with artificial intelligence, but he seems to neglect here and before that humans will still have things to do. First he seems fixated on this planet, as if mankind would never expand to crazier stuff. And I mean surrogate activities will still exist. He says if people retain power over AI there will be a small elite governing the world essentially, and here we come to AI problems, but I mean there are other possibilities.

* The Unabomber Manifesto: The Cost of the Revolution :Philosophy:
Ted wants to catch the system in a crisis to make a revolution, the counter ideal to tech in Nature. Maybe a beautiful way for mankind to go: evolve, reach the peak (which we would have to agree on what it is), and then come back, reverse evolve, take care of the problems that we created along the way, showing gratefulness for each step of the journey, a sort of spiritual acceptance. A satisfaction of finally knowing what we are, and where we came from, perhaps the most fundamental questions to be answered for mankind. This means accepting our end, something that again is not easy, not easy to give up and be satisfied, but maybe would be smarter to go out on our terms, leave space for other species that may go the same way as us, letting the Universe rest, instead of insisting, fighting and going out fighting. Tough choice, don't even know what I would choose. It comes down to: are you here for a long time or for a great time? 
And the thing too is that I'm not sure if humans who abdicated tech, knowingly, could live with their own failure, to cite Thanos. "As long as there are those who remember what was, there will always be those who are unable to accept what can be" - Thanos.
Then comes the fundamentalist part, the revolutionaries would have to give their lives to the mission of destroying industrial technological society, they would reproduce abundantly so that more people thought against tech. I mean it just starts going crazy from now. Ted is suggesting without remorse or doubt that his ideas can turn out to be wrong, that it is in the mankind interest that a genocide occurs.
