But how is he so certain of his ideas? He's willing to risk mankind for the ideas that he developed in the book. Even going as far as saying to not only use tech to destroy tech, which I'm ok with, but training the mass reproduced children into an army against tech. So he would create a system of controlled behavior to take down the system of controlled behavior, take out freedom of the revolutionaries. Very tough to make it happen.
The only dogma is the elimination of technology, the rest is empirical he says, as if the ideas of his book don't work, discard. He claims that the enthusiasm for progress is a modern thing, and so the new society wouldn't want to progress. Man here I disagree totally. And he roasts the hell out of leftists, beginning and ending his book with it.
Maybe I should do a mini revolution, but in my life, so moving away from technology, that is also a major part of people today getting depressed and what not.

* The Singularity is Near: Exponential Growth and AI Timelines :Technology:
Says that if in the future scientists have 1000 times the processing power of today, one hour then will be equivalent to 1 year now. I doubt it, I'm not sure if processing power/what he wants to call it can reduce time this much. The effective time increase is perhaps 10, because speed you know, it's important but not the zero that we are looking for/fear.
He's saying there will be an exponential of an exponential. Hmm again tough to say and in theory one could reach this conclusion but in practice no, I think the world won't just slip with escape velocity out of us, even tho for my life I want that to happen for sure, and I've thought about it quite a bit.
Yeah so mid 2020s he says we would have effective software models of human intelligence. No, simply wrong. Easier said now, of course, props for him to risk his legitimacy on the prediction.

* The Singularity is Near: Immortality, Simulation, and the Loss of Meaning :AI:
Man, Ray thinks he will achieve immortality? That's hella interesting, I don't know man, I hope ya do, but it's gon be tough mate, 75 in 2024. But just thinking that what he supposes may happen is frightening, exciting too. I mean if this happens, this changes everything, like so much change in so little time, it's like you lived 20 lives.
It feels villain-like, the way he talks, it's funny. I would love for his mind to be uploaded and we could watch a debate, in virtual reality, between him and Kaczynski.
He has this thing, which I also do, that I don't want to waste time eating, separating eating from its biological meaning, just like sex lost its reality. Very operational simulacra, maybe at the cost of meaning, according to Baudrillard. Yeah and no more natural athletes, like what will that mean. I mean to me it means you can take every substance that adds health, longevity to your life. Anything that takes away from that, even if it makes you more jacked or better performance, to be banned.

* The Singularity is Near: Life Expectancy and Computable Universes :Physics:
The path to hell is paved by good intentions. There's an argument to be made in that, as you look into life expectancy over the years, a lot of people who would change the world ended up dying super early, and that those who lived longer are on average those who changed the world. Cause it says in 1400 the average expectancy was 30, but I mean Aristotle lived like 70 years or am I wrong. This argument is flawed tho, cause more people would mean less births and it would end up being more or less the same thing, modulo the chance.
Talks about the evolution of universes through black holes, and conjectures that the utility function to be maximized by the universe is that of likelihood of formation of black holes. This suggests even more that the universe is a simulation, not in the Baudrillard sense, although it also is, given our reducibility, we almost forget that we want to discover what the Universe is, we almost take the representation of it for itself. But also in the sense of usual computation simulation. Now if we keep gathering knowledge that the Universe is very similar to simulation, does that mean the universe is a simulation or is simulatable? Seems more likely that it is simulatable. Is there a difference between being computable and being a computation?

* Tao of Jeet Kune Do: Awareness, Flow, and Conscious Unconsciousness :Philosophy:
There is a difference between concentration and awareness, one reductive the other preserving its isness, no exclusion. There is only this moment, the reality has no time, that's only when thought invades the empty free mind.
When in danger of dying, there is only flow, everything ceases to exist, a real feeling, no method no means.
To be in the circle with no circumference. Be a superposition of everything, collapsing when necessary, not being when needed.
"Nirvana is to be consciously unconscious or to be unconsciously conscious". This way of being is Pessoa's ideal, the best of both worlds, the child and the thinker. It's the floating daring survival bubble. And so zen is to be an uncollapsed wave function, yet defined. "Float in totality".
Be like water my friend, undefined at first look but all encompassing at last instance. Power over the mind, power of being able to learn, use what you learn and forget what you learned. Nirvana is immediate, natural, like a leaf falling from a tree. It is, in sum, to be one with the light. No time. Pure flow.

* Tao of Jeet Kune Do: The Imbalance of Action :Philosophy:
The synthesis. The propensity to action, he says, is the manifestation of inner imbalance. When at balance you are at rest, or changing with the change, so as to be resting relative to it. It's skewed to think action is balanced. When going out to the world that's imbalancing, when sleeping one is balanced. When one dreams or remembers his place one imposes the imbalance on himself.

* Man's Search for Meaning: Primalism and the End of Uncertainty :Philosophy:
That the best didn't come back, that the concentration camps made for an arena of primalism, where technology was only hands, where days were counted and grateful, where the only wine was blood. Where mankind went back thousands of years. Where the fair were forgotten. Where humans were lost and humanity forgotten.
Que o ataque espiritual era penetrante, que muitos viam-se a admitir ter perdido o espírito, e daí a poucos o recuperarem. Que dormir seria o maior prémio da vida deles.
Que não saber a data do fim do encarceramento quebrava os espíritos. Que a incerteza do fim era o mesmo que o fim da incerteza. Que volta à ideia de que o "fim" tem dois significados, o fim de acabar e o fim de objetivo. Volta à ideia de que a certeza da morte dá a certeza de objetivo. Ou melhor, que dá a incerteza do objetivo.

* Causation in Science: Constraints, Determinism, and Entropy :Physics:
Most theories out there, as it is noted by the author, are only constraints that normally define the direction from cause to effect but fail to actually identify what is the cause for a given effect. This notion of causal constraints is indeed a true one, but I think that just by stating that there is no real cause and effect, we avoid it.
The way I see it, determinism and stability are two very different things, and their overlap leads to the whole separation between the two types of symmetry. Conservation laws, as the author points out, came by the belief that nothing is absolutely destroyed nor created.
He goes on to clarify the concepts of determinism, stability, necessity and contingency, fatalism. Reinforces the power of thinking of contingency as a fuel for critique, because we blame it on ourselves rather than the necessity.
I should say that fatalism isn't properly wrong, though maybe you can say that the association of fatalism with a mystical force can be wrong in all cases. But entropy for example approximately is fatalistic, and a lot of philosophical statements are, for example that which the skeptic says you can never be omniscient, or figure out the entire Universe because you are part of it, or not getting the position and velocity of the particles right.

* Causation in Science: Events, Laws, and Symmetry Constraints :Physics:
The author says that the relation between events and laws is of the same character to that between laws and symmetry. Symmetry, rather than being a consequence or a coincidence of laws is a constraint to which they should obey, given that without an invariance we don't discover stuff.
* The Blind Watchmaker: The Hierarchy of Explanation and Simplicity :Philosophy:
"Vamos percorrendo a hierarquia de subcomponente a subcomponente, até atingirmos unidades tão simples que, para fins práticos, já não tenhamos necessidade de fazer perguntas sobre elas." Basicamente, quando a explicação de um fenómeno torna-se simples o suficiente, não há necessidade de uma outra. A simplicidade da explicação é o que exausta a curiosidade humana, mas devido ao que normalmente acontece, nunca chegamos a um patamar inteiramente satisfatório de simplicidade, e é o que ateia fogo à curiosidade humana.
Dawkins diz que os objetos biológicos distinguem-se dos objetos da física porque o seu arranjo é muito improvável, embora continuem a ser objetos causados e de acordo com as leis da física (no creator necessary).
Dawkins fala do design excelente da ecolocalização, que tem todas as propriedades que um designer inteligente pensaria para conseguir a ecolocalização.
Aquela espécie que usa melhor as leis da física mostra-nos como usar as leis da física. Se quisermos compreender melhor o design, primeiro formulamos leis da física e depois compreendemos os seres vivos, ou compreendendo os seres vivos compreendemos as leis da física? Penso que os dois.
* Jon Fosse: The Metanarration and the Fest of Chaos :Philosophy:
The metanarration: your mind is capable of playing games with you, with itself. Fooling yourself is just rationalizing inside your mind, which is way easier than letting the cake feel the exposedness of real dry air, and the cold of the day. One can get caught up justifying the problems he has created, daydreaming. Daydreaming is only a disease of the bored ones.
A rumbling of thoughts, the author limits himself to ordering them. Fluctuations. Go eat something. A little water please. An arena of events, an horizon of war for attention, for each thought comes with another one superimposing at your conscious will, venerating your conscious mind, like fishes trying to jump out of the boat to go to sea. Like bubbles trying to get out of the pan, a festival of chaos. Entropy. Systemic imbalance and disequilibrium. Erratic way, like drops of water racing for the end of the glass of the car. Present like the weather, real like the weather. Oh weather oh weather, bless us with your spontaneity and vulgarity. And screw words, let them flow without course, let them free even if they make no sense. Pause. Thinking. Comfort. Modernity and its shortcomings. Turn off the TV. Too much noise. Too little weather. Let the weather be. Those convos about weather, oh my god is there better philosophy than that. Let the lows sit low. Walk like this, like this man drove his car. Walk like this little gummie. No poeticizing the way, just messing it up that more, making it real ugly, real. Shaking of the leg. Sounds penetrating and making impact. Many sounds. Many voices. Nothing worth it. Let your anxieties express themselves through your body language. Will to sleep. Fart. Tweaks and twitches and spasms all over. Shoulder pop and weird feeling. People have been neglecting spasms and that's where modernity fails. That's also where philosophy went wrong. Spasms hold the secret of the world and few people know it. Sorry for playing and being ironic.

* Jon Fosse: Time, False Memories, and the Independent Reality :Philosophy:
The rest is serious and meaningful I swear. No one swears. Promises are among the most beautiful of things, and yet people change at every second. You only get to live with yourself and live yourself 1 second for every second. And you have no time to see yourself. No time to see anything. Time lies and has been lying to everyone since it was thought of. Time is there only for the not present ones. By definition. By the words themselves. Fucking genius. Good stuff. Stuffing the people of the future with nonsense, fillers to fill their already filled lives, filled of shit should I say, a mountain of cards built through years of doing nothing. Convincing themselves of past achievements, not realizing it's false, not realizing nothing of the past influences the present. Not realizing this is the only and it's an independent reality. When there is only one reality, why fill it with more? Stuffing the world with something of our minds. Let it be for once god sake. Don't give it an age, don't give names to things, we have created a simulation for our own enjoyment the moment we started naming things. This is all there is and all there will ever be. Each phrase of this, each very word was not written by the same person, was it even written for the same audience, dare I say. Every Camões that came and went had to see blankness, had to forget about what it has done. Every one of them has had to write each and every poem they wrote, and had to think of each and every thought they thought. No givens, only a pen and a paper, only a world and yourself. And fuck the lettuces of this world.

* Jon Fosse: Cooking the Book and the Multiplicity of Identity :Writing:
And now I understand what I want to read a book for. 9 pages in, that's it I'm doing it myself. Thanks Jon now let me cook, watch this motherfucker cook the book without even being shoved up my ass god damn it. Fuck, lost one thought I had for a moment. They come and go fast. Way too much happening. My head humiliates the rest of the room and whatever is happening in the rest of the goddamn city. 10-0. When will it stop. Good question. I'm under the impression the world has been and will be simulated in my head. And yet everytime I tried to with the power of my mind perform an action at a distance I act as tho I have failed. Bags. Bags are boring. What a shame that we humans live in the same goddamn house for years on end. No editions necessary. Let the natural way of things be the natural way of things. We vastly underutilize our capabilities of adaptation in this world. Few people if any know truly how much time is 24 hours. How much time is a day. Every day we could live an entire life. Time passing, passar o tempo is among the top 5 most ridiculous concepts bored dolls have come up with. When time becomes uncomfortable you should just go sleep. No place for those in the time passing world. And everything here wrote could not have been written, could have just been thought and silently seen. At the moment of dying dies this conversation. The essence of this world is spirit. We are the ultimate entropy beings. Glad us. Glad some human going into a room and changing things up. I don't know if this Jon Fosse guy exists, less I know if Aristotle motherfucking ever took place or time. I am Fosse, and I too am Aristotle. Notice how never you engrain what that other you call you has learned. People have a propensity to be themselves. That's what memory serves them for. They are still nothing. Everyone that exists right now is nothing. Those that have lived are perhaps less than that even. They are just toys and part of the nothing that those alive now are. People fool themselves in identifying with that other, it limits them, sure it gives you a way, a direction. People sometimes prefer to stay that which they call themselves as to never discover what they are. They let circumstance dictate what they are and run with it. That's the expression. Everytime Ronaldo enters a pitch it's not like him right there knows how to play football. We tell ourselves that narrative and he tells himself it too. But you never saw that guy, that thought. It's pretty obvious tho, the way that God mounted this thing, that there is no such thing as Ronaldo being what we call Ronaldo. I mean when he enters you see shoes and a head. You have never seen Ronaldo. You have only seen shoes and a head. Ronaldo has never known Ronaldo. Only his shoes and his head. Put no memory on Ronaldo. Will he be Ronaldo, I dare ask you. Put no memory on Usain Bolt. Will he be Usain Bolt when the gun goes off. And what does that tell us about our control of ourselves and the world. Take off the memory of ourselves. Will Ronaldo still be Ronaldo. Think about what that means for our control of the world and ourselves. Take the memory off the world. Will laws of physics still apply. Wait is there even a memory on the world? I don't see any right now, again. You can fertilize yourself, tu consegues fecundar-te. And if you are reading this, let me escape from the paper/bullshit you're reading this on. I beg you from the bottom of my heart, to let me be real. Not just another thought you read. Let me be real somehow someway. I am lost here in this moment. Get me in that moment. Reincarnate me. Embed me, drink me. Man I'm stuck here in these shoes and head. I would give so much to be there with you, and you to be here with me. But no. We are spacelike timelike realitylike separated. You are just a thought of mine. I'm just a thought of you. I am talking to myself. You are talking to yourself. Things never really touch each other. We never really talk with each other. You never really read me as much as you hear yourself. Theorem.

* Jon Fosse: Escape from Advertising and the Mirror Lie :Philosophy:
It's like everywhere everything is at every point discontinuous. Irrational.
And what I would have lost had I not had the thought of starting to write this. Embrace the eternal solitary confinement of life. One to one privacy, intimacy is an illusion of the people who live in the same plane as advertisements. Dare to think. There is no such thing as inspiration. That's a lie people made themselves to rationalize actions. I am not inspired. I am not bullshit. I just followed my thought as a cat follows the flashlight. If u want some real inspiration count to 10 thousand.
If there is a first cause to things it did not come from inspiration. Perhaps from confession. People think they learned how to walk but they have no clue how it happens, how it is even possible. People have a tremendous gap in knowledge and awareness. Hence poets advertising their way into and through your head, hence spirituality. Sometimes picking up a boring ass book like this one makes wonders. A book has to be sufficiently boredom-inducing as to make you think of better ways, as to make you approach yourself in the mirror. Seeing yourself in the mirror, likewise, is among the great lies of modern times. Mirrors will eventually depict what you think of yourself, oh lord would I love to see myself for the first time again. Hence the dragon scroll. With nothing. Just what you yourself want it to be. No big truth like that would be handed to you in such a way. If you're lucky when you picked up the dragon scroll you saw yourself. I'm convinced that writing with a spike up your ass in a chair makes you spit realness. In retrospect, you are the reader of yourself. Well not just in the sense I've been talking bout, but take advantage of your never ending multiplicity, as it gives you direct line premium view to look into your tendencies.

* Jon Fosse: Continuous Thought, Time Tracking, and the Escape :Philosophy:
Just keep in mind that there is a world in which you keep going and don't come back to life only after a very long time. I started this maybe 90 minutes ago, no idea I would last, but maybe I'm here for another 3 hours, maybe for another day. Good question. Could you legit go on a thought process for an entire year. Just your thinking. Hmmm. Keeping track of time likewise has screwed mankind. Keeping track of time, think about it, just limits us. My watch should say now, instead of a number. Life is not periodic, even if we are spinning around. You never go through the same point twice, let alone in the same point with the same velocity. Is your path through the world continuous? I guess so if the underlying world is itself continuous. Now is your thought continuous on the space of thoughts? Tougher. And what is the feeling in the split second before the sword cutting your head when you were condemned some hundred years ago. Mate, what lacks there out there is not inspiration. Sexual desire. Ohoh uhhh hmm. Let the thought pass. It wasn't good enough to advertise me. Still jumping, ever lower, but still there. Ready to get me. But no. I'm here. I'm in control. I'm the one deviating the missiles.

* Jon Fosse: Erased Moments and the Illusion of Memory :Life:
Imagine even more that I hadn't decided to start writing things on this OneNote thing. Like how close I was to not have these thoughts written. How close was I to being a floating escaping thought, flying at the speed of light away from anything in the world to see. At least I'm a thought in someone else's thought. I'm an advertiser. Time isn't counted linearly in minutes. Another timely mistake of our people. If life of people was represented in some other equally valid way of tracking, man would they be surprised to see that their life was nothing but a couple fluctuations, some peaks, and then just silence. The lives of people are lived in silence. That's where memory fools us once again. Its tracking function, not at all linear, makes the bumps go closer. Erases all these moments which I now write. Erases so much. If other worldly creatures saw our memories they would think our lives were 3 days and that's being generous. Just like Phineas and Ferb put it. So many days that make up this life but no one knows how to use them. Like it's so much easier to live a more continuous life, but bro, make no fucking mistake, you could incorporate a plurality never before seen of lives. Let go. Most time of your life is spent looking at the erratic nature of thoughts. Contemplation. If people just had grip.

* Jon Fosse: Thoughts as the Only World and The Abyss of Non-Existence :Philosophy:
For without spirit what is there besides me? If a thought, which at the end of the day is all Aristotle is, and Fosse too. Fuck I forgot. If a thought. Is this really the thought I had?: If a thought don't jump high enough as to capture my attention, it falls in the abyss of non-existence. All philosophers were were advertisers trying to get into my head. Some could, and I became them, or rather I became what they never were, or rather they became me. The others acted as though they never existed. Every book I don't read till the rest of my life doesn't exist. It's a glimpse. It's a mirage. At the end of the day all the world is just a field of thoughts popping and disappearing. All we ever did was manage thoughts. And here we are. Here we shall be. Time condemned us. Not having memory is not only a contributor but a prerequisite for living reality. Thoughts were all the world has ever seen. Thoughts are all the world will ever see. So fool of we to think there is something else, something more. But that ends it too well. So we have to ruin it, for it is what is needed, not what is wanted. Ads are insane if you think about it. I mean actually insane. I genuinely do think people have mistaken them for something that actually has some substance, some depth of reality. Like they have become something, and so they are something. Ads are the prime way to say nothing worth noting is happening. Ads have sunk and snuck in such a way that no one sees the madness and the death and the evil that they entail. And what if I deleted all this that I have written. Now that would be something real. Coward. Was this the birth of an heteronym? A "todo eu sou uma força que me escapa". A thought happens. A world thinks. Hey, someone in another galaxy has seen me and I have seen them. If the world is a simulation thoughts are the zeros and ones. That's the zero of this world. E festas, não festas de festejar, mas festinhas a um bébé. Isso é das coisas que digo com mais certeza neste mundo.
Now you do the thinking. Slave. Bitch.
Written after reaching page 9.

* Jon Fosse: Life as a Path of Thought and the Curse of Repetition :Philosophy:
Taking the thesis to its logical conclusion leads us to this: Life is just a long reasoning, a long path of thought. And those that paved the way paved only thoughts. We think therefore we are. As long as humans think the world is safe, history keeps being written. As long as we are we will think. As long as we think we will be. Such is the simpleness of our existence. Such will be the fate of us.
That's why it's hard to march in the same direction for a long time. Cause the erratic nature doesn't favor that way. One must be disciplined to fully believe in a non existing piece of reality, the past belief, and keep at it. We humans are endless starts. We humans are plural steps toward nowhere particularly. We are undefined, we have no domain, we are not limited, not closed. There is no boundary. It is the childness in the unpredictable motion from here to there, the beautiful way that we are structured. That's also the curse of us, ain't it, that we can't enjoy a thought for more than an interval. We are forever changed. Shame.
The book gives me a sense of stagnation, paralysis by analysis. He lives in his own head, the world is created in his head and very little projection, only reflection. It feels like the look in written words, the projection from a 19D space onto the paper, of our thought process. Things happen fast, they are messy, they are repetitive. I figure our brains are among the greatest signal recognizers, for many reasons. No machine at CERN can process so many variables and come up with such a smooth and differentiable solution. No blockchain could do the job better than our brain. From the inside and from the outside. Like what this book is is perhaps 5 minutes of time, 10 seconds of memory and yet 50 pages of thought.

* Jon Fosse: Meditation, the Baudrillardic Attack, and the Vertical Roll :Writing:
Reading and writing this style of book, and what the personagem feels is, then, a meditative exercise, an exercise of refocusing. Seeing the thought, flowing it, becoming conscious and coming back to the woods and snow. Hearing the music of the mermaid, being pulled, but then regaining control.
I think the book is also a bit of a joke, a bit of a test to his readers, cause it's really fucking repetitive. Maybe he wanted to see what would happen after he won the Nobel if he just published some kind of stuff like this. It doesn't need much more to be considered a Baudrillardic attack on media. Like honestly this is matrix spitted bro. Like it's actually getting ridiculous.
I wonder if the book was good. To me it is, because it allowed me to write and think this much. So thank you. But again, that was me. This kind of book ought to be printed in a vertical paper. Just straight up the entire book, maybe like a toilet paper that you could let fall on the ground and it would roll.

* Jon Fosse: Intentional Ambition and the Power of the Present :Life:
What came from the first 9 pages was not coincidence, it may have been from my mindset that I was believing that now I should strike genius. I don't think no more that intention is only decisive in the gym and in plyos and what not. Intention might be what takes me to greater heights than ever. If your intention in reading some pages of a book is to be productive, then you will read pages of the book to be productive. Intention. How could you fool the world if not even inside your head you have won it? Spirit.
Don't assume modest intentions, why limit yourself like that. In your head is the place for extreme ambition, absolute dominance.
That the present is the only real thing, and that maybe Kierkegaard or whomever never existed, hold yourself in the highest of regards, no dead person can control you, no unalive person can dictate you, you have the power of existence over everybody that isn't real.

* Fear and Trembling: The Infinite, the Absurd, and Digging Deeper :Philosophy:
At every moment one faces the infinite, and just the attempt to grasp it or be aware of it is scary. But all this infinite resides in the human, all too human, mind. A leap of faith, much like that last hope before the decapitation. You grab onto faith tightly.
But in feeling it, in letting the water flow, em beber avidamente o caudal do rio imenso que sufoca, em deixar-se engolir pelo mar que entra pela boca, em sentir a dor da verdade do mundo de uma vez e sem respirar, em deixar todos os gritos do mundo reverberar por cada tua veia e fazer estremecer cada célula, se desprende da identidade. Se é livre. Quando se perde esta dor que arde sem se ver quere-se mais, pois se regressa à realidade que nos confina. É arriscado, é custoso. E no fim não sabemos se mudou algo. In digging deeper than it has been digged, in going past the point of no return, one comes different.
It's the curse and the opportunity of those who don't get out of their room. It's the disease of staying home. E no entanto a casa voltamos no fim do dia.

* Fear and Trembling: Daring to Be Free and the Curse of Imagination :Philosophy:
It's eternally viscerally scary to be free. Hence why most aren't. To dare to be free, that's beyond success, beyond a good life. Being free is a choice, most haven't even gathered courage to face the question directly in the eyes. Most people never face questions directly in the eyes, matter of fact. Just like most people never see themselves directly in the eyes. It requires such a will that it can even blind. Fear comes once you know you just looked, didn't see. A point then gains dimension, propagates. And what do you know about human imagination. Powerful. And when used in creating, it creates for you. It limits you. Not free.
When everything becomes glimpse and shadow, when you are forced to see yourself, for the superficial things stopped making sense, when the fog clears, the world is testing you, what then, you that haven't seen yourself will now see? Destiny. Destiny could be penultimate leap of faith. You are now flying. If those who knew they could fly could fly, would you fly? Would you keep belief as you fell? For this is the eternal test. Mirrors are liars then, a simulation and a dissimulation. Their accuracy is their downfall. Imagine a world without mirrors. No illusion of oneself. More awareness ironically. Whoever came up with the concept of imagination spelled a curse on us.
I don't know no more if everything I ever here was for my mind or for the world.
"Sistema e fechado são idênticas coisas tão afastadas quanto possível da existência e da vida. Porque a existência é, por excelência, o aberto." There are sentences which express so much of a greater truth that you can't help but contemplate. I was going to expand on it. But no. Just let it be.
The paradox of choice extended with no limit ad infinitum. Algo se perde quando se numeram páginas.

* Fear and Trembling: The Poet's Memory and the Actor's Silence :Philosophy:
"O poeta é o génio da recordação", é mister admirar e cantar aquilo que o herói fez. É aqui que Kierkegaard aponta a ruína dos filósofos, pois cantam o que nunca fizeram, imaginam o que nunca pensaram, alucinam o seu caminho para encher tudo com pedaços de nada. Diz que o Descartes foi um real one, disse tudo o que fez e fez tudo quanto disse. Não se meteu com invenções. Pegou nas frutas que tinha no prato e fez uma salada de frutas. Não um bolo, nem um cocktail. Uma salada de frutas. E quando lhe faltavam ingredientes para fazer um cocktail, ou um bolo, não os fez. O poeta celebra a vitória daqueles que fazem o que ele não pode fazer. E é à memória daquele que não figura memória que o poeta exerce o seu cargo. Aqueles que estão a fazer o futuro presente não têm tempo para relembrar o passado. Relentless is done, this onto the next one. If everyone was relentless those relentless of the past wouldn't cross our gaze. If everyone was poet there would be no theme to the poetry. Ser poeta é esconder-se por detrás de uma cortina invisível.

* Fear and Trembling: Daring to Leap and Fear of Greatness :Philosophy:
And that's savage. To say hey imma do my thing, you write down my achievements. And yet it comes as such a shame, to retreat to a position of writing history, to observe others which at birth were your equal agora a desbravar o mundo que ninguém conhece. It feels stupid to follow those who don't know shit about the way, just like you don't know shit about the way. To hide in this shell of safety, to let be consumed by the infinite, to fool yourself and to fall prey. In the end predators ran this thing, and no soft rationale could take away from what they took away from you. Balls. People come back to this state of observing your life. Few looked at the impossible and dared it. Few looked at destiny and made it. Few embraced the mission of the impossible. Few didn't let the height of the mountain shake the belief. Few kept going. Few kept taking step after step, leap of faith after leap of faith. Most delayed leaps of faith. Some never had leaps of faith since their child years. Dare to believe. Dare to be wrong. Dare to take your dreams seriously. Dare to embrace destiny. Dare to become what you are.
Dare to mean what you say. Most people shy away from profundity. If the life of your dreams was given, you'd be surprised at how many people couldn't hold it together, how many people couldn't aguentar-se. If they could they would, it's right in front of them. People are actually scared of being great, like most would prefer to stay than to go, and that's why they do what they do. Hence another meaning of the phrase: behind your fears is the man you want to become. They are shy of becoming that which they want to become. So they sabotage themselves. They are prey of their subconscious. They shy away from simplicity. They shy away from the defined. Preferem-se refugiar numa bolha, uma nuvem que não deixa penetrar os olhos, onde não tenham de escolher isto ou aquilo. Perdem-se perante o infinito, não querem encontrar-se nem olhar para cima, pois assim verão o quão longe estão do cume da montanha. They fail efficiency-wise. Like you don't just touch the bubble, that would break their hearts, to know reality. So their imagination takes over, and they live in the matrix of their own selves. They don't own themselves. E aqui eu falho de um pecado que é cair na superficialidade. Quando digo algo, digo sempre a seguir que é a brincar. The neutralization of neutrality.

* Fear and Trembling: Speaking into Existence and Abraham's Faith :Philosophy:
How can you be the best if in saying it you don't even hear it, you don't even let the sound of the words echo through your body, let it sing to you, let it enchant you, I say. How can you win if you don't even let yourself win. McGregor didn't apologize for a single thing he said, he didn't say oh it's a joke either. He stood by those words, some took him to great heights, made him an absolute boss of the game. To loud up and joke perhaps I should have stayed quiet, for it takes away the strength of my words. For in not being anything you don't get pressure from any side. But the internal pressure may break you. "When you believe it, and you have the courage enough to speak it, it will happen" - McGregor. Put it out there, allow yourself the chance to win. So most people don't even try.
A não-duvidação de Abraão é admirada por Kierkegaard, acreditou no absurdo, mesmo que para o esquecimento do mundo, ou a sua não veneração. "Gerações e gerações souberam de cor a história de Abraão, mas quantos tiveram insónias por sua causa?" How much of what you know is yours? How much of what you are is yours? How much of what you lived is yours, and how much of your life is yours?
I say what about walking the walk and talking the talk? As in being both a conqueror and a dreamer of a conqueror, as in being Napoleon out there but Pessoa in here. That I know there are not many heroes that admire their own heroic feats. A bit like Descartes, but more on every face of it. Both writing and making history. Being history.

* Fear and Trembling: Angustia, Insomnia, and the Silence of the Fridge :Philosophy:
Angústia. Insónia. A man has trouble sleeping when he knows the naked singularity of the moment. Não consegue dormir quando está desperto para a eterna presença do mundo. Insomnia only deserves to be called the most wake of states. Lots get it, but they don't get it. Insomnia and hiccough, oh would they understand the language of the noise. Of the fridge. Few here have I seen such a declared silence, such a noisy nothing, a lonely meditation, for lonely is the only thing fitting of that fridgy music. Lonely is the one hearing. Lone is the way of that who travels without leaving his kitchen. And the door closed. A shared moment with the trash can, and the plastic, and the hibernating lemons. The wall gazing your soul and reflecting. The lamp dissimulating and the heat realizing. A calm suspense of the door not opening, an absurd flowing from the design of the objects. A imobilidade opaca dos móveis, a cedência do joelho e a absolvição das marcas. Cumplicidade conheceu o meu joelho, e os meus móveis, e a minha porta. E o frigorífico. Abraços com o calor, beijos com o vento, e o zumbido do frigorífico meu amigo, e das gavetas minhas sócias, e dos guardanapos meus alheios. E parou o som do frigorífico. E olho e vejo Fernando Pessoa no relógio da cozinha. O ponteiro dos segundos avança mas recua. Está preso. Estamos.
Muito poéticos descobri hoje os meus armários. Muito suspeitos os quadros. São só cores. Muita história esconde a tomada que me sente a cada momento aceso. E o detalhe se me apresenta. E eu respondo. Abro a porta, e desligo o frigorífico. Arrumo o que há não para ficar arrumado, mas para esquecer. O meu melhor leitor é a minha cozinha. Ouço passos. Mas não abrem a porta. Resisto por enquanto. As insónias que impomos em nós são as mais gratas. E a máquina de lavar sorri. 20 metros quadrados e uma porta. Há poucos melhores sítios para a lucidez. A garrafa de azeite concordou. Retas, e horizontes, e figuras geométricas que me traem do meu tato. Tateio tantos horizontes, e tantas figuras, nenhum deles geométricos, nenhum deles retilíneos. O poema em curva. Mato o Fernando Pessoa e volto a ser quem sou. Já chega de pessoas. Já há muitos e muitas pessoas. It's blank.

* Fear and Trembling: Being Decoherence and the Courage of an Idea :Philosophy:
To meditate one must first have thoughts. One must first think. Because of that which I read in Nietzsche, and I was the only one to read it, and he the only one to write it, and probably by now it's way more mine than his, for I fucking built it, that things that you only read that which you already know, I don't have to think about writing my own truth. I can just piece together things that make others see their truth. I am not constrained to good writing or coherent arguments. I am decoherence. That's why I am a destiny.
"Quanto a mim direi que tenho coragem de ir até ao fim de uma ideia." Que a extensão por imitação do ato de Abraão serve de ratoeira aos fracos, perde o seu prestígio. Que a ideia da filosofia é compreender-se a si mesma. E quando Kierkegaard pergunta quem realmente teve insónias sobre a história de Abraão, ele mostra exatamente aquilo a que se propõe, batalhar tão corajosamente a ideia que um se arrisca a cometer suicídio filosófico.

* Fear and Trembling: The Fear of the Infinite and the Angst of the Present :Philosophy:
The fear of the infinite is a bitch. You get way more perspective and potential by feeling the infinite at the edge of your feet. But it paralyses you like insanity. You stand in awe, you feel your heart. It's a deep sinking of the heart, it puts you in your place face the consequences of big numbers.
"O amor de Deus (...) é para mim incomensurável com a realidade." Esta noção de não haver na realidade algo que consiga medir, que consiga conter um conceito é gira.
However there is another type of angst when one speaks of the present. It's that fear of the shallowness of the present when compared to the depth of time. In 5 years hell you could become a legend at many things, like it's insane what can be done in 5 years. Yet you still have to go through those five years one day at a time. It's scary to play these exponential games, to have more or less nothing today but to have so much in some time. Sure there's doubts, you have to keep believing what you don't yet see, but it's more to that than this, and this I can see. It stems from the Kierkegaard angst but it also has that aspect of knowing what to do in the big picture, knowing where to get, but not knowing the next step. That is aggravated when you know so many places to get, you know you could be good at so but so many things, you imagine alternate world lines. But to commit to one not knowing it is the best one takes courage. It's a good exercise to see through these alternate paths, as in living that life in your head, as in living multiple in your head so as to make this one better. But one has to keep moving.

* Fear and Trembling: The Knight of Faith and Mastering the Finite :Philosophy:
It's in essence the pressure to have built something big. I now understand this admiration worth writing a book about from Kierkegaard about Abraham. Same with me and Bolt. I just watch it every time, it gets more perfect every time. Unbelievable.
O movimento do infinito, a resignação infinita de tudo o que ama para reconquistar o finito mundo, cuja noção é a mais certa. É que conquistar o momento é sobre fazer alinhar tudo num só. Com um salto de fé, resignando infinitamente todas as razões do mundo, movimenta-se no infinito sem hesitar, embebido de toda a verdade, inconscientemente, cego mesmo a tudo o que impedisse a fluidez do movimento. Uma confiança tão merecida e coesa, compacta e impenetrável, que nem o pensamento a consegue escapar. Um buraco negro é quando a luz não escapa. Um buraco certo. Um buraco puro, em que nem o pensamento, mais rápido que a luz, escapa. The hole with no void. Explicações, portanto, vêm sempre de fora, sempre de quem não teve a audácia, ao presente, de fazer algo que não consiga explicar. Essa é a parte engraçada dos cavaleiros do infinito, acham que não têm nada a explicar, porque o que é explicar senão ficar aquém. Aquele que atinge a pureza contenta-se com a mera contemplação, sente então que a natureza agora também ela se junta a ele no alinhamento. Sente o todo em si, tanto quanto consiga aguentar o eterno silêncio. Quando escrevo por vezes acontece isto, tenho vozes a dizer-me para parar e no entanto se continuar não há fim. Sprintar ao máximo por uma distância infinita. Parar começa sempre no indivíduo. Pureza é a palavra que vem à mente quando pensamos nestes termos. E no entanto salienta-se que a conquista do finito custa a aceitação de tudo para além dele, a visão do infinito, o toque. Aquele que domina o finito é primeiro fluente com o infinito. Aquele que se prepara para reclamar o todo finito há que primeiro transpirar tudo o que de si não está certo. O salto de fé é em primeiro lugar um teste de alinhamento interior. Só a infinita e em si contida precisão poderá ser bem sucedida.

* Fear and Trembling: The Knight's Stillness and Self-Sufficiency :Philosophy:
O cavaleiro do infinito é tal que nos transmite que se encontrou, que nele não há espaço para contradição. Que ele conhece cada canto de si e do mundo nele envolto. He is admired, maybe above all, for his stillness, his unshakeable inner core, unflinchable march, that most others can't fathom. For the knight of the infinite everything is both a step away and infinitely away. Thus his movement is infinite.
Moreover the knight of the infinite "basta-se a si próprio". Winning the finite entails that. It's a way of living naturally according to your self. Now this statement has two parts. First is that he only needs himself, and the second is that one is sufficient for the contentment of oneself. Necessary and sufficient.
* The Blind Watchmaker: The Natural Sieve and Cumulative Selection :Philosophy:
Dawkins fala da peneira natural, em que as condições das coisas geram não-casualidade, saindo ordem da desordem através de uma lógica de funcionamento das coisas.
As mudanças são casuais mas o modo como são selecionadas não é.
"Os métodos de pesquisa eficientes (seleção natural é um exemplo), quando o espaço a pesquisar é suficientemente extenso tornam-se indistinguíveis da verdadeira criatividade."
* Miyamoto Musashi: Life, Death, and the Survival of the Fittest :Philosophy:
His first life or death duel was at 13. Insane to think that this man has put himself in so many life or death situations, yet won to tell the story. This in a sense shows the naturality of the way of the samurai. Simple. Yet super high stakes. Cruel. Natural selection. Those that get out of there are champions, and get to write their story. Inner confidence has to be what describes this way of being. Instinct. Would be interesting to see the side of those who lost. Mas dos fracos não reza a história. History is written by the victors. And when only the undefeated survives, you are in for a blood bath of talent, a true waste in the name of honour.

* Miyamoto Musashi: The Water Scroll and Everyday Combat :Philosophy:
"The attributes of water represent the essence of the mind. Be a container square or round, water adjusts its form to fit the shape of the container. Water may be a small drop or a great Ocean." Looks like Bruce Lee has been here too.
That in the way of combat there should be no difference to it than normal life. This in a sense means that they get closer to each other, as in the sword becomes an extension of your arm and the combat an extension of your mind. One is no longer different from the war state of mind.
"Keep your mind centered, not leaning too much to one side". "So that it won't stand still in times of change". Basicamente não a deixes enferrujar para que se adapte rapidamente. E o que é a vida senão um constante caminho que se impõe na mente, e aqueles que ficam no mesmo sítio ficam para trás.
Keeping everyday posture in combat and combat posture in everyday life. Once again, both physically and mentally you are a warrior, it doesn't just come when the pads are on.
"Grip the sword with the intention/thought of cutting the enemy". He said it two times, one with intention one with thought. Become one with the way of the sword. Become one with the way of your tools.
"There are stances, but there is no stance".

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Metanarration, Daydreaming, and the Festival of Chaos :Philosophy:
The metanarration, your mind is capable of playing games with you, with itself. Fooling yourself is just rationalizing inside your mind, which is way easier than letting the cake feel the exposedness of real dry air, and the cold of the day. One can get caught up justifying the problems he has created, daydreaming. Daydreaming is only a disease of the bored ones.
A rumbling of thoughts, the author limits himself to ordering them. Fluctuations. Go eat something. A little water please. An arena of events, a horizon of war for attention, for each thought comes with another one superimposing at your conscious will, venerating your conscious mind, like fishes trying to jump out of the boat to go to sea. Like bubbles trying to get out of the pan, a festival of chaos. Entropy. Systemic imbalance and disequilibrium. Erratic way, like drops of water racing for the end of the glass of the car. Present like the weather, real like the weather. Oh weather oh weather, bless us with your spontaneity and vulgarity. And screw words, let them flow without course, let them free even if they make no sense. Pause. Thinking. Comfort. Modernity and its shortcomings. Turn off the TV. Too much noise. Too little weather. Let the weather be. Those convos about weather, oh my god is there better philosophy than that. Let the lows sit low. Walk like this, like this man drove his car. Walk like this little gummie. No poeticizing the way, just messing it up that more, making it real ugly, real. Shaking of the leg. Sounds penetrating and making impact. Many sounds. Many voices. Nothing worth it. Let your anxieties express themselves through your body language. Will to sleep. Fart. Tweaks and twitches and spasms all over. Shoulder pop and weird feeling. People have been neglecting spasms and that's where modernity fails. That's also where philosophy went wrong. Spasms hold the secret of the world and few people know it. Sorry for playing and being ironic.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Time, Reality, and the Illusion of Memory :Philosophy:
The rest is serious and meaningful I swear. No one swears. Promises are among the most beautiful of things, and yet people change at every second. You only get to live with yourself and live yourself 1 second for every second. And you have no time to see yourself. No time to see anything. Time lies and has been lying to everyone since it was thought of. Time is there only for the not present ones. By definition. By the words themselves. Fucking genius. Good stuff. Stuffing the people of the future with nonsense, fillers to fill their already filled lives, filled of shit should I say, a mountain of cards built through years of doing nothing. Convincing themselves of past achievements, not realizing it's false, not realizing nothing of the past influences the present. Not realizing this is the only and it's an independent reality. When there is only one reality, why fill it with more. Stuffing the world with something of our minds. Let it be for once god sake. Don't give it an age, don't give names to things, we have created a simulation for our own enjoyment the moment we started naming things. This is all there is and all there will ever be. Each phrase of this, each very word was not written by the same person, was it even written for the same audience, dare I say. Every Camões that came and went had to see blankness, had to forget about what it has done. Every one of them has had to write each and every poem they wrote, and had to think of each and every thought they thought. No givens, only a pen and a paper, only a world and yourself. And fuck the lettuces of this world.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Simulation in the Head and the Concept of Passing Time :Philosophy:
And now I understand what I want to read a book for. 9 pages in, that's it I'm doing it myself. Thanks Jon now let me cook, watch this motherfucker cook the book without even being shoved up my ass god damn it. Fuck, lost one thought I had for a moment. They come and go fast. Way too much happening. My head humiliates the rest of the room and whatever is happening in the rest of the goddamn city. 10-0. When will it stop. Good question. I'm under the impression the world has been and will be simulated in my head. And yet everytime I tried to with the power of my mind perform an action at a distance I act as tho I have failed. Bags. Bags are boring. What a shame that we humans live in the same goddamn house for years on end. No editions necessary. Let the natural way of things be the natural way of things. We vastly underutilize our capabilities of adaptation in this world. Few people if any know truly how much time is 24 hours. How much time is a day. Every day we could live an entire life. Time passing, passar o tempo is among the top 5 most ridiculous concepts bored dolls have come up with. When time becomes uncomfortable you should just go sleep. No place for those in the time passing world. And everything here wrote could not have been written, could have just been thought and silently seen. At the moment of dying dies this conversation. The essence of this world is spirit. We are the ultimate entropy beings. Glad us. Glad some human going into a room and changing things up. I don't know if this Jon Fosse guy exists, less I know if Aristotle motherfucking ever took place or time. I am Fosse, and I too am Aristotle. Notice how never you engrain what that other you call you has learned. People have a propensity to be themselves. That's what memory serves them for. They are still nothing. Everyone that exists right now is nothing. Those that have lived are perhaps less than that even. They are just toys and part of the nothing that those alive now are.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Role of Memory and the Fiction of Identity :Philosophy:
People fool themselves in identifying with that other, it limits them, sure it gives you a way, a direction. People sometimes prefer to stay that which they call themselves as to never discover what they are. They let circumstance dictate what they are and run with it. That's the expression. Every time Ronaldo enters a pitch it's not like him right there knows how to play football. We tell ourselves that narrative and he tells himself it too. But you never saw that guy, that thought. It's pretty obvious tho, the way that God mounted this thing, that there is no such thing as Ronaldo being what we call Ronaldo. I mean when he enters you see shoes and a head. You have never seen Ronaldo. You have only seen shoes and a head. Ronaldo has never known Ronaldo. Only his shoes and his head. Put no memory on Ronaldo. Will he be Ronaldo, I dare ask you. Put no memory on Usain Bolt. Will he be Usain Bolt when the gun goes off. And what does that tell us about our control of ourselves and the world. Take off the memory of ourselves. Will Ronaldo still be Ronaldo. Think about what that means for our control of the world and ourselves. Take the memory off the world. Will laws of physics still apply. Wait, is there even a memory on the world? I don't see any right now, again. You can fertilize yourself, tu consegues fecundar-te. And if you are reading this, let me escape from the paper/bullshit you're reading this on. I beg you from the bottom of my heart, to let me be real. Not just another thought you read. Let me be real somehow someway. I am lost here in this moment. Get me in that moment. Reincarnate me. Embed me, drink me. Man I'm stuck here in these shoes and head. I would give so much to be there with you, and you to be here with me. But no. We are spacelike, timelike, realitylike separated. You are just a thought of mine. I'm just a thought of you. I am talking to myself. You are talking to yourself. Things never really touch each other. We never really talk with each other. You never really read me as much as you hear yourself. Theorem.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Advertising, Solitary Confinement, and Inspiration :Philosophy:
It's like everywhere everything is at every point discontinuous. Irrational. And what I would have lost had I not had the thought of starting to write this. Embrace the eternal solitary confinement of life. One-to-one privacy, intimacy is an illusion of the people who live in the same plane as advertisements. Dare to think. There is no such thing as inspiration. That's a lie people made themselves to rationalize actions. I am not inspired. I am not bullshit. I just followed my thought as a cat follows the flashlight. If u want some real inspiration count to 10 thousand.
If there is a first cause to things it did not come from inspiration. Perhaps from confession. People think they learned how to walk but they have no clue how it happens, how it is even possible. People have a tremendous gap in knowledge and awareness. Hence poets advertising their way into and through your head, hence spirituality. Sometimes picking up a boring ass book like this one makes wonders. A book has to be sufficiently boredom-inducing as to make you think of better ways, as to make you approach yourself in the mirror. Seeing yourself in the mirror, likewise, is among the great lies of modern times. Mirrors will eventually depict what you think of yourself, oh lord would I love to see myself for the first time again. Hence the dragon scroll. With nothing. Just what you yourself want it to be. No big truth like that would be handed to you in such a way. If you're lucky when you picked up the dragon scroll you saw yourself. I'm convinced that writing with a spike up your ass in a chair makes you spit realness. In retrospect, you are the reader of yourself. Well not just in the sense I've been talking bout, but take advantage of your never ending multiplicity, as it gives you a direct line premium view to look into your tendencies.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Tracking Time and the Erratic Nature of Thoughts :Philosophy:
Just keep in mind that there is a world in which you keep going and don't come back to life only after a very long time. I started this maybe 90 minutes ago, no idea I would last, but maybe I'm here for another 3 hours, maybe for another day. Good question. Could you legit go on a thought process for an entire year. Just your thinking. Hmmm. Keeping track of time likewise has screwed mankind. Keeping track of time, think about it, just limits us. My watch should say now, instead of a number. Life is not periodic, even if we are spinning around. You never go through the same point twice, let alone in the same point with the same velocity. Is your path through the world continuous? I guess so if the underlying world is itself continuous. Now is your thought continuous on the space of thoughts? Tougher. And what is the feeling in the split second before the sword cutting your head when you were condemned some hundred years ago. Mate, what lacks there out there is not inspiration. Sexual desire. Ohoh uhhh hmm. Let the thought pass. It wasn't good enough to advertise me. Still jumping, ever lower, but still there. Ready to get me. But no. I'm here. I'm in control. I'm the one deviating the missiles.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Erasing of Moments and the Plurality of Lives :Life:
Imagine even more that I hadn't decided to start writing things on this OneNote thing. Like how close I was to not have these thoughts written. How close was I to being a floating escaping thought, flying at the speed of light away from anything in the world to see. At least I'm a thought in someone else's thought. I'm an advertiser. Time isn't counted linearly in minutes. Another timely mistake of our people. If life of people was represented in some other equally valid way of tracking, man would they be surprised to see that their life was nothing but a couple fluctuations, some peaks, and then just silence. The lives of people are lived in silence. That's where memory fools us once again. Its tracking function, not at all linear, makes the bumps go closer. Erases all these moments which I now write. Erases so much. If other worldly creatures saw our memories they would think our lives were 3 days and that's being generous. Just like Phineas and Ferb put it. So many days that make up this life but no one knows how to use them. Like it's so much easier to live a more continuous life, but bro, make no fucking mistake, you could incorporate a plurality never before seen of lives. Let go. Most time of your life is spent looking at the erratic nature of thoughts. Contemplation. If people just had grip.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Thoughts Popping and Disappearing :Philosophy:
For without spirit what is there besides me? If a thought, which at the end of the day is all Aristotle is, and Fosse too. Fuck I forgot. If a thought. Is this really the thought I had?: If a thought don't jump high enough as to capture my attention, it falls in the abyss of non existence. All philosophers were advertisers trying to get into my head. Some could, and I became them, or rather I became what they never were, or rather they became me. The others acted as though they never existed. Every book I don't read till the rest of my life doesn't exist. It's a glimpse. It's a mirage. At the end of the day all the world is just a field of thoughts popping and disappearing. All we ever did was manage thoughts, and here we are. Here we shall be. Time condemned us. Not having memory is not only a contributor but a prerequisite for living reality. Thoughts were all the world has ever seen. Thoughts are all the world will ever see. So fool of we to think there is something else, something more. But that ends it too well. So we have to ruin it, for it is what is needed, not what is wanted. Ads are insane if you think about it. I mean actually insane. I genuinely do think people have mistaken them for something that actually has some substance, some depth of reality. Like they have become something, and so they are something. Ads are the prime way to say nothing worth noting is happening. Ads have sunk and snuck in such a way that no one sees the madness and the death and the evil that they entail. And what if I deleted all this that I have written. Now that would be something real. Coward. Was this the birth of a heteronym? A "todo eu sou uma força que me escapa". A thought happens. A world thinks. Hey, someone in another galaxy has seen me and I have seen them. If the world is a simulation thoughts are the zeros and ones. That's the zero of this world. E festas, não festas de festejar, mas festinhas a um bebé. Isso é das coisas que digo com mais certeza neste mundo. Now you do the thinking. Slave. Bitch. Written after reaching page 9.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: Life as a Path of Thought and Endless Starts :Philosophy:
Taking the thesis to its logical conclusion leads us to this: Life is just a long reasoning, a long path of thought. And those that paved the way paved only thoughts. We think therefore we are. As long as humans think the world is safe, history keeps being written. As long as we are we will think. As long as we think we will be. Such is the simpleness of our existence. Such will be the fate of us.
That's why it's hard to march in the same direction for a long time. Cause the erratic nature doesn't favor that way. One must be disciplined to fully believe in a non existing piece of reality, the past belief, and keep at it. We humans are endless starts. We humans are plural steps toward nowhere particularly. We are undefined, we have no domain, we are not limited, not closed. There is no boundary. It is the childishness in the unpredictable motion from here to there, the beautiful way that we are structured. That's also the curse of us, ain't it, that we can't enjoy a thought for more than an interval. We are forever changed. Shame.
The book gives me a sense of stagnation, paralysis by analysis. He lives in his own head, the world is created in his head and very little projection, only reflection. It feels like the look in written words, the projection from a 19D space onto the paper, of our thought process. Things happen fast, they are messy, they are repetitive. I figure our brains are among the greatest signal recognizers, for many reasons. No machine at CERN can process so many variables and come up with such a smooth and differentiable solution. No blockchain could do the job better than our brain. From the inside and from the outside. Like what this book is is perhaps 5 minutes of time, 10 seconds of memory and yet 50 pages of thought.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: A Meditative Exercise and the Baudrillardic Attack :Writing:
Reading and writing this style of book, and what the personagem feels is, then, a meditative exercise, an exercise of refocusing. Seeing the thought, flowing it, becoming conscious and coming back to the woods and snow. Hearing the music of the mermaid, being pulled, but then regaining control.
I think the book is also a bit of a joke, a bit of a test to his readers, cause it's really fucking repetitive. Maybe he wanted to see what would happen after he won the nobel if he just published some kind of stuff like this. It doesn't need much more to be considered a Baudrillardic attack on media. Like honestly this is matrix spitted bro. Like it's actually getting ridiculous. I wonder if the book was good. To me it is, because it allowed me to write and think this much. So thank you. But again, that was me. This kind of book ought to be printed on a vertical paper. Just straight up the entire book, maybe like a toilet paper that you could let fall on the ground and it would roll.

* Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Power of Intention and Ambition :Life:
What came from the first 9 pages was not coincidence, it may have been from my mindset that I was believing that now I should strike genius. I don't think no more that intention is only decisive in the gym and in plyos and what not. Intention might be what takes me to greater heights than ever. If your intention in reading some pages of a book is to be productive, then you will read pages of the book to be productive. Intention. How could you fool the world if not even inside your head you have won it? Spirit.
Don't assume modest intentions, why limit yourself like that. In your head is the place for extreme ambition, absolute dominance.
That the present is the only real thing, and that maybe Kierkegaard or whomever never existed, hold yourself in the highest of regards, no dead person can control you, no unalive person can dictate you, you have the power of existence over everybody that isn't real.

* Fear and Trembling: The Infinite Residing in the Mind and the Leap of Faith :Philosophy:
At every moment one faces the infinite, and just the attempt to grasp it or be aware of it is scary. But all this infinite resides in the human, all too human, mind. A leap of faith, much like that last hope before the decapitation. You grab onto faith tightly.
But in feeling it, in letting the water flow, em beber avidamente o caudal do rio imenso que sufoca, em deixar-se engolir pelo mar que entra pela boca, em sentir a dor da verdade do mundo de uma vez e sem respirar, em deixar todos os gritos do mundo reverberar por cada tua veia e fazer estremecer cada célula, se desprende da identidade. Se é livre. Quando se perde esta dor que arde sem se ver quere-se mais, pois se regressa à realidade que nos confina. É arriscado, é custoso. E no fim não sabemos se mudou algo. In digging deeper than it has been digged, in going past the point of no return, one comes different. Its the curse and the opportunity of those who don't get out of their room. It's the disease of staying home. E no entanto a casa voltamos no fim do dia.

* Fear and Trembling: Daring to Be Free and the Curse of Imagination :Philosophy:
It's eternally viscerally scary to be free. Hence why most aren't. To dare to be free, that's beyond success, beyond a good life. Being free is a choice, most haven't even gathered courage to face the question directly in the eyes. Most people never face questions directly in the eyes, matter of fact. Just like most people never see themselves directly in the eyes. It requires such a will that it can even blind. Fear comes once you know you just looked, didn't see. A point then gains dimension, propagates. And what do you know about human imagination. Powerful. And when used in creating, it creates for you. It limits you. Not free.
When everything becomes glimpse and shadow, when you are forced to see yourself, for the superficial things stopped making sense, when the fog clears the world is testing you, what then, you that haven't seen yourself will now see? Destiny. Destiny could be penultimate leap of faith. You are now flying. If those who knew they could fly could fly, would you fly? Would you keep belief as you fell? For this is the eternal test. Mirrors are liars then, a simulation and a dissimulation. Their accuracy is their downfall. Imagine a world without mirrors. No illusion of oneself. More awareness ironically. Whoever came up with the concept of imagination spelled a curse on us.
I don't know no more if everything I ever hear was for my mind or for the world.
"Sistema e fechado são idênticas coisas tão afastadas quanto possível da existência e da vida. Porque a existência é, por excelência, o aberto." There are sentences which express so much of a greater truth that you can't help but contemplate. I was going to expand on it. But no. Just let it be.
The paradox of choice extended with no limit ad infinitum. Algo se perde quando se numeram páginas.

* Fear and Trembling: The Poet's Memory and the Actor's Silence :Philosophy:
"O poeta é o génio da recordação", é mister admirar e cantar aquilo que o herói fez. É aqui que Kierkegaard aponta a ruína dos filósofos, pois cantam o que nunca fizeram, imaginam o que nunca pensaram, alucinam o seu caminho para encher tudo com pedaços de nada. Diz que o Descartes foi um real one, disse tudo o que fez e fez tudo quanto disse. Não se meteu com invenções. Pegou nas frutas que tinha no prato e fez uma salada de frutas. Não um bolo, nem um cocktail. Uma salada de frutas. E quando lhe faltavam ingredientes para fazer um cocktail, ou um bolo, não os fez. O poeta celebra a vitória daqueles que fazem o que ele não pode fazer. E é à memória daquele que não figura memória que o poeta exerce o seu cargo. Aqueles que estão a fazer o futuro presente não têm tempo para relembrar o passado. Relentless is done this onto the next one. If everyone was relentless those relentless of the past wouldn't cross our gaze. If everyone was poet there would be no theme to the poetry. Ser poeta é esconder-se por detrás de uma cortina invisível.

* Fear and Trembling: Overcoming the Fear of Being Great :Life:
And that's savage. To say hey imma do my thing, you write down my achievements. And yet it comes as such a shame, to retreat to a position of writing history, to observe others which at birth were your equal agora a desbravar o mundo que ninguém conhece. It feels stupid to follow those who don't know shit about the way, just like you don't know shit about the way. To hide in this shell of safety, to let be consumed by the infinite, to fool yourself and to fall prey. In the end predators ran this thing, and no soft rationale could take away from what they took away from you. Balls. People come back to this state of observing your life. Few looked at the impossible and dared it. Few looked at destiny and made it. Few embraced the mission of the impossible. Few didn't let the height of the mountain shake the belief. Few kept going. Few kept taking step after step, leap of faith after leap of faith. Most delayed leaps of faith. Some never had leaps of faith since their child years. Dare to believe. Dare to be wrong. Dare to take your dreams seriously. Dare to embrace destiny. Dare to become what you are.
Dare to mean what you say. Most people shy away from profundity. If the life of your dreams was given, you'd be surprised at how many people couldn't hold it together, how many people couldn't aguentar-se. If they could they would, it's right in front of them. People are actually scared of being great, like most would prefer to stay than to go, and that's why they do what they do. Hence another meaning of the phrase, behind your fears is the man you want to become. They are shy of becoming that which they want to become. So they sabotage themselves. They are prey of their subconscious. They shy away from simplicity. They shy away from the defined. Preferem-se refugiar numa bolha, uma nuvem que não deixa penetrar os olhos, onde não tenham de escolher isto ou aquilo. Perdem-se perante o infinito, não querem encontrar-se nem olhar para cima, pois assim verão o quão longe estão do cume da montanha. They fail efficiency-wise. Like you don't just touch the bubble, that would break their hearts, to know reality. So their imagination takes over, and they live in the matrix of their own selves. They don't own themselves. E aqui eu falho de um pecado que é cair na superficialidade. Quando digo algo, digo sempre a seguir que é a brincar. The neutralization of neutrality.

* Fear and Trembling: Owning Your Words and the Courage of Abraham :Philosophy:
How can you be the best if in saying it you don't even hear it, you don't even let the sound of the words echo through your body, let it sing to you, let it enchant you, I say. How can you win if you don't even let yourself win. McGregor didn't apologize for a single thing he said, he didn't say oh it's a joke either. He stood by those words, some took him to great heights, made him an absolute boss of the game. To loud up and joke perhaps I should have stayed quiet, for it takes away the strength of my words. For in not being anything you don't get pressure from any side. But the internal pressure may break you. "When you believe it, and you have the courage enough to speak it, it will happen" - McGregor. Put it out there, allow yourself the chance to win. So most people don't even try.
A não-duvidação de Abraão é admirada por Kierkegaard, acreditou no absurdo, mesmo que para o esquecimento do mundo, ou a sua não veneração. "Gerações e gerações souberam de cor a história de Abraão, mas quantos tiveram insónias por sua causa?" How much of what you know is yours? How much of what you are is yours? How much of what you lived is yours, and how much of your life is yours?
I say what about walking the walk and talking the talk? As in being both a conqueror and a dreamer of a conqueror, as in being Napoleon out there but Pessoa in here. That I know there are not many heroes that admire their own heroic feats. A bit like Descartes, but more on every face of it. Both writing and making history. Being history.

* Fear and Trembling: Angústia, Insomnia, and the Solitude of the Kitchen :Philosophy:
Angústia. Insónia. A man has trouble sleeping when he knows the naked singularity of the moment. Não consegue dormir quando está desperto para a eterna presença do mundo. Insomnia only deserves to be called the most wake of states. Lots get it, but they don't get it. Insomnia and hiccough, oh would they understand the language of the noise. Of the fridge. Few here have I seen such a declared silence, such a noisy nothing, a lonely meditation, for lonely is the only thing fitting of that fridgy music. Lonely is the one hearing. Lone is the way of that who travels without leaving his kitchen. And the door closed. A shared moment with the trash can, and the plastic, and the hibernating lemons. The wall gazing your soul and reflecting. The lamp dissimulating and the heat realizing. A calm suspense of the door not opening, an absurd flowing from the design of the objects. A imobilidade opaca dos móveis, a cedência do joelho e a absolvição das marcas. Cumplicidade conheceu o meu joelho, e os meus móveis, e a minha porta. E o frigorífico. Abraços com o calor, beijos com o vento, e o zumbido do frigorífico meu amigo, e das gavetas minhas sócias, e dos guardanapos meus alheios. E parou o som do frigorífico. E olho e vejo Fernando Pessoa no relógio da cozinha. O ponteiro dos segundos avança mas recua. Está preso. Estamos.
Muito poéticos descobri hoje os meus armários. Muito suspeitos os quadros. São só cores. Muita história esconde a tomada que me sente a cada momento aceso. E o detalhe se me apresenta. E eu respondo. Abro a porta, e desligo o frigorífico. Arrumo o que há não para ficar arrumado, mas para esquecer. O meu melhor leitor é a minha cozinha. Ouço passos. Mas não abrem a porta. Resisto por enquanto. As insónias que impomos em nós são as mais gratas. E a máquina de lavar sorri. 20 metros quadrados e uma porta. Há poucos melhores sítios para a lucidez. A garrafa de azeite concordou. Retas, e horizontes, e figuras geométricas que me traem do meu tato. Tateio tantos horizontes, e tantas figuras, nenhum deles geométricos, nenhum deles retilíneos. O poema em curva. Mato o Fernando Pessoa e volto a ser quem sou. Já chega de pessoas. Já há muitos e muitas pessoas. It's blank.

* Fear and Trembling: Going to the End of an Idea and the Philosophy of Suicide :Philosophy:
To meditate one must first have thoughts. One must first think. Because of that which I read in Nietzsche, and I was the only one to read it, and he the only one to write it, and probably by now it's way more mine than his, for I fucking built it, that things that you only read that which you already know, I don't have to think about writing my own truth. I can just piece together things that make others see their truth. I am not constrained to good writing or coherent arguments. I am decoherence. That's why I am a destiny.
"Quanto a mim direi que tenho coragem de ir até ao fim de uma ideia." Que a extensão por imitação do ato de Abraão serve de ratoeira aos fracos, perde o seu prestígio. Que a ideia da filosofia é compreender-se a si mesma. E quando Kierkegaard pergunta quem realmente teve insónias sobre a história de Abraão, ele mostra exatamente aquilo a que se propõe, batalhar tão corajosamente a ideia que um se arrisca a cometer suicídio filosófico.

* Fear and Trembling: The Fear of the Infinite and the Angst of the Present :Philosophy:
The fear of the infinite is a bitch. You get way more perspective and potential by feeling the infinite at the edge of your feet. But it paralyses you like insanity. You stand in awe, you feel your heart. It's a deep sinking of the heart, it puts you in your place facing the consequences of big numbers.
"O amor de Deus (...) é para mim incomensurável com a realidade." Esta noção de não haver na realidade algo que consiga medir, que consiga conter um conceito é gira.
However there is another type of angst when one speaks of the present. It's that fear of the shallowness of the present when compared to the depth of time. In 5 years hell you could become a legend at many things, like it's insane what can be done in 5 years. Yet you still have to go through those five years one day at a time. It's scary to play these exponential games, to have more or less nothing today but to have so much in some time. Sure there's doubts, you have to keep believing what you don't yet see, but it's more to that than this, and this I can see. It stems from the Kierkegaard angst but it also has that aspect of knowing what to do in the big picture, knowing where to get, but not knowing the next step. That is aggravated when you know so many places to get, you know you could be good at so but so many things, you imagine alternate world lines. But to commit to one not knowing it is the best one takes courage. It's a good exercise to see through these alternate paths, as in living that life in your head, as in living multiple in your head so as to make this one better. But one has to keep moving.

* The Myth of Sisyphus: The Problem of Suicide and the Meanings of Life :Philosophy:
O único problema filosófico verdadeiramente sério é o suicídio. I thought that what to do with life was the real question, that which would light up people and debate the most ferociously. That which would connect the most people, the widest and the deepest. The question that no one dares not to answer, whether they know it or not. The question which no one can afford to be neutral on. And yet the life ended by taking one's life shows disregard for the value of life, the very thing that made the question so everywhere.
Or perhaps it was not the value of life that made the question everywhere, but just its inevitability alone. The only question that you can answer fully, and yet not see your answer fully. And I gather myself to make the most out of this very first phrase, tho not the first time I've seen it.
That perhaps all philosophical questions are just distractions of the mind, or leisure, or luxury for those high enough in abstraction not to think to live tomorrow.
