Simplesmente fazem o que lhes é pedido. Como os neurónios são ingénuos quanto ao seu objetivo e são obrigados a não ceder às suas tentações próprias (não se reproduzem devido ao período G0). Ou seja, a evolução evitou a consciência dos neurónios para que trabalhassem juntos, em equipa e sem reclamar, para que dessem origem a um ser mais complexo, com consciência. A evolução, nas suas tentativas e erros, manteve o seguinte princípio: não deixes criar consciência na base, senão somos descobertos e o nosso plano é destruído "from the inside". E, no entanto, nas suas tentativas e erros não percebeu que evitando a consciência originou consciência macroscópica. E cá estamos nós a desvendar o plano maléfico da evolução.

* Dennett: Counterfactuals and the Leap to AI :AI:
A informação é informação, não é nem matéria nem energia.
Basicamente uma das coisas que faria com que fosse possível criar AI é que, se os homúnculos do cérebro puderem ser reduzidos tanto que chegue a um momento em que simplesmente têm de decidir "sim ou não", então, em princípio, poderiam ser substituídos por máquinas. Mas será que o caminho para o cérebro é contínuo ou discreto!? Se for contínuo, juntando os mini robots que fazem de homúnculos, teríamos AI. Mas há algum salto que os robots não possam dar e que os seres vivos inteligentes deem? 
As criaturas gregorianas, reparei eu, usam o raciocínio causal, incluindo contrafactualidade. A evolução progride numa hierarquização contínua de: associação, intervenção, contrafactualidade, como diz Judea Pearl. Judea Pearl sugere que foi a invenção dos contrafactuais que levou os homo sapiens a destruir os neanderthals e outras espécies. O poder de imaginar levou a que se pudessem fazer promessas, criando-se uma confiança e uma ingenuidade, em que se trabalha para algo que não existe. Os cérebros vivos são de certo modo autónomos. Os processadores de informação são livres e ao mesmo tempo dependentes de um sujeito inteligente.

* Dennett: Memetic Evolution and Lamarckian Words :Philosophy:
Estas criaturas, de uma maneira ou de outra, extraem a informação do ambiente, tornando-se mais eficazes "graças à informação que agora podem utilizar para fazer todo o tipo de coisas novas, incluindo o desenvolvimento de novas formas de se informarem ainda mais. Os ricos ficam mais ricos. (...) usando a sua informação para refinar a informação que obtêm através dos sistemas que concebem para melhorar a informação de que dispõem, quando se propõem a conceber algo."
"Os dólares (versão física do dinheiro) são como muletas ontológicas". 
Objeção aos memes: dizem que são "distintos" e fielmente transmitidos, mas as evoluções culturais não mostram sinais disto. Noto agora que quando se diz que uma música, por exemplo, é intemporal, isto significa que não é local no espaço-tempo. A passagem do testemunho entre humanos foi tão eficaz que a música se espalhou pelo bloco do espaço-tempo. Quanto à replicação memética, em níveis superiores disto é necessária compreensão. Para passar de uma língua a outra é preciso compreensão, mas, depois de passado esse obstáculo, a competência sem compreensão volta a dominar. Nestes obstáculos perde-se a nitidez, a fidelidade dos memes. Os NFT são o local perfeito para os engenheiros meméticos. 
"Pode-se muitas vezes distinguir os bons dos não tão bons sem saber exatamente porque é que os bons são bons." As palavras, num certo sentido, são lamarckianas, porque quanto mais usamos, mais descendentes têm e mais usamos.
Quanto à origem da língua, Dennett faz uma pergunta que nem ocorreria à representativa quantidade de pessoas: quem foram os maiores beneficiários: as palavras ou os falantes?
"Usa uma palavra 3 vezes e é tua". "As palavras, quando chegam a outro cérebro podem entrar por um ouvido e sair por outro, ou podem, ocasionalmente, criar raízes." "As palavras são moradoras preeminentes da nossa imagem manifesta."

* Dennett: Competence vs Performance and Cultural Obligations :Technology:
Dennett acha que há uma distinção muito importante e que tem de ser levada em conta para que a ciência melhore, a nossa compreensão melhore. Há modelos de competência e modelos de desempenho. O de competência dá as instruções, o de desempenho é o que faz com que se implementem. Muitas vezes os cientistas tentam melhorar os modelos de competência para nos tornar mais eficazes, mas esquecem-se que melhorar a compreensão acerca do próprio mecanismo que implementa o modelo de competência (na maioria dos casos, o cérebro) é a melhor via.
Talvez as mentes sejam redes bayesianas, atualizando e prevendo, sem compreensão da probabilidade condicional. Estes "antecipadores bayesianos agem por razões, mas não têm razões, ao contrário dos humanos". "As razões não são coisas nas suas ontologias".
"Um dos factos da vida, tanto genéticos como culturais, é que as opções se tornam obrigatórias". Ou seja, quando uma tática é particularmente boa passa de "uma excentricidade" para "uma necessidade da espécie, encarnada num instinto." "Não existe hoje nenhuma lei que obrigue as pessoas a ter um telemóvel (...) estamos tão dependentes deles que não precisa de haver uma lei que obrigue a isso."

* Dennett: Type vs Token and the Consciousness Spectrum :Philosophy:
As palavras não são tão específicas como se pode pensar. Podem ser escritas, ouvidas, pensadas no cérebro. E até pode dar-se o caso de termos ideias sem sequer ter noção das palavras que estamos a empregar. Por vezes as palavras deixam-nos pendurados, mas por vezes não necessitamos delas para pensar nas ideias.
Dennett fala da distinção entre tipo (type) e ocorrência (token). Embora o Word (programa de computadores) do meu computador seja uma ocorrência, quando eu falo do Word estou a falar do tipo. Todas as ocorrências, de uma forma ou de outra, num formato ou noutro, acrescenta, são réplicas. Isto é, na minha opinião, uma alusão ao mundo das ideias platónico.
Dennett acha que a consciência é um processo gradual, num espetro, e não uma coisa que divide o universo em dois. "Tal como o despertar de um sono profundo é muitas vezes realizado gradualmente".

* Dennett: Memes as Symbionts and the Evolution of Logic :Philosophy:
As palavras são domesticadas pelos bebés. Transformam o que encontram na natureza (ouvir os outros a falar) em algo que incluem na sua imagem manifesta, mesmo que ainda não o saibam.
"A compreensão e aprovação humana não é necessária nem suficiente para a fixação de um meme numa cultura." Os memes têm aptidão própria, e são coisas informativas. A teoria tradicional da cultura é de que se baseia em ideias e crenças. Mas nem sempre a cultura evolui conscientemente. Podemos adotar memes sem sequer nos apercebermos disso. A expressão "that's nasty" não foi propositada, aliás, nem faz muito sentido tendo em conta o seu significado, mas mesmo assim foi propagada.
"As palavras e memes que infectam os ancestrais são simbiontes valiosos cujo valor não dependia de serem reconhecidos ou mesmo apreciados pelos seus hospedeiros."
Alguns consideram os memes meros parasitas, não fornecendo contribuição para fenómenos culturais complexos e "permanentes" como a religião. "Somos a única espécie que conseguiu encontrar uma perspetiva que desloca a aptidão genética como o propósito mais elevado, o summum bonum da vida." "Somos a única espécie que descobriu outras coisas pelas quais vale a pena morrer: liberdade, VERDADE (...)"
Há quem proponha que a origem da lógica e do nosso res cogitans é no facto de ser melhor em termos evolucionários ganhar debates, mesmo que pelas razões erradas ou com razões erradas. "O nosso talento subjacente favorece decisões que são fáceis de justificar mas não necessariamente melhores."

* Dennett: Chomsky's Merge and the Evolution of Language :Philosophy:
"As palavras são os melhores memes, mas não os primeiros memes."
A língua é vista como uma forma de produtividade e utilidade para expressar significados, que permite a comunicação sobre tudo, com um número relativamente pequeno de palavras. A meu ver, em vez de atribuir um som a uma coisa, construímos essa coisa com base em palavras estruturais, que ligam as palavras entre si para construir o significado. Imagine-se a trabalheira que era dar um nome novo à manteiga de amendoim, ou manteiga de cacau, em vez de simplesmente dizer manteiga e depois adicionar o restritivo. O nosso sistema de linguagem evoluiu a partir da acumulação gradual de pressões imediatas. "Existe uma correlação estatística negativa entre a complexidade morfológica de uma língua e a quantidade de pessoas que a falam." - Hurford.
Chomsky, na sua revisão à sua teoria, pensa num operador, um merger, que tem recursividade, o que origina a língua. O merge une as partes da frase. É a operação lógica que nos separa dos outros animais. As línguas originaram por um processo de seleção natural, vindo da protolíngua, que por divergência evolutiva deu origem às outras. Esta divergência foi originada por exemplo por drift "genético", porque quando as populações se separaram e deixaram de comunicar umas com as outras, a sua cultura foi-se afastando cada vez mais, até que chegou a uma altura em que deixaram de se perceber uns aos outros. Tornaram-se espécies diferentes.
Uma teoria da origem da linguagem nos humanos diz que passou das mãos (gestos) para a cara e depois para a boca.
"That confident, I-dare-you-to-knock-this-chip-off-my-shoulder tone has never left Chomsky. He still sounds like Euclid justifying the axioms of plane geometry. Evidence, I don’t need no evidence for these assumptions, but you do if you want to challenge them."
Chomsky diz que a linguagem é, antes de uma forma de comunicação, uma ferramenta de pensamento, que permitiu aos indivíduos que a usaram uma vantagem. Chomsky basicamente não quer saber dos comportamentos, dos produtos, mas sim dos mecanismos. Quanto aos comportamentos, se forem úteis para explicar o mecanismo boa, senão caga. Segundo ele, e já vindo de Descartes, a linguagem é diferente dos outros processos da filosofia mecânica. A linguagem não tem limites e é indeterminada.

* Dennett: Gricean Communication and the User Illusion :Philosophy:
Segundo Grice, no ato de comunicação tem de haver intenção de informar outra pessoa, tem de se assumir a responsabilidade de ter a intenção (tem de deixar que a outra pessoa saiba que tem a intenção de a informar) e tem de ser o reconhecimento da pessoa que, pelo menos em parte, cause a sua resposta, crença. "S1 pretende que A(2) reconheça que S(3) pretende que A(4) acredite em P".
Dennett sugere que: know your memes, know the world in which you know you live. Dennett diz que a domesticação dos memes é passar da "seleção inconsciente para a seleção metódica", o que para mim é um passo mais próximo do livre arbítrio.
"Everything that you can do, I can do meta" - Doug Hofstadter. Esta frase mostra que a consciência permite usar palavras para falar sobre palavras, teorias que expliquem teorias...
"Há uma obrigação de termos razões que possam explicar um comportamento."
"O primeiro dever na vida é ser tão artificial quanto possível" - Oscar Wilde.
"A linguagem foi dada aos homens para que pudessem esconder os seus pensamentos" - Charles-Maurice de Talleyrand.
Dennett vai então dizer que a consciência é uma ilusão do utilizador, a ilusão do eu, feita pela comunicação, pelo facto de deitarmos cá para fora os nossos pensamentos. Diz que construímos uma narrativa à volta da comunicação, em que o "eu" é uma parte da narrativa. O cérebro edita a narrativa para ser simples e coerente, não nos mostrando a complexidade do processo. "A experiência consciente é a forma como as nossas mentes retiram das suas operações para nós, o seu funcionamento real." - Daniel Wegner.
Parece-me muito interessante este conceito da narrativa editada pelo nosso cérebro, porque mantém-nos distraídos da sua própria natureza. Em vez de andarmos todos confusos sobre como funcionamos, pegamos na narrativa e não questionamos. Claro que a ciência agora virou-se contra o próprio cérebro, porque está a construir a sua própria narrativa que faz parte por sua vez de uma narrativa do cérebro dos cientistas.

* Dennett: Speaking to Ourselves and the Privacy of the Mind :Life:
A comunicação, diz Dennett, tem as suas vantagens, mas há um problema: abre a porta de saída de informação, o que possibilita dar informação aos adversários. Para isto não acontecer, tem de haver um operador central que tenha noção das coisas.
"Você apercebe-se de como é porque foi capaz de nos dizer, ou abster-se de nos dizer, como é ser como você é."
"Falamos, não apenas para dizer aos outros o que pensamos, mas para dizer a nós próprios o que pensamos" - John Hughlings Jackson. Tal como neste momento eu estou a escrever para dizer a mim próprio o que penso.
Um exemplo pessoal de competência sem compreensão era quando brincava num trampolim e fazia o desafio de quem salta mais alto, e eu, a dada altura, descobri e fiquei mestre em utilizar o timing a meu favor. Eu caía um pouquinho depois do outro cair. E não percebia porque é que saltava tão alto e o outro tão baixo. Foi só anos depois que, ao ver um vídeo sobre ondas, entendi que estava a usar o tempo numa espécie de ressonância, em que quando ele aterrava eu aterrava um pouco depois, dando tempo ao trampolim de vir para cima até ao ponto de equilíbrio, em que tem velocidade máxima e me lançava para cima, e o outro levava com uma onda secundária que eu criei quando aterrei, e perdia o momento. Tocar guitarra sem saber o que significam harmónicos e teoria da música também é competência sem compreensão.
Dennett diz que aquela história da nossa mente ser privada é uma excelente ferramenta de design para sobrevivência.
A inversão de raciocínio de Hume tem a ver com a sua ideia de causalidade que é projetada da nossa mente para o mundo. Isto pode ser generalizado para a imagem manifesta, que, afinal, influencia o manifesto (mundo). O manifesto influencia-nos (e à imagem manifesta), que depois projeta as propriedades subjetivas.

* Dennett: Supernatural Beliefs and the Post-Design Era :AI:
"Como Nicholas Humphrey mostra, as pessoas tendem a encarar a crença no sobrenatural como não só desculpável, mas também moralmente louvável. A credulidade está próxima da piedade. Muitos pensam que a mente humana é o último bastião do que é sagrado neste mundo e explicá-la seria destruí-la, por isso, para estarmos seguros, é melhor declarar a consciência convenientemente fora dos limites da ciência." A meu ver, isto também se aplica ao próprio livre arbítrio, que Dennett diz persistir mesmo com o avanço da imagem científica. Derrubar a mente seria o próximo passo da revolução copernicana, porque mesmo aquilo que aparentemente nos torna especiais na verdade não é especial.
O problema do ovo e da galinha surge com a pergunta que Dennett faz: O que apareceu primeiro: a inteligência que permitiu a criação de novas ferramentas de pensamento ou as ferramentas de pensamento que geraram inteligência?
"Se o cérebro fosse tão simples que o pudéssemos compreender, nós seríamos tão simples que não o poderíamos compreender" - Emerson M. Pugh.
"O que eu não posso criar, não posso perceber" - Richard Feynman.
"A civilização avança alargando o número de operações relevantes que podemos realizar sem pensar nelas" - Alfred North Whitehead.
Na era do pós-design, assistimos à Black Box Science, em que embora façamos design, criamos mas deixamos de compreender. Por exemplo, as provas matemáticas executadas por supercomputadores. Ou seja, nós ultrapassamos as outras espécies por termos compreensão e depois competência, mas esta compreensão e depois competência permitiu criar coisas com maior competência que nós, embora ainda com menos compreensão. Ou seja, andamos aqui a baloiçar entre competência e compreensão. Um processo muito competente criou-nos a nós, e a nossa compreensão criou um processo muito competente... que criará compreensão (AGI)? Serão os computadores a criar AGI antes de nós, mesmo não sabendo que o fizeram? As coisas com compreensão percebem os processos, as coisas competentes são competentes a melhorar, mesmo sem compreender.

* Dennett: The Manifest Image, Scientific Image, and Patterns :Philosophy:
Dennett definiu as razões para que hajam propriedades do design que parecem ser inúteis como razões de livre flutuação.
Dennett concorda com outra pessoa em que a filosofia é o negócio entre a imagem manifesta e a imagem científica. Há aqueles que acham que a ciência é uma ficção útil, há os do cientismo...
Dennett diz que os padrões são informações que nos permitem generalizar, para que tenhamos uma hipótese de precisão maior do que se estivéssemos perante um sistema completamente aleatório.

* The Selfish Gene: The Memetic Pool and Comfortable Illusions :Philosophy:
No pool memético, na minha opinião, sobrevivem as ideias que nos fazem confortáveis. Se são as ideias de Deus, por exemplo, ficamos confortáveis em tapar os buracos da nossa existência com uma camada de vidro. O mesmo para as piadas e isso. O que nos incomoda normalmente são as verdades (algumas), pelo que perceber a verdade é ir contra a corrente. Às vezes, mesmo as verdades mais científicas só servem para ficarmos confortáveis em pensar que temos a verdade.

* The Selfish Gene: Tit for Tat and Rebelling Against Replicators :Philosophy:
Temos o poder de nos revoltar contra os nossos genes replicadores e os nossos memes egoístas. "Só nós, na Terra, temos o poder de nos revoltar contra a tirania dos replicadores egoístas."
Ainda que as melhores estratégias dependam das estratégias dos outros, a estratégia "olho por olho" parece ser bem sucedida. Esta diz para seres altruísta em primeira instância e depois copiar o movimento anterior do oponente. Se ele trair, trai; se ele cooperar, então coopera. Ou seja, se ele trair, ataca brutalmente, num golpe rápido e eficaz. Mas depois não te deixes ficar com rancor para sempre. Se o tiveres, é para ser usado para te motivar a ser melhor. E não tenhas arrependimentos.

* The Selfish Gene: Universal Application of Replicators :Philosophy:
A ideia de Dawkins de que o seu argumento filosófico (que qualquer vida no universo ocorre por agentes replicadores, segundo o próprio) aplica-se ao universo inteiro, enquanto que as experiências de laboratório só nos ensinam sobre a vida como ela é aqui. Ou seja, a filosofia, e por exemplo a matemática, poderá aplicar-se a tudo, e daí poder não aplicar-se a nada. Ou seja, como não tem uma aplicação definida, é completamente maleável ao ponto de se aplicar em diversas áreas.

* The Selfish Gene: Blind Faith and Genes as Living Structures :Philosophy:
"A fé cega pode justificar tudo." Acho que todos somos míopes, e se descermos a um nível de análise suficientemente profundo percebemos que aí somos cegos.
"Os genes deveriam ser encarados como estruturas vivas, não só metaforicamente, mas tecnicamente."
Quando é para atacar, é para atacar.

* The Selfish Gene: Memes, Dunning-Kruger, and the Illusion of Knowledge :Life:
Em relação aos memes e em conjunção com o Dunning-Kruger effect, parece que quando ouvimos a primeira opinião sobre um assunto, como sabemos que não sabemos, ouvimo-la bem, interiorizamo-la e depois pensamos que sabemos alguma coisa sobre o assunto. Quando estamos a falar depois com alguém, damos por nós algumas vezes a literalmente repetir o que nos foi dito. E o engraçado é que a mudança é gradual. Primeiro dizemos "disseram-me que..." (sem referir quem porque ou isso tiraria autoridade ou a pessoa com quem estamos agora a falar não conhece aquele que nos disse, o que tiraria autoridade) porque temos sempre a mania de que aquilo que conhecemos é melhor que aquilo que os outros conhecem, sem sequer perceber que os outros estão na mesma situação que nós. Vivemos todos algo contentes por termos melhores experiências que os outros, e no entanto é difícil perceber que estamos todos nesta dança de ilusões sem notar nisso. Mas acho que nem valia a pena notar nisso porque sabemos que é completamente subjetivo e só daria confusão.
Entra muito nesta parte da rivalidade a game theory, e o Nash equilibrium.
* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Time and the Child's Honesty :Philosophy:
"falta uma hora, 60 minutos, é tanto, é tanto" here in the train. Wow how I miss that, how I have become distant to that. Always asking how much time left, indifferent to my opinions, we babies know what we want, or rather what we don't. And we are sure to try to get one over the other, even if it means getting mad and feeling bad about the moment, we don't pretend we have what we want. Real

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War of Ideas :Philosophy:
War of ideas. That the other lover of ideas love us from the ground up, yet are also our own worst enemy. That in the name of keeping this war alive you should love from the ground up your position, yet seek your worst enemy. Learn the most. Make it a mess of a thing of a ramble of ideas competing for your attention, not uniform. Warrior no soldier. A whole army moving freely you are my friend.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War as the Way of the Warrior :Life:
In order to seek new wars of ideas, new wars of concepts and ways of being I add ; one loves times of peace, but the short lived, not the long. Seek greater enemies and diversify. You will increase your level. You are the warrior the more wars you put yourself, the more you fight through. Nietzsche says it is war that makes causes and not causes that make wars. Every hope to become rigorous belief must be battle tested, must be forged in the midst of wars with everything it opposes, and everything you already know and are, so that its pure form may be after compression, at last distilled into you, however little of substance it had in the first place. You must be battle tested to be immovable out there. You must have crossed the 7 layers of hell and come back to be still in soul, unbreakable in spirit. Let victory, not rest be your peace. Let not peace wander around too long. In fact victory lasts not very long at all. War is the way the lifestyle of the warrior. Embracing silent war, silent wins. That perhaps it takes everything out of you to keep the war, to get the wins, and even then not moving one bit to the idea of going again. Needing it. Thriving off it. Even if you are the only one who can hear its sounds, even if you're the only one who loves, who knows, who understands, who cares, who sees. Be him who seizes.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War Over Peace :Life:
"war and courage have accomplished more great things than love of one's neighbor" there is no reason to shy away from this. War has accomplished more than peace. War has brought about the best of you, of the warrior. It was never the peaceful times where you got the most. No matter how peaceful this or that is, you know I will find a way to my place to my war. I will find a way to fight, michael jordan style. I will find a way to stop the cuteness and put the heart down.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Enemies Within, Enemies That Break You Anew :Life:
That yet your enemies are within you, part of you, symbiotic. Thus they break you into something new. That you should in a sense enjoy the ride as you have it, ride on what's going for you, for war and enemies will eventually bring about another. And so obedience to your command your twinning thought, applying and raising its hand, touring it around your life. Not here for a long time, here for a great time.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Human to Be Overcome :Philosophy:
"The human is something that is to be overcome" the whole message of the book. It seems hard to overcome the human as a human, but it is in me to get it done. I as of now gaze at the fire, was it invented or discovered? My wise bit, my uncle, said it was captured, seen in nature and tamed. This form of life made totally functional, constricted. Only allowed to burn a certain much, that is the most annoying. Having to work but not being able to give it your best. No warrior wants to live long or be spared. One wants a great war, may the best thought come out, may one change for the better, and may peace be short lived. But what is it else that hasn't been captured by human, tamed. Now the overhuman shall tame the human, tame all that's human, all too human, this is a way I see to unlock the next chapter in our experience. Destroy it all the alike and rise the new.
so that the overhuman isn't contaminated by human foundations, tho it builds on 'em.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Was Math Captured or Invented :Math:
Could we say math was similarly captured? If we took that humans invented one thing, what would it be? What is the thing that we most made for ourselves, independent of what we found out there.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Slow Suicide Called Life :Life:
A slow suicide they call life, Nietzsche says, yap there is a lot of losing, but you can only say that do you do what you know you have to do to be the winner you know you are. On a whole other level.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The State That Substitutes the People :Philosophy:
Nietzsche reckons the state substitutes the people. That it pretends to be the people, but no longer is there people and followers, there is an abstraction that engulfs the two, perhaps so by virtue of losing reality, we'll see where he goes with this.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The State Against Natural Order :Philosophy:
there is perhaps also an argument against the state as going against the natural order of people and herds, taking power away from the people.
perhaps there is an inacceptance via the perceived unfairness of the mental nature of the natural social dynamics of humans. Just maybe the state hides that from our sight. Nowadays people refuse human the animal, human with its natural hierarchies. Perhaps better named a shying away from the irrational roots that brought you here.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Death Preachers and the State's Entropy :Philosophy:
Perhaps the preachers of death finding refuge in the superior way of the state, yet only making confuse good and evil, whereas before it was bound and tamed by a people, now it is spread everywhere, as most things tend to be through entropy. One can no longer point blame or bear responsibility, one can no longer own achievement, a giant cloud of mess submerged into the supremacy of the state, stealer of all that was something. Nietzsche even goes further in suggesting the going back to the old ways when one could place all power and good and evil in one person. Thus there are too many he says.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Mud on the Throne :Philosophy:
Perhaps that the abstraction of the state allows for further development of abstractions, more space for imagination and all of a sudden lesser real goals take place, all of a sudden power over the confused and spread state is what People drive for. That there is only mud on the throne and the throne sits on top of mud, Nietzsche says

* On Irreplicable Essence :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions:
"They can get the recipe. But they can't make the sauce." this idea that it is very clear to see what makes things work, yet being unable to get the ingredients. Thing that can't be bought. The fiber that only you have, can't be taken from you (it's yours) and can't be taken away (it's only yours). Stuff that is truly valuable, beyond any clothing and surface one puts over it.
there is this great aspect of seeing someone you know do something great you didn't know they could do. Like they not telling you and then you discovering independently they did something. That is essence.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude, the Highlight Era, and the Overhuman :Philosophy:
That in solitude and from the exterior of the state may the overhuman rise and take over the mere human.
the era of the highlight, that today things amount to nothing unless a show is made out of them, Nietzsche says. Image becomes an ever more important commodity, a necessary and sufficient medium of truth, of belief, of idea. Truly ever more things you did in your life are more irrelevant if they weren't filmed. And yet we that in this generation passed through both scenarios have still this privacy to us, that no one knows what we've been doing all our lives, it gives so much space to achieve so much without anyone knowing.
I think we shouldn't neglect the brain drain that happens right inside our homes, with little children future owners of mankind watching tv shows that at times bless nothing onto them. Even worse for old people, that are allowed watching nothing binge-watched shows that drain life away. They in reality show that one doesn't need meaning to live, as the discussion of Camus made me think. One can just pass along not bothering much. When death becomes widely recognized as more avoidable I wonder what will be done to these people. Or to these shows rather.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Creators of Values vs Actors of the Show :Philosophy:
Nietzsche says the way of the world is that it revolves around the creators of new values, but people revolve around the play actors and the show.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On the Dilution of Authority and Values :Philosophy:
There is a real sense in which the dilution of authority, responsibility, into the collective being, brings about a certain confusion as to the problems you face, and honorable and more generally value driven people would abstain from playing this game. They'd rather die in battle than live with the mess of modern problems of which they have no clarity. They don't want to live for free if that means giving away their individual values. Thus one sacrifices even his life, thus one seeks the clear rules of into the wild a la Kaczynski.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming What You Sought to Destroy :Life:
This thing that adults and cooler teenagers who are living their lives to the fullest get to stay up till late, doing cool people stuff, this wave is now through me, and I am reaching the end of the ride. I confessed not to become this, yet see myself become the thing I sought to destroy, behold, now I present to you the overhuman, the overme. Coming back to truths stronger.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Natural, After Taming :Life:
Being a natural, and following nature are worthy of admiration. The perfect innocence of senses, after they were deadened and taken into your control, and then by immense control did you give yourself to free them. After taming all conquers of yours, letting them flow their new yours given way.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom from Desire and the Champion's Ease :Life:
Becoming free from them gives you this chill, not replicable by those who visually are holding tension, after battling out with their will, whether about eating that cheesecake, whether to get on studying, see they have pressure on themselves to work, to do what they are supposed to do. You? You just know wholeheartedly you will. You have gone through all those battles, you are now freed from desire. It's refreshing too for people to see champions that seem to take stuff so lightly and value them so much.

* On Solitude and the Inner Dialogue :Philosophy:
For the solitary one person around is too many, Nietzsche says, that it prevents the two of the solitary, him and his mind, to take the moment into greater depths. Thus I say company is enemy of truth. If right now what you wish is truth, all it takes and it takes all, is you alone. To be able to journey within into depths you are afraid to show another. There is no single one that knows you beyond the depths you are willing to go if you are alone, or rather with anyone you are not willing to go beyond certain depths. Truly being alone, that means being with nobody, not talking to Nietzsche, or who you are talking to in some of your free time, not having the company of society's mass produced memes. Truly yourself. Here can you search the big questions, and can you be free to do so. How much can you do that you can't do while with others? How many conversations there are no ears for in this world except your mind. How many places can you go only you comprehend. In essence how many limits the world places on your mind, that is what can be overcome as you stand with yourself. How much can you discover beyond the usual field of play you've been bound to. What would you actually do were you handed solitude for some amount of time? Those are some interesting questions. What voices are being muted by each bounding factor. What thoughts are better vocalized and more eloquently expressed when you are by yourself? This one particularly hits home for me. I'm much better able to produce ideas by writing or thinking by myself than when asked from people with people

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- At Least Be My Enemy :Philosophy:
At least be my enemy if you can't be my friend, says Nietzsche in somewhat these words. If no one is your enemy then you perhaps don't really matter, or don't do something big. Heard this too from Andrew Tate, and from polarizability in general, people want to see your action, whether they are friends and want to see you rise or enemies and want to see you crumble. There is of course bullshit and stupidity in Andrew Tate, but there is also things to be taken, value to be taken by going where others don't dare perhaps for the deprecation of those around them. Perhaps being dangerous and choosing to work as a team is a build of solid friendship. Making your friendship valuable through mutual knowing of each others capabilities to end it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Friends as Mirrors of What to Overcome :Philosophy:
Nietzsche here perhaps insinuating that in your friends you see you that must be overcome. Your friend show you how you must improve, for you see in them what you look like and what you have to improve.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Good and Evil as Signs of Will to Power :Philosophy:
Nietzsche seems to imply that everyone's good and evil tells us about their overcomings and their will to power.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Tablet of Good and Evil as Prediction Tool :Philosophy:
Perhaps obvious but never by me said that this tablet of good and evil in every person which dress their will to power is the single most valuable thing in understanding someone and predict them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude as All You Need :Philosophy:
Perhaps the straddled principle across these lately ideas, that solitude is all you should ever need. You can live with you and your cause. More than living humans, loving causes he preaches. You love in the other what you envy he says. Be yourself a cause and thus have the highest love for yourself. I am the cause. I am the revolution. If you can't endure being with yourself, you are doomed to keep searching and lurking and looting and looking. What you look to find you should bring about from yourself. You should create that person you love talking to, and the one who does things the way you love. Your perception is your perception not others'

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Knowing Your Power :Life:
That talking contrary to what you don't know is a greater lie than contrary to what you know. This Nietzsche says.
for in the first one you fool yourself, in the latter you betray yourself. You don't yet see what you are, what you will be, the extent of your empire without limits, so talking about it as if you know it is failing oneself. Tens de conhecer o teu poder. What a shame if you get out of this place without knowing your true power, perhaps because you were afraid to unleash it, perhaps you let it slip. But not coming to see your final form, whether physical, which Socrates pointed out, whether spiritually mentally lifely, is failing yourself. Perhaps one of the big questions is what can you be.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Individuality Before the Group :Philosophy:
One should then realize this before handing oneself to the neighbor. Not letting your individuality get faded to the group, that you be letting go of control, but rather augmenting it through it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Loving Yourself: Polishing the Vision :Philosophy:
Loving yourself becomes much about polishing and cleaning the dust off your vision, always having it fresh, embracing the moment in the light of the overhuman presence you seek to realize.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being the Strength, Not Just Having It :Life:
Not just having a strength, not just having a right, not just having a cause and having a why, but being the strength and the right, and the cause and the why.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Climb Past, Then Fly :Life:
Being able to climb past, but then fly.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building the Impregnable Identity :Life:
Again the process of rebuilding oneself. To build the most impregnable identity, unshakable force of nature within you, you can't wait for it to get destroyed out there. You battle test it, you put your beliefs and wills out there through the roughest conditions, you bombard until only the strongest survive, until the most solid foundations let themselves out to be seen. You dress down all into the bare body which you carry with you into the next battle. Too much weight wears you, you must stick with the simplest yet most absolute bases before you start building on top. You thus have the most high density of quality robust trustworthy alignment within. Your troops, absolutely loyal to you. You try to kill these things which you have on your side, yet to strengthen the deserved few.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Love and the Creation Beyond the Self :Philosophy:
Love of yourself makes you despise yourself in going for the creation beyond yourself, makes you perish. Becoming the overhuman is being the overhuman perhaps.

* On Silence, Frame Control, and True Questions :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions: it is tendency to think that that thing which we lack so much, which I lack so much, which is keeping in mind the long run in the now, having that greatness instinct even when you are doing the most dull thing, that truthness that you forget and substitute thus for lesser things, thus you think you have nothing to do, when you have very much the very opposite. This you think is solved by throwing in stuff to the mind, like music, motivational videos or whatever, when in reality it is found in silence. Truth is better cultivated alone and alone of the rest. Doing nothing for 2 hours may bring back the truth you once recognized. It can furthermore make you see things your body and mind are perhaps trying to say to you of a long time, that yet didn't go through all the noise, couldn't make it to the surface amidst all the pressure that was put on the moment. More is less. The force builds in you clarity. You have a real question, and don't know the answer? You have a dilemma? Sit in silence and the answers will be clear. A la antiga a la kung fu panda, most of the training and time spent by the warrior is reflecting, looking at the water and seeing it look back. Most of the time is spent in the confinement of your mind, in the light of your shadow, finding yourself. The show that others see is in scope very limited to all you've blessed. It is only the very top of the iceberg that others can see, that others can understand. Your job is to make this blackbox of a thing come to life, take all that only you can understand all that's puzzling the self and figure it all out, all, and only you can, and make it come true in what is seen by others.
people then ask why in movies they have such assuredness, why they do it like in movies, natural unwavering faith in their outcome. True main character stuff, that you go your way and your way is the way. But there it is they know damn better than others the world they want to be, and they make it, and they make others live in it. The stronger the frame the more likely it becomes everyone's frame. The more truthness and suredness the frame carries, the stronger it gets, The more undeniable,
talk about frame control in the life man. this is what they have. What they hold. So much power is in the frame.
I will, the masters of frame know.
those who have learned to live without reality are the ones who come to find their reality and make it. Those who tune to the deeper frequencies of thought
lower levels of questions.
This way is not about feeling moving, but about finding the right direction to move to, then go for it. Finding the worthy opponents, the worthy bridges of overcoming, the worthy flags in top of the mountains, into the overhuman. There are few questions that you must answer in your life, most of people spend life, not enjoy it, actually spend it without facing the questions, perhaps because just off the top of their head they don't see clearly the answer, and they have no interest in seeking deeper. Life is mostly about getting away from noise and finding the gem in the sea. Because its huge, this is the way to go. Quality of decisions. Quality of output into the true great questions

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Sensing Uncomprehended Strength :Life:
Nietzsche, that there is such a thing as sensing strength but not being able to comprehend it. I feel like this we fear, when we sense strength and we comprehend it after, it diminishes it. We don't fear our place in the will to power wheel. Our ego can sleep rested. But if we see strength where we can't understand it. Then we run, then we either deal with it, and let it become our strength or we deny it, we push it further down, slimming down yet expanding the bubble size of the cookie monster. It is power thus to have unseen strength, to have uncomprehended strength.
dude why people admit they are tired. This is a nonsense I cannot tolerate by hearing and not writing no more. Like what. Unbelievable few understand this. Actually ridiculously unbelievable, and perhaps it is children who better grasp the concept. I don't see a world where it making sense. But yeah back on topic,

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment :Life:
Once again the pressure came, but we are still undefeated, had to get out of training, lost the subway because took too much time finding the pass in the bag, thought the pressure was catching up but after all it was chill, then get home and there take too much time but still chill getting to the subway, about a 5 min margin from sub to train, but the subway gets late, is full, had to force myself in, then it stops at a station for like 5 minutes, because someone went unconscious leaving the subway. I sprint to the train, and it was late and we got the w.

* Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment II :Life:
Quotes and questions :Once again the pressure came to us, but we are still undefeated, had to get out of training, lost the subway because took too much time finding the pass in the bag, thought the pressure was catching up but after all it was chill, then get home and there take too much time but still chill getting to the subway, about a 5 min margin from sub to train, but the subway gets late, is full, had to force myself in, then it stops at a station for like 5 minutes, because someone went unconscious leaving the subway. I sprint to the train, and it was late and we got the w.

* On Small Worries and the Meritocracy of Competence :Life:
still quotes and questions:
it is quite pathetic too that small worries take over the lives of people. That the more, people let themselves be controlled and submitted by impulses small little impulses. Like hunger. Chill, I know I'll eat when I get home, so this signal my body is sending is to be ignored. Don't let it stop you. Have control over, and now you are free to fly. Now you can focus on the real bigger things that can throw you off. Now you can come back and join that internal locus of control that you have. That deep self that you know you can't avoid, now you can again connect with him and get aligned.
fame alone isn't something one wants. It is only constricting. If there was such a thing as just being famous, the hell out with it. You couldn't go by any way, seas of people would swarm you, you'd have the private means you may say, but that is not fame, money we associate with fame, yet distinct they remain. Power we associate with fame, goodness of being, skill, competence, no.
the brutal meritocracy of competence Tate I heard say he believes in, and the hell it is true. The hell it is true. It gives the absolute most power and control to us, thus we believe it thus its true, it's the biggest bang for buck belief, it favours you the most, its a fixed point. Investing in your self type of belief.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Living Ahead of Your Time :Technology:
Sometimes its a matter of being young enough to comprehend strength. To see strength and opportunity different from those too old
living ahead of your time. This is a hard of a thing. To see what is going on now and be able to know it will be so futile in the future, the way we do things now, our time, my friend, will all be replaced and seen as dull and slow and dumb. And to picture yourself there at the future looking back on the way we did things, n
thats disconcerting
Like looking at the sound this train makes. I can go through it, doesn't bother me one bit. But somewhere out there in the future is absolute seamless transport, one that extends what this train already does so well for me and allows me, after developing the necessary strength, to be zoned out as if not travelling. To me it is the exact same being here or being at home at the table reading or being at the library reading. Equalizer. Your strength as weapon makes equalizers. But yeah one day rather than this for most displeaserous sound there will be a sound that activates productivity and happiness and meaning all at once, while being able to do what you want and not just remain seated. Distance will no longer be a factor. Time too in this constrict sense that it won't matter to you how much time a trip takes to get there. But still travelling in time will yield losses. you still will lose time by moving forward in time.
most can't even live in the moment let alone be present in the future.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Men in the Matrix :Life:
Quite saddening too to look at grown ass men still in the matrix.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Fluidity of Movement and Unfazedness :Life:
Remember, peace of mind brings fluidity of movement. Have it sorted out, know the direction and achieve peace through low level thinking.
and I'm telling you, if the sound of the subway causes you anger, if you can't take that hit, if hunger makes you lose intention and volition, then my man you are not even playing at the right game yet. You still are battling against yourself. First align yourself, then make the world align to you. And I was guilty of that at some point, by letting small losses that don't matter take me down and make me not understand that I have to be the best, I am. If you let anything external get to you, fix that. Nothing can stop me I'm all the way up. Thus one develops this peace of being with everything external, an unfazedness regarding everything it may bring. You are unreactive to all the fake battles it suggests to you. You know what the real battles are, you know when you should panic and not. The naturality of the cat, unfazed to everything yet knows when its time to go ham. Not afraid of the dark, the last to move, but the first to move the fastest when given enough evidence, the first to get out. Luck takes work. Takes preparation. What stoicism looks like when it isn't covered by all the media spam wording and connotation and glamour vision. Eventually you become virtually undefeatable, there is nothing outside of you that can hurt you. Comfort becomes a luxury, and it can't pollute your heart. In these moments you've seen you climbed, and despite also doing a lot of bullshit, you climbed, this gives you a certainty that you will climb so much more. I am Inevitable. Perhaps because one gets that extra push after seeing others give up. Seeing others getting frustrated gives you energy. Seeing others give up when I ran cross country and endurance events, seeing I won, that gave such an extra push. And a big fat one'- sleepiness. This one is a motherfucker, I lost control on it sometimes

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Freedom of Not Giving a Damn :Life:
Its above all a peace and rest as in the opposite of unrest to not give a damn what others think and especially not caring if they recognize your worth. Short story, they won't period. But not giving a f about that allows you to win so much more, you are not dependent on that vision that others have of you. It lets you keep moving forward through island and sea, through storm and sunset, through hell and paradise and back.
all of a sudden you come back to respecting yourself so much more and valuing your time so much more.
being able to vibe to a music with no headphones and through the sound of the train.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Accepting the Passage of Time :Life:
Further accept the passage of time. Here in the train I haven't checked time in a minute, I have no clue, and I'd rather keep it that way. Time can't work against you, rather it works for you. I have accepted staying here forever and just doing this. One must imagine Sisyphus happy. Don't even know if I'm travelling forwards or backwards

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Endurance and Repetitive Motions :Life:
And man I genuinely believe I could easily get through that chinese torture of the perpetuous drop of water, I think I'd just be chilling. But there is such a thing as going crazy over these unusual repetitive motions, your brain can actually get freaked out. I think at least I have more endurance in this aspect than most.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building Yourself Before Overcoming :Life:
"Over and beyond yourself shall you build. But first you must be built yourself." to prolong and accept as if willing to stop all your life for you, through means of children, asks of you that you have yourself dominated. Now you have accountability, now you can't afford mistakes. All that is to be done is done and all is said too. Are you ready to call it? Well don't if its going to be you and your child against the world, especially if it is against parts of you that you are fighting with. "not only onward shall you propagate onto, but upward!". Overcoming oneself before so then you can reach farther. The higher the leaves are dropped from the tree you have grown to love, the farther they reach, the farther you see.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Surprise Effect of Hidden Knowledge :Life:
What I was saying about you not giving a damn what others think of you and so not trying to show you know, being content with only you understanding yourself and your worth. This it brings the surprise effect when you actually are asked for the opportunity to show your knowledge in a certain specialty of yours. Like me here and there actually getting excited talking about training or whatever to others.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Free Death :Philosophy:
On free death is the title
if you allow your death to come free, my friend you're not. And you pay the price on this loss of control.
it's as if a basketball player didn't retire but rather wait for the day where his knees fell off. We like to mark ends to things, except our life. Or ending a movie or a podcast when the battery of the camera died out.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Relative Aging of Spirit and Body :Life:
The interplay of staying young till late yet aging through some of the victories and truths of youth. Changing in body but remaining in spirit. Changing in dress but staying in body. Nietzsche says in some the heart ages in others the spirit. And this is one hell of a truth people don't take into account, the aging process of each thing that makes one up. Bryan johnson has unlocked the relative aging of all cells, but one ought to go further and see how certain jokes are relative to thoughts relative to how your maturity ages versus how your fashion ages and how your face ages and how your physical condition ages and how your spirit and beliefs age.
you want to evolve, not age, for as long as possible. Never in final form. The natural tendency is alignment of all ages, it's a stable equilibrium. If your spirit is younger than your body, you might go a little too harsh doing sport with your son and end up on the sidelines, effectively aging your body. This perhaps an argument for generality entity, being an all purpose agent. Attacking everything. This I know and do and few do and know.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Preserving the Youth of the Spirit :Life:
I think its way closer than everyone thinks in terms of what you can preserve young, people think spirit is the only one and still lose it

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Longevity and the Late Bloomer :Life:
Understand too that if you are going to have more longevity, you are going to be a late bloomer, all will be increasing the distance to you until age starts working against them. It's a long term game. The most ripe fruit takes the most time being prepped.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Life Affirming Death :Philosophy:
Nietzsche paints the picture that your dying should be that of a life affirming being. Your energy should glow into love of the earthly and human. Free for and free from dying. Do not waste your death, and that means make people ripe from of your life. May your dying pass on the value of your living to others. May you thus let space for life to be lived, that life that you brought about. Lead the life, and die for the students to live theirs. A leader must then give space to his followers so they are their own followers.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Value of Uselessness and Immortality :Philosophy:
That the highest value is conferred to gold for its scarcity, shine and uselessness. By the last one can't measure its value, it is valueless and thereby can be given any value. It is visible by everybody, and few can have it. So the most valuable thing is what everyone sees, what no one can't have, and is useful. But this only confers value. One can define value through uselessness. Immortality confirms itself as the highest value thing if it was wrapped into an object like figure. Control of time. Of the Universe. What would be the gold of the Universe then, for all worlds out there. Perhaps the inside of a black hole? No for it can't be seen. Dark matter isn't luminous enough and perhaps can be useful. Perhaps all useless things are much less known because of that, and so its hard to even name one.

* On Evolution and Competition :Life:
Quotes and questions
think of someone you know and see their evolution, and do not reject that they've changed, do not regret that they've gotten severely better. See the argument there is to make that they are winning in life against you. Confront that possibility for everyone, as if you are playing by yourself against the world.
And the battle never ends. Don't think just because you think you won back then with something that you have the right to not maintain and improve your situation. The fairness of life comes in the absoluteness of the moment, the inexistence of anything beyond that. The past is only your servant, any win either propels you forward or else does nothing.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Bestowing Virtue and Predator of Values :Philosophy:
In search of the bestowing virtue. A predator of values one becomes, but whole and holy calls Nietzsche this mission. To pile up all riches in the soul he says.

* The Bestowing Virtue and Transcending Levels :Philosophy:
The bestowing virtue is an elevation over and beyond. A conquest of the world and yourself, having battled it out, having been through it all, you emerge through the clouds not to be bothered again by those opponents you defeated and took as one of your own. One bridges to the overhuman through expansion of the human all too human. The human is something that is to be overcome. One doesn't progress the quality of life by playing in ever more fields, but by playing in higher fields. By transcending the levels.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- AI, Memory, and the Death of the Internet :AI:
As the internet dissolves into LLMs, into AI, in its search and generate information capacity, those same AIs will have less data to pull from, so more important will become the data that they get from our conversations and interactions with those ais. Suddenly context length can be greater, it no longer suffices to give a good answer, one ought to get a good answer for one's specific situation. So all the context is our previous conversations, not just what we say. It will engulf human behaviour thus, with memory he shifts on the spectrum from writing knowledge to write us. Finally one can look at the death of internet through mediation for AIs. Barely any service will be better done with humans than AI. Quora already has the answer of POE ahead of people's answers. Still we take this bet, its a self generating thing, we bet that our generation and production that feeds the models will help us produce more

* On the Duality of Speed and Depth :Life:
Quotes and questions-
I have these two strong inclinations that yet seem very exclusive to each other. One the efficiency ultra high bandwidth maximizing the speed of my life and consuming more information being exposed to more through information than anyone out there, having seen all the greats and been with them, with their mindsets. And yet the other view of maximum depth and thus no exposure, being only a matter of one's thought, not seeing all thoughts out there. Slow and meaningful.
I have been developing them separately, but it remains to be seen how I will crystallize them into something so much bigger.

* High Bandwidth, Searching Vast Spaces, and Long Term Vision :Technology:
Quotes and questions-
with these big scale information networks as an agent you don't want to get lost on the fluff, you need the ability to search vast spaces and get the most value, reach the gems of it and get out. This contributes to the fast and wide part of my duality that I want to crystallize. The other is the slow and deep, the value you create slowly from within, meaningful. So right now searching google on a topic and retrieving the relevant points of information of all corners you seek is fundamental for you to get stuff done fast. Perhaps a start to solving this is when in the battlefield have the capability to move like hell, to compress all and get it done, so then you have time for deeper things. One gets this idea that by having bandwidth, which has the consequence of effectively increasing intelligence, as you see others moving slowly and are capable of having more output, you get to for example watch youtube videos at 2.5x speed. Which on the one hand, you may contend, makes you remember few and not understand much, but on the other, it allows you to skip all uninteresting stuff and get to the real juice, at which point you may open your ears more by slowing down and deepening. It allows you to search the space so much faster and have so many more options. Don't feel guilty thus if you don't finish something or you didn't pay attention along the way. Move on, the world was not made to see every alley, you must be able to move beyond the dirt that slows you down. If you didn't get something the first time, consider not going in there a second. Consider that maybe you are right and that which perhaps others in the world said was interesting for you simply doesn't do it. Don't worry that you will never have mission complete, there is always something more. The game of life distinguishes itself from video games until now for this aspect of extension. An experiment it is, the path to the overhuman. I'm an experiment, that is perhaps emphasized by the emacs thing we will have. Insofar as these tools are concerned, no learning curve or challenge should let your long term vision fall, you shall bet on the long term. If there is a best, then go for it. Just like in travel, get just those things that change your life.

* On the Unexplored and Untamed :Life:
Quotes and questions-
there is this certain conspiracy and camaraderie and partner in crime vibe of you and the stars, you and your play. It isn't filmed or written down, just yours. Cause we get too caught up in what human built, we live inside that human's life, not to realize that there are still things impenetrable to all other humans. You and the world, not you and this of the humans thing. There is still untamed, there is still unexplored, you can step at any time into places no other will know.

* On Computation and Creation :Technology:
Quotes and question-
Arguably because of P being different from NP you should be careful in verifying work and doing it on your own. If something only takes a bit longer to do on your own rather than verifying what others did, you gain in doing it on your own. Or if there is unwanted work in transforming from others' space of actions and thoughts to your own, rather than just engulfing the problem into your configuration space. Strive for compression when absorbing, strive for going the way when creating. Its about managing computation. Sometimes if others don't prepare their work for you, like just copying and pasting their conversation with the LLM that led them to do some project, then you'd rather do it on your own. It becomes an interesting question whether you should do more of what you can do on your own or what you can't. Cause what you can you should automate if you don't learn any longer from doing it, while you can learn by seeing others do something you can't and trying it. Maximizing learning at every moment isn't conducive to ending projects and getting stuff organized, cause usually the finish of a project you don't learn much you simply want the badge of the end. But in the long term it can allow you to finish bigger more final projects.

* On Bandwidth and Supercharged Learning :Technology:
Quotes and questions
supercharging your learning is a real thing, having that higher bandwidth is a cheat code. The ability to move faster than anyone should be feared by those who don't have it. I will figure this out perhaps on a technological level with AI, beyond getting the most out of time.
George Hotz said this thing that he doesn't need to hide his ideas, for he believes no one can compete with him in doing something with it faster than him. This thing that whatever you have going for you I'm gonna beat you to it. Effectively being ahead of one's time has this component of computation speed, like Nvidia CEO saying their chips allow you to travel in time

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Elevation and the Godlike Will :Philosophy:
Think about it. Elevation brings you closer to the sky, closer to being a god. Having will and being over, that is, overcoming the things gives you god like power. Do you think god worries or spends time on little human things. Rise thus, elevate yourself, gain control of all around you and god shall you be crowned.
one step for me that I haven't taken is elevating beyond good and evil. This overcoming on the path to the overhuman is one Nietzsche dwells a lot with.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power as the Bestowing Virtue :Philosophy:
Power it is the bestowing virtue. Seeking power the way of elevation
will to power. My virtue. Nietzsche preaches the use of this power of virtue into the real, not letting this virtue get shattered by the unreality of the eternal, rather have it very present your virtue, be it very present.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Shadow of the Overhuman :Philosophy:
That we are still a shadow of the overhuman. A projection. A collapse. A reduction in dimension. In volume. A reduction. Harnessing the power of our higher fuller selves on our way to self actualization and self overcoming.
and yet the overhuman is inasmuch the shadow of us. Its non expression comes as our repression. It is our shadow that we fear manifest. Its in there somewhere in the depths of our minds those forces that could elevate us over and beyond, after, like Zarathustra, undergoing. Unleashing the dormant part of ourselves to being the most alive

* Sam Altman on Productivity Enhancement :AI:
Quotes and questions
on his three observations sam altman postulates that in some decades any human can be more productive and impactful than anyone alive today. This comes down to the enhancement of the human through AI for example. Having ever greater tools and abstractions that allows to move at speeds indeed previously inaccessible to humans. We move by train while they are going by car, and in the future movement will be made in air through planes, making each and every obstacles of the ground to which humans gave great efforts to conquer obsolete, our solutions won't be improved upon or rejected but simply irrelevant, the most painful of sentences. When you think of problems of the past, they don't even make sense. We move through them.

* On Social Mirroring II :Life:
Quotes and questions.
so funny the effect of looking at people. If they look at you for example in a train you look away, and collective movement is a thing, if you all of a sudden move people will follow that and adjust themselves too.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Shame and the Right Reasons :Philosophy:
Nietzsche says shame is the history of the human.
yet it seems we are ashamed all for the wrong reasons. We should be ashamed when you see someone suffering more than us, someone working harder than us, someone learning more than us, doing more than us, and above all living more life than us, loving living more than us, or living loving more than us, or wanting more than us, or dreaming higher than us, or believing greater than us. Pitying rather than being ashamed Nietzsche says, those bastards pitiful bastards.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Irrelevancy and the Ghost's Freedom :Philosophy:
The greatest unfairness, says Nietzsche, sickness I called it before, is not to be repugnant to someone, but him meaning nothing. Irrelevancy is the most disarming of experiences. Yet it brings you a certain distance away from the humans, gets you away from the noise. Gives you a certain freedom. Being a foreigner everywhere and having no one that knows you or cares about you is still freeing. You have no obligation towards anyone, you live in third person, as if watching humans, as a ghost. You can walk around with no pressure, no standard to meet, no form to take. Having nothing to hold onto, having no home, still gives a hell of a ride.

* What is Life With Mind and Matter and Autobiographical Sketches- The Scale of Sustainable Complex Life :Physics:
That sustainable complex life must find a scale where there are no too many fluctuations, where measurement makes sense and accurate laws can be deduced. Thus too small of life here on earth would have to fight brownian motion, would have little control over environment, and the time scales at which it can work are much more little. The bigger the organism the bigger scale in space and time, thus you see suns and planets exist in the most stable of configurations. They need stability to grow into that scale and live on it.

* What is Life With Mind and Matter and Autobiographical Sketches- The World That Rewards Shallowness :Life:
This world at times rewards shallowness and width, for conversations don't usually get to the point where you show depth, rather a lot of conversations are best suited for those who know a lot but don't really know it. Books are read on their covers, their titles, but do you actually know about the thing you know exist? Its cool to know that hitler wrote a book, do you actually know what its about, have you read it? Sometimes we get too fixated on having meta knowledge without actually diving into content.

* The Champion Has a Name :Life:
Quotes and questions
there is a certain pain, but to me it goes away very fast and only rarely comes about, about being the only one. About seeing most around me making the wrong decisions, not understanding deeper things. There has never been none and there is only one. Me. The champion has a name baby. Not but now in seriousness, this is an experiment on human potential, this is a self actualization of the overhuman. I carry this message. I came so humanity understands itself, so the universe understands itself, so I understand myself. Just from a very projected to the ground point of view, no one out there has the grip like I do on knowledge and mindset, no one has as many doors open as me. There is no world expert at the intersection of many fields, let alone those that don't exist.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero Who Must Not Become Priest :Philosophy:
For a hero to not fall in the trap of the priest, he must come back around and be at peace with all that of hero was he. Know deep down inasmuch vigor that one has found the best path and that one must be content with knowing that alone, needing not to show others his vision thus. If one has suffered, know that it was voluntary, accept in the extreme case that all suffering one has yielded was voluntary and wasn't needed to get where one's got. Thus freeing oneself from the temptation of imposing that to others, acting as much part of the people as any other inverse of hero has done. Swallowing it whole. Not becoming priest of being a hero. For that imposes form and structure, form and structure that, as much as one has given his life to perfect, one has to accept the futility of it beyond anything that transcends the self. That any other will not understand this vision one preaches, that any other, alone, yet parallel to all others that move beyond the masses and rise to become themselves, will have to find his own form and structure. Thus one paradoxically reaches a state where one has the most to say, yet none of it can be heard. For example, after a long tribulous yet self actualizing career one is in the position to relate it all, yet it can't produce, that narration cannot bring further value than that career has produced. Only after doing all the climb does one know the way to climb. Only after all the work can one get the view that could have previously led him up. Thus the hero fools himself one last time reaching and expressing all his heroicity, not being able to contain it within.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero's Last Self-Deception :Philosophy:
Thus the hero fools himself one last time, now that he wants to express his heroicity.
he doesn't understand all he did was for himself. All was voluntary. He starts to believe is mode a little too much, his narrative and vision start to become real to him, so much so that he wants to impose them onto others. He fails to realize that all that in him was hero is not anymore by this sharing. He cannot produce more than he has through making public all he went through to produce his stuff.
structure

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Knowing the Way Only at the End :Life:
It is quite ironic that only at the end of the climb does he know exactly how to climb and did what he did. Only when he has much to share does he not have much to do.
he doesn't realize he is distancing himself from hero as he remembers his tale. Hero in reality was he that made the way unknowingly, discovered the way. The hero makes itself. He can hope that the structure that has taken so much out of him to be produced to get him somewhere can serve as map to someone else, not realizing it amounts to nothing. His vision amounts to nothing, and can only be as good as the places he got with it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Every Hero Must Go Through Their Own Journey :Life:
All the heros that will be made will have to go through the journey themselves, developing their own structure in many ways probably different. They can't make others understand their way. Thus one alone, yet in parallel with all the heros that there once was, rises above the masses and is unable to give back to them. To even show one has something to give to them

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Accepting the Voluntariness of Suffering :Life:
Not falling prey to one's own story, that one thus shouldn't preach, is in part about accepting the extreme case that all that one did was voluntary and not needed. That all the suffering one put oneself

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Yours Is Yours :Life:
Not falling pray to one's own story, that one thus shouldn't preach, is in part about accepting the extreme case that all that one did was voluntary and not needed. That all the suffering one put oneself through was voluntary. That all you did it for was for the joy of it, accept this and all other reasons be yours. Giving away, painfully, that your way is no more useful, it was only useful to you in your way. This brings exclusivity. Computational irreducibility in that others can't skip through their journey by knowing yours. Yours is yours. heros don't tell their story. They can put it out there even if it makes them feel better, but there will be no ears.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Prisoners of Their Own Delusion :Philosophy:
Prisoners of their delusion that their reality is not just theirs, even if they are right about it, one may go as far as to say. Giving away your story, what you so dearly deared real, having it be squandered, its tough. The realization that one may have wasted life away is as daunting as it is irrational. Cause the momentum builds up, and if one is brought down from the height of the world he has created, if that doubt starts creeping and tearing it, so that one can no longer even enjoy it, so that one cannot not just remember, but live with it, that it brings the highest of men to his knees. That one in this descent can thus fall far below the level of the ground. There is such a thing as sensitive information. One can easily overestimate his power against words and information, the overhuman overcomes this, so that he may die for words, yet live all the same for those he wishes. Most don't dare weather this storm. There is really such a thing as knowledge of something that can make you have no drive to live, lose all your will. I must go beyond this if I am to go further. The world is cruel, and just like we don't really grasp infinity, or the value of life, we don't grasp either what it feels like to know some things. We are but one human. How much can one human take. It is as easy to let something have no effect and let it kill you. A true 50\50, the fairest of things

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Desiring Both Coherence and Self-Actualization :Philosophy:
That one may desire both coherency and self actualization, that then one would find peace, owning both the model and reality, doing the right and having understood the reason to it. Paving the right journey and knowing its true meaning. Paving the path as much as one knows its story. Mostly we don't. We move for different motives than those that will be recounted further on by those who tell the tales of the heros. Heroes were thus never understood. again they couldn't be. The complicity alone between them should suffice for them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Priests as Victims of Delusion :Philosophy:
The picture here painted by Nietzsche is that priests are victims of delusion. They filled the gaps their spirit had with God, thus becoming a simulacrum of a good soul, whose base crumbles at touch. Lackless they think themselves to be, yet lackful, via unaddressing, they are if they are wrong. They put themselves through pain, cause pain is the most real feeling, to start to believe in their own narrative, they thus invest and dive head on to their voluntary movement, and sometimes may forget that it is a voluntariness, that in the worst case there was nothing to be gained by that undergoing. It is in one's interest of maximizing life that one believes that each moment should be maximized, that one owes nothing to anyone or any story. There is no memory. We live a local life. The moment is all there is. Thus at any moment one can be lived anew. One can totally change what one is. Don't let yourself be slave of your past plans, of what you thought back then or what someone else did. If you are so stuck in the framework of your church, think that the god you preach would beyond all have wanted you to maximize your short stay at his hotel.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Rule of Thumb: Reject Those Who Sell Misery :Life:
Rule of thumb- if someone tries to sell you misery, maybe for some more or less eloquent reason, you probably don't want it.
so always have this pinch of realism in whatever narrative you create for yourself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- From Smallest to Greatest: Same Flesh :Philosophy:
That continuous still is the transition from smallest to greatest human, topologically equivalent they are, naked they are but of the same flesh. There is no semblance of the overhuman yet

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- When Reading Yields Nothing :Writing:
Just rode the whole chapter and didn't get anything.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- When Reading Yields Nothing II :Writing:
Just read the whole chapter and didn't get anything.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Virtue as Individuality :Philosophy:
That perhaps your virtue is your individuality, thus putting yourself in front guide of your ship so you go your way. Everyone is an expert at good and evil, points Nietzsche out, yet there is no overhuman yet. All that is created by humans can likewise be created by yourself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Virtue Sold Cheaply :Philosophy:
So cheap everyone sells virtue to themselves. So many virtues lying around, but so little selfs to carry their weight. Thus so lightly they invest to their virtue. So transitory their virtue is, rather a momentaneous ego booster than an unshakable tree that has grown out of them

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Let Yourself Be Your Greatest Virtue :Life:
Let yourself be your great virtue and your great source of virtue. You should be your own greatest project. The overhuman sees in you its virtue. Virtue is not the state but the momentum. The virtue of the champion before he becomes the champion. The virtue is thus best seen naked, when it doesn't come with all that it has produced. Let the virtue guide you to become you. Let the joy of virtue be in itself all you need to keep on building it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drinking Life Through Others' Bottles :Life:
Unfortunate for those who only drank the pleasure of life through other's bottles. What a shame that there are those who will never know beyond the masses, never come to see their way in life without them. Unclean and impure is the flame that you build with them, by putting wood into their construct.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Masses Must Be Wrong :Philosophy:
Untrue the masses's spirit has to be, for there is still no overhuman. Wrong they have to be in some ways.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Masses' Inherited Power :Philosophy:
We shouldn't neglect the amount of power that the masses hold and pass generation to generation, how much they can shape all of yesterday and tomorrow, a la 1984, even if no single one of them can grasp all their impact, or even know why the spirit is a certain way. Those that started and knew their reasons may be far gone, yet their consequences can live on if they aren't intentionally taken care of. We can be stuck on obeying orders from those that don't even exist.
the other side of the coin of collective building and standing on the shoulders of giants

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Knowing the Border of the Game :Life:
