being on another different level is much about being aware of the border of the game that the masses play, having no delusion about its scope and its domain of effectiveness. Not being fooled by the glow of what the game gives to those who play it. Those who will it their lives to trying to win this game. Those who thus rest their power on the belief on this game.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Be a Strong Wind Above :Life:
Be a strong wind that flies over everyone, lives above the masses, has its representative a la god down there, that yet shows no glimpse of the sheer power it holds above.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Dangerous Thought of Redundancy :Life:
the dangerous that those of the masses may not be needed, they may be redundant. That perhaps most people playing the game of the masses and drinking from its well may truly be irrelevant. That maybe most live truly unmeaningful lives. That Zarathustra almost chokes to death on this. This is a dangerous thought not to be carried out to its practical consequences, but to make one run away from the condition of having its life given away for outrem. It puts urgency into living one's way. This rabble shall carry yourself out of it. Shall be the source of your awareness and escape into your life. Thus one intentionally starts living. Being able to smell and swallow the masses pills, yet totally separated, verily of spirit, from this wind of the low lands. Pay it no mind. Pay every single ounce of mind you have into your way. Yet be unrepugnable by anyhow.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Thank the Many Too Many :Life:
Thank thus the many too many that allowed you to become one, the many too many pieces that allowed you to become whole and sharp. They showed you the way to climb.

* On the Preacher of Equality :Philosophy:
Preacher of equality of all another great idealists. Untrue to the nature of the world. Incapable of accepting the unfairness of the world sure, but incapable to adapt to win and win in that world? That is inefficient. The concept of justice is blind to the possibility that the present is all there is, and thus is fond of a revenge sentiment buildup in the soul. One thus forges within these forces of narrative, these unreal motives that drive one away from. The at every moment least action path. One runs the danger of dwelling on the past and thus trying to force equality. One can be thrown away from the path of victory for some past centered idea that instead could be made of more use by channeling of its energy into will or even, at times, of total withdrawal from memory.

* On Enforcing Sameness :Philosophy:
the preacher of equality may not just look for equality of justice but making everyone have the same condition as him, enforcing that others be the same as him.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Those Who Want to Punish Cannot Live in the Present :Philosophy:
Those that want to punish cannot live in the present. They cannot believe in the unreality of the past. They seek power for the wrong reasons, to be at peace with the world they live in, not understanding neither that it isn't theirs nor that it has anything that needs rescue.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- From Diversity to Unification :Philosophy:
The human is built on diversity, in diversification. The overhuman, on unification, the full cycle of what the seed that was planted as humans came to be had to give. From that on, its no longer human. Here we go.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Values as Signs of Overcomings :Philosophy:
Values like good and evil, like signs of overcomings that the human still has to go through, says Nietzsche. There are such clashing signs of overcomings in one's life. If something still occupies your thought, you haven't overcome it, and if you haven't won it, you also haven't overcame it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Life Wants to Grow to Fly :Life:
Tis life itself, that is, my life, wants to grow into the heights, so that it can go far when it learns to fly.
very simple phrase but that explains a lot

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming Builds the Height to Fly :Life:
Thus with one's overcoming of values and concepts, one builds the height within, one wins the game, before one can fly away to level up. Weapons these values and concepts are for the destruction of the life you've been living into a greater one without them

* On Talent, Equality, and Winning the Moment :Life:
I still hold the absolute belief of an equality but that to me is not this one. And maybe it is. Maybe I'm incoherent, and that gives me more power. But I have this belief from a very long time that Everyone has the same talent when integrated over all possible parameters. At least when one is born. That everyone has one thing they were born to be the best in the world in. But even here equality doesn't hang on very long, for environmental factors outside your control and, above all, your actions that are in your control
Yet at every living moment there can be as a semi thought experiment leaderboard of who is winning the moment, who is winning life in this very moment, who is having the best life this very moment. And it is possible that one wins every single time. So no equality in that sense can make for this. It is possible to live a life better than every other combined.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Work Done in Vain :Life:
The notion of stuff done in vain is one most visceral to me in what can be related to the tarantula's preaching of equality. That it is possible to work very hard on stuff that doesn't matter for anyone, maybe not even for you. That your intentions sometimes may count for nothing, and utilitarianism wins. That indeed peace of mind, like many other things, may be a choice, and that you may live your life lying strategically, having all the inner workings figured out by yourself and show only the final product to people.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Redemption from Adorations :Philosophy:
Redemption from adorations. This was the aspect of the free spirit master of deserts, will of a lion seeker, the truthful, that most spiked me. Redeem yourself from things that you love and are addicted to unintentionally. Learn to want to want what you want. All those things that you caught onto, don't let them have power over you. Use them

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Spirit as Life of Life :Philosophy:
Spirit as the life of life. The aliveness of your aliveness. The reality of your reality. The power of your power.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Human Sees the Power of Spirit :Philosophy:
And that yet no human can see the power of spirit, what it can do.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Finding Resonance with the World :Life:
There is this aspect of the self coupling with the environment, whether it be nature or the like. When you are in tune to the world, what it is trying to say, what you are trying to know. You can achieve resonance with the world you live. You can find resonances with places, they catch your spirit and set it free and dancing. Verily they do just that, resonance- they amplify you into heights you haven't heretofore imagined, they allow you to go further and get more spirit, more power of power. There is much advice spoiled and spilled on the world that converges on finding resonances in them, and then exploiting them maximally. Not stopping when one finds one. There is an unstill and untillable, undistilled and undistillable force within. A craving that one with words falls short of covering, and that one should follow like one follows light into a tunnel, a glimmer of hope into the way out of plato's cave and into the real world. An instinctive drive that lets one fearless, flow. Fearless and without narration above all. That is what makes the moment so pure and unnamed and untamed. There is nothing you can write about it to bring you closer to it. There is nothing you can write about it that doesn't destroy it as it is being written. One who recounts the moment or narrates it or describes it cannot be living it. One has to distill the undistillable to pass it on, thus one can't pass but a wrong proxy, a simulacra delta dirac that for only he who truly knows it holds value and holds tremendous value. If people were just to stay shut they would see much beyond what they have lived, they would recognize the heights and the value of the moments that only they had.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as Tool, World as Experience Beyond It :Philosophy:
Language is a tool of thought and communication, but the world as an invention is much older and perfected than it. Your eyes tell you so much that you underestimate just cause you can't put it into words. Your experience goes so much beyond what can even be projected onto and represented by language. If I couldn't write down and contribute to the cult of the overhuman through human's progress, if I couldn't document this journey of the overhuman, then I would just allow myself to experience. To experience reality better than anyone who is already too closed minded to think that the resonances and the coupling was to be made solely through language this new thing.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Saying a Word Ruins the Moment :Philosophy:
There is some very very real sense in which saying a word ruins the moment. You have the temptation to say it to show others you are all on the same page, but it just ruins it. One just had to follow this temptation and label the moment. Not allowing it to grow, not allowing it to be something else, what it wants to be, what it is. Pure is the moment, that it is uncaptured, unfigured out, unsolved in your heart, your heart, you now free spirit, free from the temptation to categorize, free from conquering instead of discovering, the former can always be done, the latter only a seeker, a truthful with a greater will than the last who dared honestly and truthfully to go as far as anyone, you raw likewise resonant coupling untamed beast that no other can put into words or he'll miss you, you now live over the human.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Embracing Incoherence and Open Doors :Philosophy:
You that aren't afraid of being incoherent, you that embrace the pain of not having it all figured out, you that live with all unfigured out. Maybe only you overhuman can figure out everything everywhere all at once. You never cut yourself too short. You always allow room, which is a burden for one to never see the end of things, it is harder than it seems to leave all doors open. One can be consumed by one's freedom, by one's will and reach. One's sense of ignorance and smallness can take over. You thus walk the path of no return, of no returns for you never finish anything, you never erase the possibility of it being necessary in the final great reconstruction and unification of all that you have, or haven't, figured out. the path of progression, of choice, joy you make outta those two, just to complete Ben Goertzel's three principles for life. There is the possibility that the puzzle will tame all doors you opened to be solved, that it takes everything everywhere all at once.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Dilemma of the Overhuman :Philosophy:
The dilemma of the Zarathustra, the dilemma of an overhuman, that is solitary, yet isn't understood by the regular human, and the other overhumans solitary too, hardly will the conditions follow that they illuminate one another if they exist. Yet to go to the untruthful human as a famous wise man, or be truthful and crazy.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Everything Fits Only at the End :Life:
A more real sense in which everything is supposed to fit together only at the end. Rest only at the end not in the middle. Its that only after you've gone through it all perhaps are you ready to go through it all over and get the parts that you didn't. Now that you know it you can actually understand it, considering of doing this with this book. Cause if Zarathustra talking to humans or the overhuman talking to humans, they wouldn't understand a thing at first, order is taught, for you learn biases when learning certain things first. You get exposed not to things you understand and get progressively closer to the vision that is being teached but rather you start getting exposed to it and try to hang on and actually start getting it. So at the beginning a lot of what you see in it you don't understand and can only understand after being through other things that that vision has to offer. So technically you can only start to understand the whole thing after ending it. This is my vision. If you're presented with a whole new vision then this is the path to take. Perhaps we cut ourselves super inefficiently short by only reading a book like this once. Perhaps we did 80 percent of the work to get only 20 per cent of the rewards.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- First Hear, Then Resonate :Philosophy:
First hear the sound of the world, only then can you vibrate to its frequency. Only then can you hope to Resonate with it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Greatest Praise of Life :Life:
Maybe the greatest praise of life is the love and will and desire and absolute desire to maximize it. Especially when it doesn't flow your way, when it seems your life is the most fucked.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Dance Between Life and Wisdom :Philosophy:
The dance and the day and Zarathustra and his lover life, wisdom his little secret that life may be worried about. Wisdom and life so similar, thus competing.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Silent Grave of Past Moments :Life:
The silent grave of the past of those moments that are constantly being killed.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Power of Dead Past Moments :Life:
The power of those dead past moments that come so quick and catch your love of life and arouse your ultimate desire that yet leave traceless and leave you alone at sea. They all make your life the greatest gift. One that even with almost no retribution still is worth the shot.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Who Possessed Whom :Life:
Who possessed who between you and your moments, and the disloyalty that you both innocently abandon each other.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Smile That Fades :Life:
A smile that dies away at a distance. The most harshest of murders for the moment, for it doesn't get exterminated, but rather fades away, never really dying, never really warning. The death of one and onto the next, this perhaps could really be the most fundamental law of nature and law of life, nature in the wild sense. Outros melhores!

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Playful Spirits of Youth :Life:
The playful spirits that played along with you in those youthful wisdom filled life filled visions that fled with the moment, fled much more thoroughly, much more costly than those of today, which we take easy, but would we that we would grab with teeth and nail those much precious moments that were truly lived. Moments that instead of dying live ferociously. Transcend time. Eternal they were cut short, murdered our innocence, now we can only get a close up, we lack mostly reality. That's what we want to get back. That's my job to live every moment like the overhuman learned from his underhuman child that yet went over and beyond all that humans come to be ripe for. Freedom is interesting to ponder
when it was fun to imagine! when you could and were willing to and could afford to throw a day off to some imagination, irrespective of the whole world that wasn't inside your creation. Indifferent to any complications. This people seek, the freedom from complication, but they still only with that fall short of the full child experience. Conserve principles that you long had planted in your heart, and get back to those you have abandoned so they come on even stronger. Everyday is a new day, many people forget that and its consequences.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Death of Natural Life :Philosophy:
At ultimatum, the death of the natural life. The death of the dance that one has when one is children with the world. For other adorations unknown to life and nature and wisdom we have come to be convinced by. Visions and hopes that don't live to see another day, that stayed there where the moment left and lost its spirit, its life of life.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming the Lost Life :Life:
Overcoming the lost life is the matter of regaining the lost spirit.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Will That Survives All Seasons :Philosophy:
The will survives. The will is the all equalizer greatest common divisor. Concurrent and parallel and whole and single. The will stays unentered and only affected on the exterior past the seasons and the weathers. It phases the seasons and seasons the weathers and weathers the storms. Will to life. Will to power. Will of spirit. For too long have we repressed the unholy our will. We have forgotten to live by it. To follow it. We have come to learn to believe more in unreal thing over the ever real will. We need more reality. Find the will. Let it free. Unleash the dangerous controlled will. I will my will, and I will my life and my life wills it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Will Needs Fulfillment :Philosophy:
Silently thus the will pass by still unchanged. It isn't complete. It needs fulfillment, and it projects its reality onto every other lesser.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Ancient Wheel Deserving Rediscovery :Philosophy:
Ancient wheel that needs and deserves rediscovery, deserve completeness. Deserves to see the light of day. Deserves to see itself become what it is. Destiny it is to bring about the reality of the most real.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- So Much Death of Moments :Life:
So much death of moments, that at last at least the will may see itself in this life or the next! Unredeemed is the unfree one. One can't be fulfilled unless one is free.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom of Will in Childhood :Life:
It is interesting again to think about freedom in childhood. But are you free? The phrase to put. Here and everywhere.
Well the will of the child is free and set free, even if all the rest is imposed onto her, even if she doesn't know any better. She does know better. For over time we trade this life affirming freedom of will for freedom of world where our will isn't free. That is the tradeoff Kaczynski wasn't willing to take. Incompleteness of will is the disease of mankind as it entered human age. And its overcoming is the cure to bring about the overhuman, whose freedom is the most real and powerful.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Brings the Moment to Life :Philosophy:
Will that brings that moment to life. That makes the moment eternal, that saves from the genocide, that transcends time, that outnatures the child.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will to Truth and Freedom :Philosophy:
Will to truth, that is yet unfounded doesn't it come from the naturality of the newborn overhuman. No one who isn't free can be driven by the will to truth to truth.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Overcoming :Philosophy:
Self overcoming, I am to be overcome, the human is something that is to be overcome.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willing the Thinkability of All Beings :Philosophy:
Nietzsche suggests willing the thinkability of all beings, of all things. Can this help you? Well yeah for you can conquer their thoughts, you can spread yourself more, you can will the world to your will, thus your will wills it. You can will the world to your win and win the world to your will. More freedom more power more life. Your will shall in essence be universal, something that can talk with any being, something that can dance and resonate to the frequencies of the world your world. The world as will and representation.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will the Greatness of the Universe :Philosophy:
So will the greatness and power of the will to bring about the overuniverse. The overmoment. The overlife and the overspirit. The overpower of your will. Believe thus that all the universe is watching your very will in action, all of it talks to you and between one another about you. That what you do in life echoes in eternity. Let every moment of your life echo in eternity and eternally in yourself. And every eternal life echoes in your moment. And the whole world resonates, thus progression and freedom.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Commands the Way :Philosophy:
Will commands the way. If truth you seek truth you can truth you will, yet truth is in the act of the will in the overmoment. You can make truth not in the sense that there is no truth but in that there is truth and you can make it, the best of both worlds, should you dare to believe it. Thus we start at the overbelief.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Godlike Belief, Will, and Action :Philosophy:
Call your will to become that of a God. Let your belief and will and action thus carry themselves to that of a God. Godlike belief with Godlike will and no action is deprivation. Godlike belief with no will is waste.

* The Competition of Wills and Beliefs :Philosophy:
Again the world is a competition of wills, as much as it is a competition of ideas and beliefs. Who wills it stronger will have it willed. What he wills Stronger will be. Thus will is the passport to freedom. And yet to the privileged slavery of having to act to avoid deprivation. Preachers of equality don't notice the absolute equality of will and its access.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Belief, Will, Action, and Reality :Philosophy:
Things are more possibly real the more they are believed. They become the more real the more they are willed. They are the most real the more they are acted on.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Believe in the Power of Your Reason :Philosophy:
You shall thus believe harder in the power of your reason, that your reason be so great it is understood and propelled by every living piece of computronium that this world has to offer and in general in your spirit, the power of your power.

* Health vs Performance :Life:
Quotes and questions:
health and performance are different things. Is health a part of your performance or your performance a part of your health.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Smooth and Malleable World :Technology:
Smooth and malleable do you want the world. Of a computationally to your will efficient you want its material, of malleability to your will, blood of the same blood, comrade do you want it. Visible, and touchable, and changeable do you want it. Willable and thinkable, one which to every activity of yours is lower than you, obeys your overpower over it, it is equal to you in its essence, for then it is conquerable, competable, of a war like kind, and you shall win it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All Matter as Mirror of Spirit :Philosophy:
All matter shall be mirror and reflected image of the spirit, says Nietzsche. Mirror for you shall see yourself in it everywhere. Reflected for it shall see you and be according to you.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The World as Big as Your Will :Philosophy:
The world as will and representation. As your entire will. The world is as big as your will allows it to be, it is as big as you will it. It shall be big and yours.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Beyond All Overcomings :Philosophy:
See this will beyond all overcomings which you face before it. Beyond good and evil, beyond valuations that others will to the detriment of your almighty will to power. If there is subjective it shall be yours. It shall be your way all that isn't predefined, and that is all, for you near god define everything now.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming the Creator of Worlds :Philosophy:
I am become life, the creator of worlds. You like a god of gods would want to create a world that is yet so much beyond himself that one may lay back and kneel in admiration. You overhuman shall bring the overuniverse. If you dare you can create beyond all that you have made, beyond yourself, your world. The cycle closes. You are already a god when the world is yours, so overgod must you be to make something even the bigger. Ultimately this world isn't enough for you, this life isn't enough to fill your appetite.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Full Until Intoxication :Life:
Full you want to be, until intoxication, until you get disgusted at this world of so much living it and eating it. You hope for a meal that now challenges you.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcome: Two Meanings :Philosophy:
You gotta overcome the world to overcome it. Overcome has two meanings that the language till now driven by the thought of the people wasn't considerate and strong enough to distinguish. Overcome in the aspect of overcoming, as in working through it, to get to the place of overcoming that comes after when you precisely don't work through it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Create the World That You Obey :Philosophy:
You want in every odd step to create the world that you obey by virtue of its obedience. It supersedes yourself so much that you find selflessness through it that was made out of the ultimate selfishness.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Unwise Outsource Their World :Philosophy:
The unwise see life like a river that they ride for fortune, they see it eternal, they underestimate beyond the maximum of the space of daring beliefs their role in the river, their importance, their necessity for its flow, they guard themselves of responsibility, hiding behind the naturalness that the eternal they imagine seems to have. They outsource their world.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Idealizations Needed to Light the Fire :Life:
That with no idealizations you can still light a fire deep within your heart. That shall be your power, that you need no rescue of higher powers, that the very real powers shall be all you need, that yet all the powers shall be what you seek and want.
that greatness yet can be measured through your reach, the power of the drives you are able to hold within. The power of your power, your spirit, is the measure of the power which you can hold through those drives that want to escape you, want to be free yet are slaves to your mastery you that exercise control over all that your mind can fathom. The stillness of holding the universe at one fist, above all its an ability of shutting off everything you want. People underestimate how incapable they are of shutting all down. Meditation is the very start where you can let your thoughts go. But people don't understand they can get attached to stuff deeper than passing thoughts, in fact some live a whole life a way that wouldn't be if they could shut off some drives, if they knew themselves and made themselves. You should be a universal instructor and constructor of yourself. You are a universal machine capable of being anything, and a universal constructor capable of building it out whatever it is.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Easy Ways Out of Reality :Philosophy:
There are easy ways out of reality, you can lose it, you can lose grasp of it, hard is to hold all drives still and hold all options open and feel the full power of superposition that your true power implies. Most people would rather collapse their whole into something easier something more graspable by themselves and others, something which can be written, something understandable, something rational, justifiable, not a raw untamed wild beast that goes beyond the projections the mind uses to exercise control, goes beyond the very finite limits tractable to our mind, beyond any language and beyond any program, beyond concepts and conceptions, beyond idea and experience.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Easy Ways Out of Reality II :Philosophy:
There are easy ways out of reality, you can lose it, you can lose grasp of it, hard is to hold all drives still and hold all options open and feel the full power of superposition that your true power implies. Most people would rather collapse their whole into something easier something more graspable by themselves and others, something which can be written, something understandable, something rational, justifiable, not a raw untamed wild beast that goes beyond the projections the mind uses to exercise control, goes beyond the very finite limits tractable to our mind, beyond any language and beyond any program, beyond concepts and conceptions, beyond idea and experience.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Humans as Masters of Making Things Understandable :Philosophy:
Easy ways out are what humans thus have become masters of. We should marvel at the little world we have made for ourselves and our little heads and scopes, and sight and vision. Our greatest masterpiece as a species is that we made something understandable, we picked out the best piece of the world to be understood and relied on.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Lost in Fiction, Against Reality :Philosophy:
One can then get lost in his way out of reality, in his fiction man loses himself, and his fiction talks to him so gently he even turns against the very reality that brought him there. One can start to despise the reality that now takes the form of shadow of a greater cause.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Paradoxical Ungratefulness of Fiction :Philosophy:
The paradoxical ungratefulness of humans beyond their capacity of fiction, that they should be immensely grateful to reality that they can see beyond it, that they can venture far and forward, yet some become salty of their reality not being that which they can see which they haven't brought about.

* On Life Affirmation as Persistent Quality :Philosophy:
Beyond the reality of the world this affirmation of life and the self and the world and the yes saying to all that exists, all that all the more is but a spark, rather a persistent quality, persistent qualities are scarce in a world that at every moment takes from you all that you had that very moment.

* On Life Affirmation as Practical Course of Action :Life:
Faced with the reality of the world it is the most useful, the most pragmatic course of action yet much of ideal course of inaction it is too. Its this affirmation of life and the self and the world and the yes saying to all that exists, all that all the more is but a spark, rather a persistent quality, persistent qualities are scarce in a world that at every moment takes from you all that you had that very moment.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Having Seen It All: Light and Free :Philosophy:
When one has seen it all and one has been beyond the ideal and the real, when one has digested the ugly and the beautiful, one lives light, unafraid of what may come next, for there is nothing that one has been hiding from. There is nothing one has kept in the back of his mind feeding of the attention it doesn't get. There is no surprises, no secret, no cards under the table. One has no worry but to live. Powerful he can be and directive and assertive and directing and precise he can be with his allocation of his power and spirit. Forward he can move at every moment, not ever having to look back. Uncertainty is the terrain of this overhuman that is characterized by the juggling of all contradictions and drives that opposed one another yet through him coexist. Only through him. God of superposition that he handles it all and has none of it truly figured out, is able to withstand that he doesn't have anything figured out, that maybe only at the end does it all come together, yet that his path there can now traverse many dimensions unavailable to those who couldn't resist the growing of the bubble that tightens them up in their own system. That he can find things where others can't. Relations that others can't. Compartmentalization reduces RAM usage but massively lowers potential of output.
this is the blessing of dimensionality, as opposed to the curse of dimensionality..

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Conditions for Greatness Are Disadvantageous :Life:
Conditions for your most fundamental greatness are obviously disadvantageous, if that greatness is truly the motive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Conditions for Greatness Are Disadvantageous II :Life:
Conditions for your most fundamental greatness are obviously disadvantageous, if that greatness is truly the motive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Fortifying Beliefs :Philosophy:
Among those beliefs that self fortify are the belief that power can be attained, can be good, and that all drives can be fulfilled. Fundamentally placing no limit in the power of your being, in how much you can juggle. Everything everywhere all at once. It is possible that it is possible to maximize every drive, and that in fact the pushing of any one drive is independent of any other, that they all add up that they all interfere constructively, however hard may it be.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drives That Are Contrarian and Inaccessible :Life:
Drives those that are as tested as much as they are contrarian, inaccessible. Those that language or program treat poorly, in modernity or in humanity. Yet find no reason to ditch the easy ones that most already have. There is no further merit to contrarian than that it can work

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Stretching the Soul Through Extreme Drives :Philosophy:
Through extreme drives and opposites stretching the soul Pushing its limits. So that one may produce beyond what was already produced. The more these elastic drives are pushed together the greater the potential and the greater the flight when they get released.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Valuations as Arbitrary Self-Fulfilling Principles :Philosophy:
That valuations as arbitrary as the self fulfilling principles of power sometimes carry people away, they thus drift by the hands of power of else who's.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Will on the River of Becoming :Philosophy:
"Your will and your values have you placed on the river of becoming." you bet your values on yourself. You bet your power on your power. You are a mixture of what it is that you bet in and what it is that those who bet on you are.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will More and It Shall Be :Philosophy:
And will to power comes from a who who wills it. Will it more and it shall be.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Choose With Whom You Cross the River :Life:
You choose with whom you cross the river. With your becoming or someone else's being.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Becoming or Someone Else's Being :Philosophy:
You choose with whom you cross the river. With your becoming or someone else's being. You embark with your values or someone else's. You are either the master of your ship or you are in someone else's "frota"

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Worship Only Your Own Will :Philosophy:
And if you are going to worship something, please be it not the will of another human, you that believe in the ultimate power of your will that you would never do that. You that believe in your spirit own your ship as much as you own the whole ocean in which all ships sail forth

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Always Win: The Meta-Belief :Life:
My brother ruben said hypnosis- win, I always win. What if you are crazy enough to believe you always win? That is advantageous, if of course you still learn from your mistakes, you simply have the meta belief that whatever happens was still a win in the long run. That makes you keep up the pace, never letting go for you are in a winning position and keep it must be. You never have reason to be let down, you always go into the next moment as a winner shall you be.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Own the River :Life:
You own the river and thus it propels you even further, for you ride your wave and that of the world, for you make the world move with the current that brings you farther.
So even if the wills of other's that you worshiped manage to be kept away from your ship the river can still be against you, but there you know it and can fight it. Thus a la descartes one must, beyond truth and into will the world maker, we must reject all thought that isn't clear and correct, all wills that we have not tasted and proven, for one's will to be pure, one has to build it from the ground up. One must accept the nothing of the world to make it fully his own. One must believe in its unreality to make it his own reality.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- They Can Have the Recipe :Life:
They can have the recipe but they can't make the sauce. They see a very distilled and to their world compressed view, a shallow projection of the immensity of your will they can't grasp. At the deeper level they can't read your will. You must make that so. It should be so yours as so not of the world and of everyone and be written in a language so yours and not of the world and be about something so yours that only you shall will it. Your will is yours to will it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Make Every Will Descend from Yours :Philosophy:
Make every will descendent of your mother will their master! Make the boats thus go behind you that go with that much more force now. Make every will of the world be such to strengthen yours. It should be that you believe all of those wills are here just to push yours forward, just so you develop yours the longer, and see for it a greater dimension than intuition alone would dare imagine. Conquer them then overcome them, incorporate every will that has already been worked out by those of the past into your arsenal. Master

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Will to Power Is Your Danger :Philosophy:
Your will to power is your danger, for it is undeniable and unstoppable and real. That which cannot exhaust, that which isn't finite or bound, limited or encapsulated. That which no box can cover, no tingling can't turn around, that for which no price can be attributed or negotiated, that which the finite, the bound and limited, the encapsulated, cannot corrupt.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Way of All Living :Philosophy:
The way of all living and the all way of living. The latter's understanding is a way of reaching the former, in its full understanding.
intuition built a la deep learning, namely with representation, and little tidbits of symbolic aspects that you attach to it allows you to see things before even working them out, you already saw the existence of the idea before you made it out. I noticed this happened with writing and also with algebra as in seeing a possible way of writing a complex expression.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Read Beyond Words :Philosophy:
Look at all that people tell you, not just what comes out of their mouth. I mean this and the less ambitious version that you really see what they tell you, what they communicate explicitly, not just implicitly through actions, but also explicitly through their eye and expression. One hell of a potent cheat code that you could expand your language, for hearing the way of the world and for expressing your way. The world opens to you if you know how to talk to it. Yet through this master the art of while having the language of everything not collapsing stuff into it, having the ability to thus hear what is not even there, that is an even greater step. And make your language evolve in the process that you read something that your language doesn't have, which implies that you got it outside of that language.
if language can dictate thinking then you want the filter to be as little disruptive as possible, you want to be able to accommodate in your language all that there is yet be able to see it outside of your language. Thus you converge at reality from the direction of Seeing itself pure with no words to put on what you see, and put words ever closer to the pure that you destroyed at the start.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Quantum Collapse of Language :Physics:
There is a suspiciously similar dynamic in this characterization of the world and the measurement with collapse in quantum mechanics. You cannot put words on something without destroying what it was. Not even see it, nor any interaction with it. You can never contain the world in which you live, the world. This is the escape for reality. That it can only be perceived without register, without trace,

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as Sufficient for Freedom :Philosophy:
For some the world is big enough and possibly language big enough that all it takes for them is that they keep experiencing new things, which can be expressed with the farther reaches of that language which expands the usual. novelty can be obtained alone from language. And is that not freedom that it lets us express the greatest and greater things. It lets us experience that. And a shared world that we were condemned to is better shared if we can also share, through language, all that we can express about it. If we make that language public people will meet you at that level. For some joy choice and progress is obtained through language alone. Just so you understand that if language was all there ever was we'd be good to go and win the world

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as the Huxley Tape :Philosophy:
Just so you understand with language alone you could win the world and express and experience everything. The huxley machine, the huxley tape could be made out of language. This duality of the convergence of the limit is at the base of simulacrum and simulation, red pill and blue pill.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Living Are Stuck to Their Gift :Philosophy:
Yet the living are in a sense stuck. They got the gift of existence, they are bound to it and its creator.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Slave Only to Your Own Will :Philosophy:
So it seems the solution to this every man is a slave to something issue is precisely being a slave only and only of your own will. One and only with your will. So losing all your freedom to the escape to your freedom. That the way of the living is a way of obedience. This aligns with there being no free will. It means that there is always a will, whether yours or another's. You can will your wills but not will what you will, said Schopenhauer. There is no free man in the strong sense perhaps, let there be at least in the weak sense that you choose your will, and let it be yours.
so Gil's hierarchy of freedom- you can choose your will among the available, then you can choose your will in that you make it, so you go from deciding a posteriori to a priori, and then you are free from wills, something that Nietzsche hasn't mentioned and is my task to develop. For that last transition you gotta first be owner of all wills, slave to none. Only then can you live without them that they have made you rise over the free human. The over free human. Free from wills, here we dive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Free from Wills: Loving Your Creations to Freedom :Philosophy:
Free from wills means free from the love you have towards what you create. Your creations become independent even though you created. Dirac delta's start flying everywhere, that you so much love something to make it come alive, thus you bless it with the game of life, yet so fast you overcome it and not let it stall you, thus you both escape and play the game of life.

* Information Quality and the Expert's Presence :Technology:
We also get a simulacrum condition of the convergence of the limit when talking about the quality of information you get at the personal aspect. What would be higher quality? A lecture by Cohen-Tannoudji with him alone and the blackboard or him with slides? Of course empowerment through slides is more efficient, but all of a sudden you can have someone who doesn't know anything taking advantage of the slides, whereas Cohen-Tannoudji would still give a quality lecture without them. So for the maximum output you want both the information in an accessible way, so accessible that you may think you'd rather just take it at home while you eat, where you can go at your speed, but you also want the person to be in total domination of the material, and have great questions and answers, but if the material is good enough it simply is better than the person talking, for it took perhaps days of work from that person to put down. So you get unreality yet reality that you believe Cohen-Tannoudji is credible.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Enslaved by Everything to Be Enslaved by Nothing :Philosophy:
So the way out of slavery that we are all slaves to something is in the way also of being enslaved by everything so that you can no longer be enslaved by anything, so that the things that can enslave you cannot any longer. Join this to the freedom from it that you choose what enslaves you, and you climb the ladder once more.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willpower on the Will to Power :Philosophy:
your power to will is determined by your willpower on the will to power.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Know When to Leave :Life:
Like in everything, you gotta know when to leave. In the casino, when you are figuring something out. Rather leave it just not totally complete than fill it and take away from yourself the opportunity to complete it. Be of the use of time, many people want to finish something right after starting it, not realizing the power to retrieve from letting time pass and coming back to it different, that is what makes it more complete in reality, and big things can be build by consistent coming back to it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power, Responsibility, and Good Will :Philosophy:
Have no power and much responsibility, and your responsibility will be overpowered and rendered useless, have much power and no responsibility and you will be reckless, have both and your good will pass on

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Human Progress as More Possibilities :Technology:
Human progress was always in the way of more possibilities, well invention is just that so not much surprise there. But we forget many inventions. Those that are so obvious we forget, to the point that we may not even remember they are inventions. Language. The concept of a lie, which had to be invented and we gained power and possibility through it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Many Won't Want to Command :Philosophy:
And many a people won't want to command, won't want to let go of that which enslaves them. A sort of stockholm syndrome on a smaller scale. Many people are just fine with obeying, even if others, let alone commanding, even the more others.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Risks Harder When Comfortable :Life:
Taking risks become harder the more you like where you are. Yet so crucial to go over and beyond to be always taking risks

* [NEEDS TITLE — Sunday, 18 May 2025 13:31:39] :Life:
Commanding takes really that you expose yourself, that you allow yourself to be object of obeying, that you go one to many hand to hand. That you must gather the multiplicity within to take on multiple people at the same time, this is one way to grow tall, to be equivalent to many, physically mentally linguistically. Commanding yourself the more multiplicity it yields, for for all yous you have to battle with. Both go to war with side by side, but also be able to take on every last one of them at once, thus you form your strongest self, which in risk and experiment, by winning can only be the one commandant of all the squad and all the forces that you are. There you must reach.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Play and Internal Evolution :Technology:
Thus one gets to this self play self paced conception of progress within. Evolutionary selection at your pace that can far exceed others's ability to even comprehend.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Core, Hidden from Others :Life:
And how could others interfere with this internal judge and internal battles and internal evolution that they can't see, they who see only the winning commander, and mostly don't even see him? This is your core, that you stick to, and that lives beyond the selves with others that you have.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Law of the Strongest and Life as Master :Philosophy:
Nietzsche puts forward this explanation that the law of the strongest being the master of the weaker comes from everyone and not only the strongest let alone the stronger. That even the strongest yields to life itself its master. That all the both weaker and stronger to something serve the stronger and endorse this yielding so that they can be masters of the weaker. Thus one plays this game of life. Thus life is obeying, that Nietzsche puts great emphasis in having figured out. Even the greatest, and precisely because that coined term makes only sense for those who play this game, is still slave to something, hasn't escaped his slavery to life itself. On the one hand it makes some sense for the master of the game to keep playing the game by being itself a slave, but symmetrically to all others, thus the argument extends.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Induced Order for Progression :Philosophy:
This is the induced order from only which a progression can be made out of. One thus axiomatically builds the requisites of life.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Playing with Death: Beyond Life :Philosophy:
And so the greatest which has risen through and throughout, he plays with death, for everything is left is to escape life. People have this conception of death as dying and waking up in another place. That is the kind of that that he who has mastered all but life can now master. Free from wills. People don't know if he who was born outta life can go beyond it, can be independent of it, nobody knows whether really. They don't know if a god can be made outta man. They don't know if this life of ours can be escaped. If one can stop playing the game of life without that killing him not just in life but everywhere. Can one play another game. Can one detach from mother nature that brought him.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Master's Game and Hierarchical Emergence :Technology:
That yet the master may give much freedom to its slave, the masterness is more so that the slave plays the game of the master, not that inside it it can't do whatever it wants, and hold enslaved others as master too. And so to life it is even more ungrateful and even more shocking that one would transcend it that allows you to master everything, as long as you keep playing its game. It allows you to be master of all wills and even free from wills. Thus the universe would stand in shock that one would even do it. So sometimes you play other's games because you don't know better. thus bosses make their game out of their employees who don't know they can be bosses too.
yet the autonomy between scales, in this hierarchical structure of emergent games, (software in the natural world- a computational approach to hierarchical emergence informational closure, that you can compress different scales as their own autonomous systems, that within them you can compress, which is to say, correctly model from the data of that scale alone and causally connect the objects of that scale.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming Life Itself :Philosophy:
That even the greatest which is master to the greatest Nietzsche had mentioned, which is life, itself can thus be overcome in the singularity. Overcoming itself through getting out of itself through what it gave, the overlife

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Life Not Yet Overcome :Philosophy:
Life has spread itself, it has made itself after and from itself, but still there hasn't lied its overbearing, still remains to be done and undone life, that the overlife may come about.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Purpose Through the Master-Slave Order :Philosophy:
Maybe a purpose can only be introduced through the ordering of master slave. That thus master and slave need each other. They feed off each other. That through ordering one has place to go, namely over and beyond, and that can be the purpose of not our life but life itself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Life Induces the Overcoming :Philosophy:
Perhaps just maybe its the other way around. Life that has the overcoming by nature and induces the ordering on all that plays its game.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- One Is Struggle and Conflict of Purposes :Philosophy:
One is struggle and thus others struggle to understand you. One is a struggle but a becoming says Nietzsche. Yet one is also a conflict of purposes he adds. That your soul stretches to many sides makes you more indecipherable. They will never know which principle you will invoke. You are always anew. If you had a clear and public or mappable traceable purpose it could be used against you. Thus in the unspoken ununderstood way to over and beyond you are unpredictable. And purely random, in the limit, so absolutely impregnable, intractable, unattackable, do you become when you become free from wills. You are yourself more than anyone is themselves for your whole is all you are, you are unmistakable exactly for your untraceability. Either you are or you are not.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Love Something Enough to Free It :Philosophy:
For if you love something sufficiently enough you will bless it with freedom, you will perhaps assist with not knowing where it came from, that it may not shed shadow on where it can grow. Your most precious creations you don't want to put your fingerprint on not to damage them. Thus god raised the universe and disappeared at once.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Beloved Creation Inside Your Mind :Life:
Even perhaps your most beloved creation which is inside your mind, him to whom you talk and he understands you.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Will of Existence :Philosophy:
That there can't be will of existence, cause those that could will it that they don't exist can't will it. Only wills higher than existence can thus be willed, will to truth and will to power among them. Only those wills to something that one can or cannot have can be willed. Will can only will to win thus.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will to Power, Not Just to Live :Philosophy:
Only life can will, and will to power it is that it wills says Nietzsche. I say will to power even if it manifests as will to keep living, for that is power. And many powers does life value more than keep living. Will to the superpowers!

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Permanent Good and Evil :Philosophy:
There is no permanent good and evil, there is no persistence in this world, what is of the moment must fight to keep be and must overcome itself to keep being meaningful. You will never strike something that will work for you forever. There is no happy eras. There is no non zero measure thing that stays with you without you overcoming it and it overcoming itself. There is nothing that without intention will live on. Will to power necessary to keep living. Will for overcoming necessary for change.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The World Is Transactional :Business:
There is no good and evil at all beyond that which at a given moment keeps one moving. The world is transactional, and nothing to you is given. Nothing at any moment is telling you something. You make it all up for your own benefit, just don't get permanently attached to things that you gave yourself. At every moment your intention decides what gets to keep living and what doesn't.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overflow of the Soul for Love of Life :Life:
Overflow of your soul for the love of life

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Shatter Good and Evil, Rebuild Anew :Philosophy:
You first shatter any notion of good and evil that has been imposed on you, to then build your values anew and stronger.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Only After Shattering: Highest Good and Worst Evil :Philosophy:
Only then after shattering all good and evil do you have the possibility for the highest good and also the worst evil. No great evil was ever put forth that didn't come from within. Yet this journey must be trespassed into he who seeks the greatest good. He must confront and say no to the evil he meets along the way.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Be Afraid of Nothing, Even Harsh Truths :Life:
Be afraid of nothing, even harsh truths, we had already gotten there previously. Now silenced truths become toxic, says Nietzsche. If others have seen more of these than you, if you shied away from one of these that may come to haunt you, it may present as weakness on you, that you are not capable of realizing it. That certain smell gets you away. Yet no smell ought to get you away, you have battled all evil, none of it can intimidate you.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Elephants in the Room Grow :Life:
The elephants in the room grow ever more the longer they are unaddressed. Unchecked. Unbalanced. If you don't check the prisoner evil is inside its cell, if you don't gather yourself to look at it, it may have already escaped. It may have already latched onto to other things unknowingly. With these evils you want to tame them, contain them, and isolate them, that they may be yours and never something of you be theirs.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Mysteries: What Makes You Top-Performing :Business:
This is what makes you abnormal and top performant. Is that there are no mysteries. While for others there are ever more mysteries that they don't see the process and only the magic, for you it is all clear, you've seen it all.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Vision Dictates How Much You Shatter :Philosophy:
Your vision of how much there is still to build dictates how much you are willing to shatter.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building the Greatest Good Within :Philosophy:
And our greatest good that which we will build within ourselves to overcome ourselves.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hard Core with No Form :Life:
The image you want to have is that of a hard core that can either be absolutely still or teeming with the greatest form of life. When it comes down to it, be built out of building blocks that are like water, they can flow or they can crash. That hard as anything core that is unbreakable precisely cause it has no form.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Everyone Sees Themselves at the Center :Philosophy:
Everyone sees the world through them being at the center. Everyone sees things in such a way that they who see it are the balanced ones. Thus when an author for example tries to add something to knowledge with his own thing, trying to fit it in the wheel and make it correct for him, even tho he tried to have the complete theory with him adding his own bit and yet having the balance to connect everything, people reading will want to associate him much more with just the bit he added, making him a champion of it, and they themselves as the balanced ones who now incorporate that into the whole.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- He Who Has a Why, He Who Has a How :Life:
That he who has a why to live can bare anyhow. But also that he who has a how to live can bare any why, said Jonathan Bi probably from someone else too.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- In Secret the Overhuman Is Born :Philosophy:
Thus in secret the overhuman is born. But it shall not be explained for now. Singularity.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Capable of All Disorder, Master of Stillness :Life:
Be capable of all disorder, yet as much capable of shutting it all down and be still. May no wild beast be able to wreck your focus. That you be able to handle all chaos and everything everywhere all at once. Maybe you overcome those beasts, not avoid them. May you not be withdrawn from the full experience there is.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Apply Tension, Then Let Go :Life:
You gotta be able to apply tension to control it all, yet let yourself go with the most random of flows. Tense yourself too early, and tensing means you come back to your core, that all other modes of movement don't roll with you, and you may be losing on a lot. Be able to be super flexible and extend the soul in all directions, yet at all times knowing you could control it all. There is more power to knowing you can do stuff than people realize. The counterfactual world truly has consequence in the so called real world. If you know how something would go if you did it, you may not be bound to it, you may be free to think other things, you don't have to go ahead and confirm. If you already saw the end of the line through one path, you may not need to follow it if you see it has not great value. You may have greater pace of executing and operating inside your head, that your mental model of others for example can run faster than themselves, even tho there is some component that you don't learn because you didn't get the randomness of the real world.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Withdrawn from Valueless, Not from Unknowing :Life:
You want to be Withdrawn from parts of the world without value, not without knowing.

* [NEEDS TITLE — Tuesday, 3 June 2025 22:05:29] :Life:
Que gostos nao se discutem, mas que a vida é sobre disutir gostos, that all life is disputing over taste and tasting says Nietzsche. life as the battle of wills presents no more than the subjective to be discussed. Because it isn't discussed says the proverb, can you discuss it without hearing anyone. Because no one can take away from you your wills, for they are totally subjective. You can actually have them and change them and see where they lead you, whereas in non willed things you progress onto something, which is good but isn't generator of time evolution, rather it sets the time we are in.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Hungry Lion Is Stronger :Life:
That a hungry lion is stronger. This idea that when you are stripped of what you want you want it that much more and you are more ferocious and able to tap into a primal nerve that propels you. Make it yours this drive. All of the food for thought and food and food of good that enters you you must burn it to keep yourself from the mindstate of the content.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Winning the Wills of Others :Philosophy:
And that winning the will of others is thus what can be won, for non willed things are totally public fair and square. they can't be allocated, deployed, gamed, hacked, yours.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All Choice Is About Taste :Philosophy:
And all choice is about taste and tasting after all.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Taste as the Weight and Measure :Philosophy:
Nietzsche says taste is the weight and what weighs. Is it the measure and the measured. The compared and the source of comparison. It is that by which you judge things, that they can be judged means they are tastes of some form, and at the same time the value of your own, that is only your own if it is your judgment, your taste.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming Free of Shadow :Philosophy:
Become free of shadow. No shadow for you have lived with it and now it is nothing, it is part of you, it is not any longer a shadow of variable size that can seem like a monster when its a tiny rabbit. That you turn away from yourself and conquer the dark lands of your shadow, not just the unconscious, but the conscious shadow, that which you consciously put away, that which can't stand and you thus send to the far and cold, brief and loud domain of the shadows. You conquer those lands and nothing from it can now sparkle unrest. Fear it you may, but fundamental confidence you have to walk those your terrains.
A step to free from wills is to be free from tastes. To grab them all and in the same way let them go. To become spectator to them, in control. Rationalize them, as in they are no more than a fact you confirm or disconfirm, not a thing that sparkles your need of them,
oo
the power of normalization. Some people give halos to normalization, normalizing your victories, moving on fast. Yet it is much too, that is also much, about leaving no stone unturned, and no what ifs unrealized. Knowing the possibilities, not casting some shadow or some divine light at some things.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Tastes vs Wills :Philosophy:
Tastes as one knows can be much more superficial yet unconscious than wills, those intentional beasts, that yet can run so deep into your being.
Taste decides for you many things, or rather your taste of something gets many of its features before you even taste it. Free from taste means you've seen it all that your mind comprehends, having no part of your mind left uncomprehended, so that no projection from above or rising of a shadow from the depths could corrupt the experience. But actually this is the part that you comprehend you comprehend. I feel one shouldn't shy away from, which again brings the self referential paradox, having one part of the mind totally unknown, that is part of wanting to know more, is that you have something that doesn't quite make the whole thing perfect. There must be always something getting on the way, a little mosquito that ruins the moment yet gives you space to taste it right there and there about, that doesn't quite allow you to say you've got it all. That keeps you moving.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Still Bits of Despising :Life:
There is still bits of despising, that means his body hasn't yet developed the enzymes to treat anyhow, weakness. One becomes neutral, then one develops tastes anew, whose fire shows only truth brought about by neutrality.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Power of Normalization :Life:
Normalising allows above all to get stuff done, to not think of the upcoming moment as holy, thus one doesn't have to pray to it, or hear music to reach an elevated mental state which can just fall below baseline after he gets away from that moment. It allows you to move past and get more. If you know deep down you want something, this lack of celebration in the middle, this lack of rest in the middle, won't harm your will.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Removing the Inherited Filters :Philosophy:
There are still some filters to be taken out from the inside of your mind. Some conceptions, they call it preconceptions, because it wasn't this your being now present who decided upon them, it was thrown into you, whether by external things or even by previous versions of you. Free yourself from the stuff that previous versions of you used for their own sake. Do not confuse yourself with this,
open your mind. Open your mind means opening the doors that you haven't opened, pressing the buttons that you haven't, and understanding your mind, picking apart its parts.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Don't Rest Until You Know :Life:
Don't rest until you know it and be at the end all be all.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Preacher of the Earthly and the Normal :Philosophy:
And how advantageous is it to be a preacher of that which is all consistent, the earthly, the normal. No need for anything abnormal. You can rise over and beyond this world through all normality.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Fairness of the Lived Moment :Philosophy:
If there is further fairness and equality in this world is in that the moment is lived. There is really no memory in that you may have crossed all 7 layers of hell right before being born into a moment, you are all the same. All the like the same. This can be taken advantage of, that you truly can go through these layers, head down, consciousness of if you so please and only awaken in the other side. You cannot trade now for later. Either you grab something now or never. Now or never, ever. If in doubt flat out. No saving now will increase your later output. That is always the same lie.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Withdrawn: Believers in Time's Extension :Philosophy:
Withdrawn are many a people, deprived by themselves, that they believe in extensions of time. That they believe something like the past exists, that they believe they have much power over themselves in the future. Withdrawn from their fullest expression. There is only to add, nothing can ever diminish you now or ever.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Soul of the Bull :Life:
The soul of the bull pushing in all directions and ragely so, ragely at that which he cannot control, which still gives him power and control over what he controls. Yes he focus so much on what he can control that he leverages what he cannot for it
use the rage against the minimum thing that has been given to you that yet you believe won't change, like the finiteness of life or whatever. Yet precisely through what those fortunes that have been handed to you you can be grateful of the cards you are handed, you can recognize opportunity.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom in Present-Moment Adaptability :Life:
There is much freedom in adaptability, in present moment to moment adaptability. Being able to carry all the past and the future within, yet being able to let go of all of it, be here now and change direction, and then succeed in the new avenue you find yourself in. That is survival, power and freedom.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Making Time Stand Still :Life:
With the cards you are handed don't neglect the power of making time stand still. That you have nothing you can do for example. That maybe your train is 5 hours late and you are in the middle of nowhere, with only your thoughts and maybe something to immortalize them, a pen and paper for example. No rush, no duty, you have it right now, free time.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Illusions About Time :Philosophy:
Have no illusions about your time here on earth and otherwise. If something got delayed you didn't win time, you may have even lost it if you adapt yourself to that new time. The time on earth is finite and if you lose days you are doing just that, losing, the count on the days here won't increase, it never does.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Winning From Others' Frustrations :Life:
And of what you can't control and off of delays and things that mess up life you can actually win by pulling on other's frustrations to move ahead, you see their reaction and you smell blood in the water, you go win.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All Feelings Make You Stronger :Life:
All feelings should make you stronger,

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Stockholm Syndrome with Lies :Life:
It feels that even after lies are dismantled people still waste time believing them, some sort of stockholm syndrome mixed with bias for what you previously believed, attachment.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Delusionality Has Power :Life:
Delusionality, there is power in it. Building a mystique around yourself, it goes along with it. For example this belief I now share with you, helper, even if obviously untrue for some. That I see the people who watch my instagram stories and believe they all see it, even people I thought wouldn't, and those to whom I don't correspond by also seeing theirs, I believe they see it as support, they know I'm the one and they believe in me, they look to see if I have already become all that they saw in me to be.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Not Self-Made, Made by God :Philosophy:
Not self made. Made by god. I read. You don't yet see the whole picture, but place no limits on whats possible with what you don't know.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Memoryless World, Memoryless You :Philosophy:
Memoryless this world is and memoryless should you be, not letting yourself be shadowed by your deed, nor your eye by the vision you developed.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being Active with Your Investments of Time :Life:
Being active with your investments, always controlling that which can either be controlled by you or you have no control over. That means when given 5 minutes starting to read, willing and prepared to read for 5 hours if the train doesn't arrive.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Knowing Others' Wills to Win :Business:
Fierce the world is. and the more you know about others's wills and intentions, the more you can see through their game, the more you can predict them and control them, the more you can push and go through them to go further and win. If you see someone in a group who is only there for the girls and not for you, you can quickly recognize it and act on it. Cause to take away someone's power you have to know what they want to do with it, what they seek in that power, what power they seek. You can submit them, and furthermore you can do it without them knowing of that, you don't have to scream you won, mostly that only diminishes you

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Power of Memorylessness :Life:
And the belief of memorylessness simply has power. You spend less time in shaky terrains, that past and future. You get to find more opportunities in the present, you get to make more, you only use past as long as it cavoes you now, through past win for example, you are unaffected by your loses and get to go with maximum confidence now when it matters.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Things Move On Without You: Freedom :Life:
That things move on without you should only give you strength, that you can come in and out at anytime, you owe no responsibility to it. You can be a total unknown to everyone and infiltrate yourself in many worlds, knowing and following only your passion.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All This World Is a Dispute of Trying :Philosophy:
All this world is a dispute of trying and all your wins are about trying

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Don't Live Under a Rock :Life:
Don't let yourself live under a rock. You don't feel it but can see in other people that it is sad, that one lives masked from truth, that one self restricts and withdraws even if not knowingly. Now there are many rocks one can live under. In many of them you should live under, we are here talking about that which matters. Many distractions would better be used dissolving in air than getting inside your rock

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Pushing Through Others' Reactions :Life:
And there are my explicitations of this thing of pushing through others reaction to thing one doesn't control. Like seeing them complain. That can only give you more power to move and go get it. It can only fuel your fire. Its almost like you team up with what brings those bad events.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Beauty From Tamed Beasts :Philosophy:
Beauty one can argue comes when the greatest beasts are tamed. Still is made out of the fervent waters.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Don't Stop When Full :Life:
That one shouldn't stop when one is full, that actually one should never allow oneself to get full, only when beauty is acquired says Nietzsche, sometimes your goal is way past feeling full. Sometimes force feeding becomes necessary, push through complacency. Memorylessness helps with this aspect of exploding at every moment.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drifting Beyond the Human :Philosophy:
And beyond the human all too human you move. Bit by bit we drift away from being human, further into doing things humans didn't thought possible

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero Fails at Beauty :Philosophy:
But the hero fails at beauty that he needs to use his will and muscle, being able to keep it all down. As normal. As human. Gracious and constrained power to win her heart, beauty the hardest of givers.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Descent of Gracious Power Is Beauty :Philosophy:
the descent of gracious power into the visible is the beauty says Nietzsche. Only you can control that power. That makes it yours. But being so on it that it is gracious and even letting it be touched by others that can't have it, that is the beauty. Yes that is the value everyone can get, thats whats called beauty, neverminding that the commander of that power experiences the greatest value, even if only he can seize it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Environment and Friends Change Your Mind :Life:
Your environment very much influences you, we know that. Now one very specific case is if you are with friends. Literally your thoughts change. If you are reading a book instead of just thinking on it or other things half of your brain will have thoughts relevant to conversation with the friends. You can focus as much. One small brainrot by one and now all descend spiralling into fun.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power Is What Gives Graciousness Its Rarity :Philosophy:
But with no power behind it to back it up, with no capability of violence and all evil, graciousness is default, there is no beast to tame, there is no scarcity, everyone can get it, no one can reach over and beyond.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Why People Find Beautiful Things Harmless :Philosophy:
That is why people find things beautiful harmless, they see by definition only the toned down tamed gracious fire of the beast, they see nothing what it is capable of. They don't understand its power nor its strength, nor of it nor of its master.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Battle of Powers Within :Philosophy:
there is always a battle of powers within, Nietzsche says that the ultimate self overpowering should be goodness. That it rises above all causes all things considered.
now goodness without power for evil is not as real, is not tested in battle, can easily crumble under the temptation to evil when it arises.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Bigger the Ascent, the Bigger the Descent :Philosophy:
The bigger the ascent, the bigger the descent, the bigger risk of being thrown way at the gradients and into the easy roll to evil, evil facilitates its entrance, it always has the door open, and the door stares ever harder at you each step you take upwards over and beyond the human. At some point you go pass the clouds, humans are lost down there, you are born anew without them, without knowing them, being only something else entirely.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Never Limit Yourself :Life:
Never limit yourself. There is always an option. People don't think on first principles. There are many soft limits before the hard limit. Exploit them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Godlike Desires :Philosophy:
Being godlike starts with having godlike desires.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Living in the Future :Technology:
That in the movement of the going over and beyond and descending into humans, the going under one travels far in time. You start to live in the future for you predict, for you control what was, you have a grip on past and present, a la orwell you reach the future before it reaches you, for you anticipate and live around there and get yourself familiar with it. It's advantage. When all others arrive you already own the place and know it much better than everyone else. You are the future. And if you see it you can measure it and can build it. You can steer.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Laughing at the Present From the Future :Philosophy:
That laughing uncontrollably may be the reaction of a being so far ahead to us. A human of the future could only laugh at us. Where would he start? A being ahead of us that yet hasn't come from us won't even see us, won't even get our presence, will be in so many dimensions and in so greater of a dimension as to render us useless. But one who gets our presence, namely that human of the future, could only laugh at that presence. Our inefficiencies, our blunders, the things we don't see and realize, the wrong beliefs... Perhaps to laugh further at the project of mankind beyond its present manifestation. To laugh at the idea that it was people from this present world that built the future world, yet that they went so over and beyond the human all too human to make that future come to life and that present come to life.
the same as internally in your life. You will laugh at yourself of the present so bad, and those who haven't climbed and only see the product of the descent, the final beauty, those to whom you disappeared as you ventured beyond the clouds, to them it will be such a discontinuity that they can't believe it is still you, cause it isn't, and that is actually the greatest argument of truth for the above thought experiment.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Own Face as the Best Mask :Life:
That there is no better masks for those of the present than their own faces. You are already as unrecognizable as it gets. This says Nietzsche. And as unrecognizable for unlike masks it doesn't hide, it shows, and to others it clearly shows its not you, masks could actually introduce some doubt.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Overhuman of the Distant Future :Philosophy:
