* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Kierkegaard: Teleological Suspension and the Individual :Philosophy:
Kierkegaard
that a moment is all you got and a moment is all you ought to win, Kierkegaard draws the picture that individuality, your telos, can only be of the domain of the finite. Of the domain of zero measure. One eternal moment, and no more, which comes back to the paradoxical nature of the act of abraham, of the infinite movement that one cannot understand through one's corrupt system, like that of usain bolt. Perhaps hinting that morality is what one should surrender to, omnipresent to all moments and movements, yet beyond one's telos, beyond one's range. Dual and apparently in contradiction to the principle of the self that I get from Nietzsche. Of the will all powerful that one can bring about. Yet one would be limiting himself if himself was all that he had to count on. To be whole one, even more ambitious than previously, has to play with the mutual enhancement of the external and the internal, the overmoment the real, the eternal the ideal that yet gives strength to the killing of the moment, that we sacrifice beyond now our god, really ourselves, like abraham in his movement did. God did. We can get disattached to reality, as much as it can be useful and increasing of the will to power. The duality of the self contained teleologically suspended morality with the ever changing will driven telos of the self.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Not Lost in the General, Not Limited in the Individual :Philosophy:

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Not Getting Lost in the General :Philosophy:

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Faith Brings the Individual Higher :Philosophy:
I have doubts as to whether faith brings the individual lower or higher than god. Faith well pulled can make the individual transcend, rather than be a slave of an higher cause. In fact this may be the very challenge, what makes it hard to reconcile these two reactive beasts, these two antiparticles of a thing that must thus live in one's mind. * Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Hearing People Too Literally :Life:
And many times we and I make the mistake of hearing too literally what the people say. Maybe giving too much importance to that which comes out of the mouth in detriment of implicit action that they may or may not be aware, that they may or may not intend. In detriment of the feeling that powers those words, that they may or may not be aware, that they may or may not intend. That the real effects at times are not to be found in the meanings of the words, that language in this reduced sense is arbitrary and very particular, we forget that, that we may neglect the faith that may be powering the beliefs powering the words. * Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Open-Mindedness and the Infinite Faith :Philosophy:
That encourages open mindedness and respect and tolerance for others' beliefs and thoughts and actions and words. Missing that is reducing the whole world to something not even we understand. So take advantage of all information that comes with a word. At the end of the day the maximum performant effective as in useful processing power in processing one second of the universe at every second, for you process it all, including yourself, in this convergent ever less adding series of informations that connect the whole. This argument actually has some problems. the point is that to express maximally not only do you want to be fluent in every language but you want to hear of them at once, never being stuck to one for one occasion, so you can wholistic understanding and win time. Not just gain time, but win time. With that processing power.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Greater Lesson: Faith as a Zero :Philosophy:
The greater lesson we can draw from here with faith is that you don't want to neglect the paradoxical and that which doesn't fit in your system. Even for what doesn't fit paradoxically there should be space. Integrate into your ship, your arsenal, this touch of paradox, of unexplainable, touch of the reason of no reason. Faith in this sense is really a zero, whose invention made us more free. We don't need reasons so as to act on something, so as to win the world. Even without justifications we are able to do so in one movement. The history of finding zeroes is really one of writting off assumptions that limit our power. In essence really taking a meta faith on our power to have power, which leads us to get rid of the self created monsters. Assume you can do more with less. And that you already have a tremendous amount of things you want to do in this world, then you can afford to dare skip what you don't want, getting out of it only what you want with the minimum possible taken from your flight over and beyond the mere human.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Acts Without Apparent Reason :Philosophy:

know that you may not know anything about the world yet going for it all the same. This is the courage only human is capable of, and only the human that can know so much to know he doesn't know and yet believe all the same.

Thus an all encompassing will will have many traits, however closed or disclosed they shall be, they may never be used, but they shall be there for when one needs it. One can thus always rise to the occasion, as a matter of principle, that one may not shy away from the craziest of mental states and beliefs and faiths that others may find incoherent, contradictory, irrational.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Living All Possible Worlds :Philosophy:

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Needs No Reason :Philosophy:
The knight of faith can act out of no reason apparent. He doesn't need one. While others need to see ahead, he doesn't. While others need certification of success, while others need approval and guarantee of heroic status for a sacrifice, he doesn't, he is intrinsic and pure in his action, he doesn't need to place expectations, he can act out of freedom And do the unimaginable in the sense that one sees no reason for it to do the unimaginable in the sense that no one else knows how he could do it. First no one knows how he can do something, for there is no reason for it, then they don't know how he did something, that whose greatness they think they cannot taste. For our comprehension of anything is too little beyond the absurd of the world, and with so little knowledge of what happens only doing what is rational, only experimenting what makes sense limits you to what has already been approved and what is known, which again is very little. Further one needs absolutely nothing to do the greatest of things, he can do it out of spite and as naturally as one blinks an eye. He needs no narratives, no frameworks, no motivations, he can become the master of the smallest and biggest craft. He is not limited by his past. He lives the present, the all too absurd. That is absurd.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Rises Over Morality :Philosophy:

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- At What Level Can You Talk to People :Life:
At what level can you talk to people. Like talk to them, not just say things to them. Do you believe so little in them you have to say stuff just to be polite. Or you find them valuable and try to extract something from them. Or you are genuinely curious about them and thus are able to just hear them. But how deep do you get with them. In the sense that the more you both share with one another, and that can be through the usual common thing in one another or actually sharing, that is, explicitly becoming entangled with them, created as it were, then you are able to skip the higher levels and get to the real stuff. You don't need to seem polite, or interested for that matter. You may both only select the real value, seeing the whole thing as transactional. Or you may have shared so much you know both of you are there no matter what. So in the lower level you can motivate them, like give them pep talks, but if they are about it as much as you then they already know the motivation, they already have it, and you can start right away from a place of no exaggerations and no metaphors, you can speak reality. On what language are you able to speak with someone. On what grounds does your connection lay. Cause it can be grounded in a deep appreciation that both of you are trying to be the best version they can be.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Catching the Train II :Life:
Now we have accumulated an undefeated record one again of catching trains. Last time I wrote we had done the craziest journey there, and yet the last two times we took it levels above. Not sure if I wrote one where I went running from the stadium to the underground with cramps all over the calves, but after that there was one where I'd accepted I didn't have time to catch it simply, to change plans and go to saldanha on foot and then entrecampos catching the train at the second stop, the train was late and we got it. Today I was even later and arrived at the underground which pointed 8 minutes, so I had to bet that the train would arrive some 10 minutes late if I was to have any chance of catching it. Went and as I arrived it was already there and after getting in some 20 seconds later it went away.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Coach-Athlete Relationship at the Highest Level :Life:
Think of a relation between high level coach and athlete. Khabib and islam. Does khabib sit there and motivate islam? Of course not, it runs so much deeper than that. Islam is as motivated as anyone and khabib and him are on the same page. Khabib can talk to islam at such a level that just one or two words can carry more truth to islam than reading an entire book could. They probably have their own language at this point, not that the alphabet is different, but simply only islam and khabib's turing machines accept that language, if you model dagestan as a universal computer and islam and khabib as finite automata.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Growing Through Language :Life:

Their sensibility to truth has decreased, yet their radar for truth is ever more potent. You can emulate all lower levels but you aspire to surround yourself in an environment that pushes the highest level to be even greater still.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Having Children as a Singularity :Life:

Only they can match you from the get go. So you have it right there in front of you, something that goes beyond you yet is you. * Immortality and Competition :Life:
Quotes and question:
people are under this illusion that if they were given immortality, as in freedom to die whenever they'd want through suicide, they would have all the time in the world to do stuff, they could always leave stuff for later. Sure in a strict sense you have time for everything, but there are also infinite things to do, and if someone is 2x faster than you at everything they will do twice of you, given they are immortal too. The point is you live in the finite even if its forever, so there will always be competition and meaning to invest time, opportunity cost. Fundamentally there doesn't exist a ceiling on how good one can be. Non finiteness could even spark a more tense form of competition, since now you have to choose at every moment to keep living, that freedom means if you are to be immortal you will have to make that choice, no great filter or death will make you urgent, it will all have to come from within.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa: Being All In :Philosophy:
Fernando pessoa:
The question of being all in is a major one. At some moments it feels like if you are not going all in for something it is not even worth it. Either it is a thing or it isn't. Theres tremendous power both in this and in the opposite, where you are able to ingrain something without even giving it all your soul and attention. Those innocent gains stack. But mostly, either there is something I can measure and see improvement, or I'm not gonna do it. Now this is for subgoals.
for a whole life that encompasses all these goals, everyone is all in, whether they realize it or not, whether they want to believe it or not, whether they want to play this game or not, whether they want to know everyone is all in. You can say no rushes, no worries, no hurries, in your life, but then you see the bus coming and winning pulls you out of that hole, and there you go running to catch it, you won for you didn't have to wait 10 minutes for the next one. And as you naturally allow yourself to go after this, now you have something to keep pushing you, that those 10 minutes weren't for nothing. So now you put in the effort. In every action you either went for it or you didn't. There are no people who don't have wishes or ambitions. Those that say that most likely wish they didn't had wishes.
for some things you went, some you didn't. But still the choice is binary.
And it is good for you that you believe everyone is all in.

Quotes and questions:
just like in AI, where real world data carries with it more relations that we know and that AI can leverage to get better, looking at nature is superior to looking, observing man made things, for the depth of the latter is finite, as opposed to the former, where no limits are there. A movie, if you are looking only at the human aspect and not the human inside the nature, has less, is limited, watching nature directly is limitless, it wasn't programmed, there is more to discover.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa on Seeing the Visible :Philosophy:
Fernando pessoa:
And the not quite there yet man will see nature for its beauty, not for being itself.
and it is hard to see only the visible, to not see beyond it. Hard to not be contaminated by the world of ideas, that you look and can't attribute any word to that you see. Its hard to pay attention without thinking. Hard to put the mind still. Hard to forget all that you learned and it betrays you when you look at the things that weren't created out of your learnings, but from which human learnings derive. Hard to see for what they are. Hard to strike reality. Hard to unsee in something all that you already saw and you think is the same. To look with new eyes and new brain. To experience a totally different world from that you teached yourself. To at every moment be born anew to the eternal newness of the world, one needs this.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Active Effort and Avoiding Stability :Life:
Just like without active effort you are going to end up surrounded by people similar in condition to you, if you don't intentionally put in effort, you are going to end up very very similar to your parents. So many times you see the chaos of youth end up unravelling into stability of someone around one of their parents. They settle for that.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Blessing of Finding Nothing :Life:
Its actually a blessing that when you search something up, some feeling some way of doing, you don't find anything and you have to do it yourself. It means you are onto something new, and you won't get discouraged cause someone already did it. You gonna have to actually make your own unique product. turn it into reality. You wouldn't want these things that you found in your head to be solved, no, you can't satisfy right away those desires. They remain open loops so that you have to get back to them and these visions of yours onto the world. True, you are mad that no one understands what you mean, and theres nothing about it, there isn't the perfect book or video that is going to show you the solution. You have to carve it yourself, and only you have it in your mind to. A constant notification. One underestimates the power of keeping things in the mind for a long time, everything else that you see in the meantime reflects off of that hanging thing. You always have a thing to go back to and do. You know you'll never run out of things, that you keep this list of things that you or other versions of you would do, but you are an apex predator, you go after different versions of you. In some timelines you win more in life than others, but this yours one you go after every way of winning that all the branches have produced. That is the multitude into one of being that you seek. Thats also what it means to become the best version of yourself. Its given all versions making them all together, getting all other versions together. And there are many things that I say I would do if this was just one more life and wasn't the life. If some god gave me an extra life in which whatever happens here doesn't affect the actual life, I would do tattoos no doubt, I would watch a lot of shows and play some games I wished. If this was another life I would enjoy addicting to gta vi. Or watch breaking bad. Or screw brain damage and go full in on mma.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Meta-Thinking and the Ultimate Timeline :Philosophy:
This is the kind of meta thinking. You can appreciate distilled and compressed portions of those things, but this is the life above every other, one can't afford to get lost on one timeline. And trust me all the greater things come in a bunch. As mankind evolves, the best time to play games is now, the best time to do AI is now, the best time to do sport is now. All the great things come together, so going for one is necessarily a huge opportunity cost that has to be measured with the value brought by it.

* Chosen and Backed by God :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions continuation:
you can consider yourself chosen. Favored by god. Backed by god. You were chosen and embraced, and love at once came and was. Its not the same mood of elevation as that temporary energy burst, its not explosive, just smoothly flying in the clouds. Not some extraordinary rise and fall, but a movement of the baseline itself. Something that doesn't leave you. Something that adds to you and becomes you.

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Paradox of Preparation :Life:
The paradox that life is unpredictable and you should be a master of unpredictability yet have the intentions of preparing definitely. So believing that when you get out of today, for example, you will know for your life everything necessary about relationships. You dive super deep so as to close the loop. The paradox is that this belief is good and will translate into real action and consequence, but you will eventually have to get back to the topic of relationships, whether a posteriori, or to remember things, or to actually understand things you saw previously in a better way, now that you are in a position to understand them. So learning is continuous, so is doing, even if your best intention may be to break it into blocks. So every loop is open, and may seem closed only approximately.
que o dia morre todos os dias

* Apex Predator Competition :Life:
Quotes and questions:
This apex predator concept is for competing against yourself,

* Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Reading, Writing, and the Urgency of Time :Writing:
And this thing that you will be writing instead of reading, well this places a bound on what you will read. Possibly you already know every book you will read in your life. This is true for many people of course that don't read but for you that devour it seems weird, yet as you read more you have more to say, and it becomes harder to stay shut. So geometrically you will read less, you may reach an asymptote. Now of course you may also read some of the works that are coming in the future and whose authors may not even be born yet, but those its hard to know about. Cause I find I read slower the greater works like Zarathustra, so urgency is needed, select very well what you read, just like anything you do really. Thats how you solve to some extent the massive regret and disappointment you find in the end of life, looking back at what you could have done by preparing now. By noticing how fast time flies before it takes flight. Before you embark on it. It does fly ever faster, and if you only know that after the fact you will regret it. People say how time flies, yet seem to do nothing about it

* Levels of Urgency :Life:
Quotes and questions:
there are levels to urgency. You understand that as things happen to you. There are different levels to urgency. The breaking bad urgency. You get glimpses of higher levels.

* Some Movies Change You :Life:
Quotes and questions:
Some movies and sceneries inspire you, rise your immediate will, your rage to change, your power to act now. Some inspire thoughts, bring to surface things. With some you increase your aliveness, with others you learn, you discover, you create. But with a special few, you change. Immediately the chip goes in, immediately you know your duties, and you not only see a new reality but take it as your own, you incorporate it and your flesh is stitched where this reality caused the wound. It opened a gate from this reality to yours. You see it immediately. It goes right into your soul and purifies it with some of its water. The purest is water. The simple yet pure, so each reality brings its pure water. There is no need for thought. This is pure assimilation. This is observing the world as it is. No confusions. There are no open loops that your thought works and grinds to close. It presents to you naturally. You become something else right at that interception point. You only hear your breathing, that which in so much time of meditation fails you. You are truthful for once.

* Memorizing vs Understanding :Life:
Quotes and questions:
if you memorize something you don't understand it. You can simply incorporate what you understand. It now comes from within. Now there is admittedly some sexiness to memorizing, it makes parts of stories feel more concrete, for you know exactly who said what, and the date... It makes it good to know of some famous people others admire to have said something, but it takes away from your truth.

Imagine being ready to win so much more, we don't know quite how much, and being stopped short. Perhaps instead of a basketball player we would call him after his life a master of life.

* What is Life With Mind and Matter and Autobiographical Sketches- Low Standard to Write, High Standard to Mean It :Writing:
You gotta master the duality between having low standard for what you write down, in that you don't see anything as too crazy, in fact it both loses and gains reality when you write something down, so that you are free from judgment, so that your thoughts may roam free. Yet have a very great standard of what deserves writing down. For when you get writing good thoughts here and there you may find yourself forcing the greatness of them just to say nothing. Like in podcasts that there are so many and they think they have something to say. Only say something when you have something to say.

* What is Life With Mind and Matter and Autobiographical Sketches- Natural Selection Acts on Itself :Philosophy:
That natural selection acts on itself, that those who have genes that are too mutable, in that they are too sensitive to mutation, will most likely mutate into non survival paths. So it's natural selection that selects the properties of the selection process, namely the mutation process and others.

* The Challenge of Learning and Creating Languages :Technology:
Quotes and questions: people are not aware of how hard it is to learn a language from scratch, when you hear a language you haven't heard its not that you just don't understand the meaning, but that you can't keep up. It goes so fast, your ability of compression being zero invalidates your understanding. It even seems way less information is conveyed, you only hear a couple of sounds
and if you were to create a language for you and a couple others, you would soon run the risk of not having working memory to talk. Every freakin word requiring an associated sound and way of writing. By hand it comes hard, unless the language is computable, you have some rules for it which make further compression possible. So creating languages and maximizing certain things within them is in the realm of tractability of computers, not us.

* What Do You Care What Other_- Swimming Far: The Confidence of Return :Life:
You find interesting the mindset that arises when swimming in a long lake, or at sea, where theres a lot of space. The further you swim the further you will have to swim back. And if you stop at one spot you are dead. So going forward involves confidence that you still have what it takes to go back. But then you realize this is what always happens. In life even if you are not noticing the more you progress, the more you will have to walk back. Now we don't notice sometimes, for most things in life. Thats how confident we are. Its not even a question. Thats the power.

* LLMs and the Meta View :AI:
Quotes and questions:
with LLMs we are still at the point, and to me this is a consequence of the architecture and the structure of next token prediction, of the AI reading your words from the direct view and not the meta view. For some prompts, like make a totally new correct theory of physics that unifies quantum and gravity, it seems that it understands what is asked but doesn't have the capability, whereas for others like don't answer this question, like actually not answer, it seems to be betrayed by its mode of conversation. So skill and knowledge are still very much required to get a lot out of it. In the future, I think this will still be the case but obviously with greater results, in fact the gap may even increase. You will perhaps be able to push intelligence and superintelligence much further than those without skills and knowledge, with their greater intelligence their space of exploration increases massively and great steering will make the difference.

* What Do You Care What Other_- Having the Biggest Why :Life:

* What Do You Care What Other_- Ideas You Believe In Become You :Philosophy:

* What Do You Care What Other_- By Principle Always Come Back :Life:
By principle to any place you go you can always come back. If you got in you can get out. So in those journeys where its going and coming back, know that you can come back. Half is the perfect number, when you get there you have to go back and there is no other option.
So fear not going too far and flying too high and getting too deep into sand or sea.
always start, pass that initial potential well. For example, if you have something to write but don't have a system for writing, it will take work just to write down the single small thought you have, and you think it's not worth it, but even if you don't know you will probably get many more of those small ideas throughout your life. You can never start and miss them all, or you can start and end up building a whole civilization. Scale is both the cause and the consequence of starting, even if you start small and initially it is not worth it. if you do start then eventually you get greater and greater ideas, ones that take so much time to write that you would never even considered them before you wrote the small idea that first time. And now you have the infrastructure to accommodate them.

* The Complete Musashi The Book of Five Rings and Other Works- Superior Intention Through Full Alignment :Life:
Superior intention through full alignment. That is flow.

* The Complete Musashi The Book of Five Rings and Other Works- The Two Gazes: Looking In and Looking At :Life:
As pertains to gaze, says musashi, there is only looking in and looking at. Your opponent, looking in to see his mind, enter his world and know it, not letting him know yours, looking at, distant, seeing his actions and not letting it hypnotize you. This to every target. Looking in to understand the lesson behind The action that comes from the looking at. This the student of the game knows best. Achieve both sides of looking, know both ways, leave nothing to the unexpected musashi says. Read from one into the other, see how they are intertwined and if his eyes and his foot are aligned, if he goes where he looks and looks where he goes. From his inner world take whats best to fill your own world, yet protect it with walls so you cannot be understood. Poker. Only give out information intentionally, while gathering all kinds of information. Thus you gain the power to steer someone without them knowing. So get out of your world while you have the patience and youth to be far from home for long. Search every world, build the best for your world and be able to act the best.

* Notes from Underground- People Who Need to Feel Independent :Philosophy:
That some people just want to feel they are exercising independent wants, let alone if they are profitable wants or not. They want to thus take decisions even if they contribute nothing or even if there's an illusion of choice at play, they want to feel empowered that they know something that others don't, and they make sure they use it, even if its of no use. they sometimes even ask things they know, everyone knows, to certify, to put their stamp on it.

* Time to Rest When You Die :Life:
Quotes and questions:
that there will be a lot of time to rest when you die. You will have eternity, an infinite amount of rest so don't rest now.

* Notes from Underground- The Overly Conscious and Self-Awareness as Strength :Philosophy:
Overly conscious is the man of the underground. Self awareness is actually, I'd think, one of the biggest strengths of the overhuman, of myself. So conscious as to be able to control consciousness, thus not falling prey to the winning ingenuity of fools, that win big despite knowing less, or the bliss of the ignorant or the focus of the flowing ones who commit and dive into something losing grip on themselves. Thats how you solve this paradox.

* Notes from Underground- Paralysis by Analysis :Life:
So the man of the underground admits he has such high consciousness that he developed inertia, paralysis by analysis, any action is refuted by another thought, and another one once more.

* Notes from Underground- The Curse of the Highly Conscious :Philosophy:
The curse of the highly conscious is in caring, if he is too intelligent and capable and because he cares he will bear responsibility, he can do anything. It takes a certain kind of bravery to let go, the same bravery when you let your kids do something by themselves in their way, even tho you know there was a better way, but you gotta let them do the mistake to learn.

* Notes from Underground- The Fire of Revenge vs Slow Remorse :Philosophy:
That perhaps rage and vengeance is the mother of all action. It sure gives you urgency. It prompts you to the state of mind of I want it all I want it now. You are damn sure you are going to act and do so to great consequences. On the other hand the man of high consciousness refrains from that, not only does he have inertia to act in general but even the more so if guided by ripples in a pond. He folds, Dostoevsky says, way before the vengeful man, which still folds. The vengeful man must quickly use up his fuel, for it runs out quickly, and once you are out of that state you don't get it back. And I tell you something, there is the burning all consuming fire of revenge that is quick coming and quick going, yet quite intense, and then there is the more rational slow building remorse even, that builds underneath and drives from within. The latter one people have categorized as the more dangerous one, that it fuels some of the biggest evils of mankind. It sets a position of you to the world, very well thought out, very strategic, ingenious kind of strategic. With the first kind of immediate vengeance, you have the tendency to sprint, put down the greatest speed of your life, running, rowing in a lake until the finish line, straining and pushing, but once you get to the finish line the burning skies and core of the earth transform into a beautiful sunset, in front of which few will keep sprinting, few will deny themselves the gift after the curse, most will find it natural to give in, to fold before the great wall they find, this is the curse of being natural and following the emotion, that you ride the wave as it passes you and you sit in the valley as the wave passes by you.

* Notes from Underground- Acting Out of Wickedness :Philosophy:
That the man of high consciousness can only act out of wickedness, only with the awareness that he has no basis on which to stand, he has seen everything too closely to understand its weakness. Whereas others base themselves in cloudy platforms of ideas, like justice, he would have to pretend to believe in such things to act by them.

* Notes from Underground- Man Always Wanted to Act His Way :Philosophy:
That man always wanted to act his way, not the profitable way. He may want to profit, but only if its him declaring the way to the profit. If a better machine that took all free will away calculated the optimal path still man would choose his way. So we are blessed that we don't have something tell us the optimal path, that way we can go about going our own ways and having the feeling of discovering profit, even if we profit less overall.

* Notes from Underground- Would Proof of No Free Will Change Anything :Philosophy:
Interesting question;
would a definite proof of no free will for humans change anything? We could follow its consequences and thus change, arguing that before it was in the air and now it is known and we should condition our behavior to profit the most with that given data. But at the same time we already didn't have free will, so we can choose to, if more profitable, keep living as if we weren't sure we had or not.
but the rational thing is to keep going, because just knowing we have free will is not enough, we still feel it, a machine predicting us would change things, but of course that comes with a lot of problems philosophical and physical.

* Notes from Underground- The Paradox of Ultimate Human Ability :Philosophy:
Again the paradox, much similar to gods ability to make a stone he can't lift, that if we believe humans are so good then they can create something that predicts the future fully. For the maximum human ability you give away either free will or the ability to get to such an invention. One can take the easier spiritual route of stating free will as the greatest maxim. That way the god sayers can say that humans do evil and god does nothing because free will is more important. And to me it is. Freedom is up there. Now freedom includes not being driven by the desire to be independent, and so lose unwanted profits on the basis of that.
now the question can be posed, still the answer is real freedom, if it would make a difference that we didn't knew what the machine said, a pseudo randomness in the prediction of ourselves, that data made the machine able to predict us but act over us in such a way we did not know we were being tricked.

* Notes from Underground- The Desire to Use All of Life :Life:
No doubt there is this desire, which aligns with the desire to be natural, of using all life, using all that humans are capable of, satisfy it in your life, not just reasoning, or the culture of the body, but everything human is capable of.

* Notes from Underground- Letting Someone Want Regardless of Profit :Life:
And people underestimate how hard it is to really want something regardless of profit, and to let someone want something regardless of profit. The gladiator fought for freedom inside the arena, but he fought the wars of Germania for freedom, which he would exerce in his way by go home and being with his family. This is not profitable for the others, as they see all that would be wasted, all that ability and competence devoted to living a life of agriculture. They don't let him. Because profit is really something you lose, forever, and admitting that, being able to let that go is a hard but necessary step to freedom.

* Notes from Underground- High Ground and Underground :Life:
Having the high ground and the underground, the paradox is you should have both. You should have the high ground even if people think you are in the underground, and the underground is quite useful, there is a certain advantage to seeing the human world from below, at the level of feet where decisiveness prospers and people move with intention, despite moving nowhere. On the high ground, they move nowhere, but collectively you see the path, the intention. There is a certain, not too profitable, kind of cool thing about being in the underground, afraid to step outside into the real world and make real steps. When you are at home and this the most introverted vein kicks in it feels all the better when you see that in a matter of seconds you can enter the empire state of mind and be at the high ground, where details of the humans matter very little.

quotes and questions:
this argument of taking things to both limits, and concluding that the maximum is somewhere between them, that both limits are Zero and its continuous functions, applies to many things in life, you apply it many times.

* Earn Your Introvertedness :Life:
