you gotta earn your introvertedness if you long for more of it. Dominating being extroverted, be good at it, so that being introverted then is a personal choice and not one out of necessity that its the only way for you to win. Earn your right to rest by working hard if thats what you are after.

* Notes from Underground- The Underground Man's Freedom and Angst :Philosophy:
The freedom of the underground man, his trying to be unpredictable. He tries to show the world he is free, but the world does nothing back. It's a form of escaping the matrix. Not following the script. Its a sure way to show you are free. To be free and still play the game would be better, but the underground man self destructs, he puts himself against the world, and he becomes the greatest victim of Kierkegaard's angst. Being afraid to take any decision, so after freedom he is yet gets stuck in his own head. Freedom consumes him until he lets go of it. He loses control as he says. Its all a movement of oscillation between control and letting go.

* Owning the Permanent :Life:
Quotes and questions;
I am very much a seeker of what is permanent, what goes for eras, what can be forever. I wouldn't want to spend all my life scrolling, so I don't scroll at all. Nor eating. Nor feeling the pleasure of good food, that transient bastard. Nor being slave to these transitory human feelings. Over human. Over the human things. If between having a bit of wine and a bit of rice I would go for rice, for to some extent I can just keep on eating it, forever, much more than in the wine at least, in which I'd had to moderate myself. If I'm going to start to workout, I'm going to do it forever. I won't train something if its not to add permanently to myself. I don't buy, I own.

* Notes from Underground- The Apprentice's Moment of Rage :Life:
There is this moment in the journey of the apprentice, where he stops being the good boy and following orders without over the top knowledge of what they do or what he is doing or going. When he rages and demands answers. It was Shao Dre with the jacket, it was me with the notes from the underground. I kept on pushing, but eventually went to ask chat gpt what this book is about, what makes it good.

* A New Greatest Competition :Life:
Quotes and questions:
the olympics still have the problem that they are of countries. Mma feels weird in the olympics, for all the individuality that makes the ufc great would be diluted with its champions in the circus of nation, with all procedures and protocols it comes with. A new greatest competition would be good, where you represent only yourself, what you are in all you are. A competition greater, with many sports, a decathlon of all sports.

* Notes from Underground- Angst Rests on the Premise That Life Takes Work :Philosophy:
That angst rests on a premise that life takes work, that it takes work to love, to live the day, to keep living, that its a price to pay. And in that case one is confronted with choosing very well what to do. Also one can be quite the opposite yet do the same, that again one values so much life and one is dumb enough to forget to actually live it instead of thinking how to live it. So angst always comes out of dumbness, and numbness to what it means to be alive, to live, to keep living.
some people even have an angst that doesn't show itself in the little microactions they do all the time, but in the overarching lack of milestone orientation, they distract themselves and forget about progressing to the next level.

* Notes from Underground- Angst Solved by Orientation :Philosophy:
This angsts is solved of course with lack of freedom. You just orientate people in some way, and they will follow, perhaps thinking they are independent, and with no angst. Striking both freedom and freedom from angst is what real living is.
there are even the poor ones that work all the way to freedom, give themselves all they have to one day be free, yet as that day arrives they see nothing beyond it, they can't face the meaninglessness of choice. They prefer to be ordered by others, that that way at least they know they are working for something objective, something that is impossible for only them to want, and it ends up being someone else subjectively taking charge.
freedom is only the beginning, if you treat it like an end, an end it will be, and you won't be able to walk upon it.
Proof of freedom thus is essential, you being able to prove to others and to yourself that you are free, whatever you may be doing.

* Notes from Underground- Master Every Branch: Overhuman's Determination :Philosophy:
To master every branch, every timeline, as the overhuman does, you have to be able to go into these timelines, take choices that take you away from the whole and into something very particular of measure zero, that yet you believe will take you to the whole. The way to be overhuman thus simultaneously requires and solves for you not having angst, or even having, act as tho you don't have it, take choices with the same determination. Thus the overhuman is the most determined, and the most free.

* Notes from Underground- The Ridiculous Exaggeration Turned on You :Philosophy:
And this book really is that: showing a ridiculous exaggeration that you come to not understand, it shows you such a bizarre thing that you can't understand and understand how someone can be like that and then turns it on to you, affirming, fyodor, that just to a lesser extent this is what you, of the ground, are and are noticed by the man taking notes from the underground.
yet the man of the underground is highly conscious, conscious of even that. He knows damn well his exaggerations, he announces them but still does the bad he identifies at times, while we, who didn't carry those tendencies all the way as he did, don't notice it, we hide it under other labels.

* Notes from Underground- Rewards Only at the End :Life:
This is a different kind of learning experience. It rewards you only at the end. You have to put up with the same stuff, sure you get a complete picture and go on confirming and expanding it, you draw the whole map, but only at the end do you realize the territory you've drawn, do you look at the picture as one, and as something for itself.

* Notes from Underground- Exposing Our Deceptions :Philosophy:
He didn't deceive himself, and in so doing may have exposed our deceptions of ourselves.
the magnified angst is highlighted by the huge amount of things out there related to how to live life, how to choose what to do, this is the question no one knows the answer to, and there is plenty of distraction out there about it for you to indulge it yourself. The more freedom we get the greater its proportion and portion.

* Notes from Underground- The Magnified Angst of Freedom :Philosophy:
The magnified angst is highlighted by the vast amount of things out there about how to live life, what to do and how to do things, that's the question no one has the answer to, and you can spend a lot of time stuck in this angst. Be weary of these distractions then. As we gain more freedom these distractions gain proportion and portion.

* Notes from Underground- The Guide Is Always Yourself :Philosophy:
People will look for the guides everywhere, but ultimately we come back to that the guide is yourself. Thinking for yourself is the only way then to keep from angst while gaining freedom.

* Notes from Underground- Consciousness Generates Angst :Philosophy:
With consciousness that generates the angst we run the danger of losing track to the way of ourselves. Still the overhuman comes deeply from the human, it follows very much itself the human, he doesn't start by unreal things, it has its base quite solid at the level of the reality of meat and flesh and bone and rock. Still it rises.

* Notes from Underground- Stillborn: Born with Consciousness :Philosophy:
Stillborn, born dead. Born with consciousness. If you come yourself to freedom and can't do anything about it, you are born dead, that you have started living but haven't found a way to live. Some...

* lifestreams ubiquitous :Technology:
It'd be cool if there was a site online with multiple bodycams, like if you had a bodycam you put it on and essentially lots of people in the world would have this on simultaneously so that you could see other's life as they are going. Ease of access, one click away from seeing the experience of this collective that shares the present. Making be real obsolete, a realer be real.

* better universal language :Technology:
Startup of making the best universal language (quotes and question) 
If the language is sufficiently good perhaps a matching market would be made out of it. Some people really use notation to help them in physics for example, but language is taking it a notch over.

* engineering landscapes and laying downside up :Technology:
Obviously kinda exaggerated and non practical but what if (developing in the rant of laying down looking at the stars from quotes and questions) we had a similar setup but for laying down and seeing our world instead of the rest. So we could place platforms at different heights, including closer and even in space, looking at different scales at the plant, at humans, so we would have to put the platform, then make a centripetal force that made it so gravity went in the opposite direction, or some angle in between too, and then matching the frequency of the rotation with the frequency that our eyes catch the image (fps) so that we seemed static, the centripetal force could be then manipulated by the radius of curvature.

* better social media :Business:
Why isn't there a social media user friendly in the life sense? Like profit is made from time spent on the app, but its not so much about time, just seeing ads here and there. Perhaps people paying with ads, not actually paying but just knowingly watching strong ads. Perhaps if you give more value people will come back because of that value, not so much because you will keep them hooked in, make it so good that they can live with seeing a strong ad here and there.

* recommender system quirks :Technology:
How big the pool of people to initially test the content on some platforms social media. Like do you wanna show the video to 300 on the same cluster of your social engineering that your algorithm incorporates or do you want some from each cluster?

* solving eating :Technology:
If eating less frequently, using less the digestive system, less work for the body, couldn't you eat once a day, and then just straight up inject all the other nutrients into the blood? It would have to be a slow process perhaps, can't throw in 100g of sugar all at once.

* human's athletic limit :Life:
The thesis that humans have peaked athletically, meaning that the logarithmic curve has reached a point where fluctuations start to matter more than raw ability. So crazy athletes that surged 15 years ago may not yet be surpassed today. Look at Usain Bolt, how long before his records get broken? I reckon at least 10 years. The only ones who have a chance are basically those that haven't yet appeared on scene. For example that Gout Gout guy, maybe he has a very legit shot, we just haven't seen what he is got. Those that we have seen, I don't think will be able to get there.

* robot sport in the era of the highlight :Technology:
Sure, there are still some low hanging fruits in humans, like making every country developed and make every sport accessible, for example, there is a lot of untapped potential in african countries, with people that would be beast athletes, but their hopes have not seen the light of the day, or maybe they don't even know the sport that they would dominate. Technology gives us some forms of improvement, and that further supports what I'm about to say. Competitiveness will slowly or fastly honestly fade away from human competitions to robots, there is just so much more margin for progression. Not necessarily in our sports, but also, and that will be the start, but mostly sports that we make for robots themselves, games that play to their advantages will be of great interest in the future. We already sort of have that with formula 1, with hundreds of people working on the robot that one drives. A easier low profile option to make people ease in to this idea is those sports where robots will play a major component, whether people realize it or not, like F1. Cyborg type sports, where human and robot matter almost as much. The change is not that abrupt. We already have equestrian in which the horse is the major player, there is great entertainment in these animal driven sports. They in essence provide a different skillset from the humans counterparts, and so offer opportunity for entertainment and challenge. For the purposes of the growing of these sports, we will have to in some sort of narrative comprehend what the AI's will be doing. We have to relate, they have to be slow and predictable and systematic enough for us to pay attention. Robots playing go at such an high level that we don't even comprehend is not enjoyable to watch. Also imagine robots playing ping pong so but so fast that we couldn't even see the ball until the point was won. There has to be space for emotion, space for us to see and think whats next. That can be done, as in F1, with constraints on the power of the machine. I believe that you could build an AI agent that wins the F1 world championship. You would have to discretize the space of variables that are playable when driving the f1, like acceleration and percentage of "application of the accelerator", breaking, turning angle, mode of racing, then feed it all the data on all the laps ever filmed on every track, and it would become the best driver ever no doubt, with such diversity of experience, it's like a human was able to be driving in a simulator for thousands of lives worth of duration.
The era of the highlight demands all the more content. Do you understand the amount and quality of the content that can be created through the sheer scale of robot sports. You will have farmable clips dancing every freaking day. If anything you could make the robots play millions of games and choose the best ones. Maybe thats the nature of future robot competitions. Not the moment, like humans, cause yes with humans it's all about winning the moment, and maybe AI won't be able to reproduce that, but in pure performance over millions of games, consistency too. There will be lots of clips for the infinite tik tok. Then comes the aspect of change. If there are fewer and fewer marketable world records, or great moments, in sports, if there is stagnation in the eras, as it feels that we reach just one more era, not one that changes everything, then compute solves that. There will be change and evolution every year, the scalability will lend it the win to robots. It would be tough for humans tho, as most would just watch the tape and not find the drive in themselves to do sport any longer.

* new clock app feature :Technology:
Instead of runner using watches, elite ones could use a chip or something inside them that hears the gun, starts automatically, and then beeps at the right times, programmed beforehand through an app. Whether you want pacing, or want it to beep when the world record time happens, its fun and useful, its so obvious yet no one has done it. Bugs in the system would be much more dangerous tho.

* business smart contact lenses :Technology:
Intelligent contact lenses, connected to an app with filters, nanotechnologically maneuvered to immerse you in a new experience of reality. Maybe no contacts would be needed, as a generalized brain computer interface would do the job from the inside.

* automated traffic dynamics :Technology:
Also related to automated traffic, no longer would waves form, as in everybody would start at the green light at the same time, in the most efficient fashion. A cost function of the sum of the times of travel of every person could be implemented, but it could be weighted by urgency, you could pay to go faster, that is to give priority in the algorithm. Now with people crossing the street it becomes harder, the human factor alters some things. Then a local wave would be formed, there would only be need for interference with the local traffic.

* selflessness is rare :Life:
Through tenths of paragraphs Nietzsche paints the picture of man as utterly selfish, his motives guided by his state. Thats why truly selfless acts are so so valuable, yet so damn rare to find, also because the most selfless don't even come to be known (when there are witnesses one has a greater drive to do great acts).

* recommender systems in radios, not spotify :Business:
Kinda insane that there isn't yet an algorithm for recommendations and on radio for music, like there is in social media. The radios are putting the same music for everyone, not personalized, and the same happens in tik tok and reels, as in you have peaking phases, along with base building phases, where in the latter it tests new vertices of the space whereas in the former it gives you just what you want, to keep you hooked, giving those dopamine hits here and there. However these progressions could be programmed, as in not just random progressive overload, through the algorithm and future AI. It would have to be an integration of spotify into radio or maybe a chip, but rights of the musics would have to be conceded. Maybe that this doesn't yet exist is why people don't see music as this indulging drug of distraction.

* Dali Biography: Absolute Commitment to Character :Life:
He jokes and jokes, but since he had previously done art, art it is. He is his philosophy, and when he said that he was his art, he wasn't joking.
* Dali Biography: Backing up the Genius :Life:
The whole Dali thing is: be so committed to something that you give no option to people but to believe you.
* Dali Biography: Persona as the Ultimate Artwork :Writing:
I don't know if his art is his paintings or his persona.
* Dali Biography: Credibility and Control :Life:
Ironic, trolling everybody and everyone, and yet producing great art.
* Dali Biography: Believing in the Genius :Life:
"oh Salvador, se brincares aos génios um dia tornas-te num."
* Dali Biography: Lucid Madness vs Unconscious Madness :Philosophy:
"O palhaço não sou eu! Mas esta sociedade monstruosamente cínica..."

* Philosophy of science :Philosophy:
O processo da pesquisa científica: Michael Saunders divide as assunções em: ontológicas: qual a natureza da realidade que vai ser tratada, epistemológicas: sobre a validade do processo de pesquisa, o que conta como conhecimento, axiológicas: quanto à ética das coisas, de quem é a função de confirmar os dados, é correto expor ratos às variáveis? Tem a ver também com a pesquisa ficar mais pessoal, mais dependente da tua filosofia de pesquisa do que propriamente a pesquisa em si. É deixar que os nossos valores entrem em cena. O realista crítico, quanto à ontologia trata as coisas como reais mas não se quer deixar enganar. Para serem reais tem de ser compatíveis e coerentes. O realista crítico tem em conta diferentes camadas de realidade: o que é fisicamente real, o qual é é fictício... Os modos de pesquisa podem ser indutivos, dedutivos ou abductivos (em que se começa ou com indutivismo ou dedutivismo, mas no decorrer da experiência há coisas inesperadas que nos levam a ir buscar mais informação, vamos então saltando entre indução e dedução. O construtivista acha que a realidade é construída a partir de interações sociais em que se partilha significado, propósito.  
No pós-modernismo, há uma dúvida quanto ao propósito das disciplinas: são como o pi ou como a borracha? Ser como o pi é terem um rumo marcado, numa aproximação cada vez melhor (Popper), as borracha só é boa ou má dependendo do contexto, as teorias são incomensuráveis. Para o pós-modernista as metanarrativas da sociedade não podem ser testadas ou confirmadas, por isso não sabemos se estamos a caminhar para uma melhor arte, uma sociedade mais justa... O pós modernismo diz que quando achamos uma obra de arte bonita é devido ao contexto, ou seja, esta visão dá primazia à subjetividade, dão até a ideia de que não há regras definidas para os jogos, depende apenas do contexto. A verdade absoluta é que tudo é relativo, aproximadamente. Para o pós-modernista, os trabalhos não transcendem a cultura, eles desenvolvem-se a partir dela, tal como o indivíduo só se desenvolve em função da sociedade, como Michael Sandel sugere. 
Everything is approximately pseudoscience, approximately, approximately approximately, ad infinitum, approximately.

* From Life 3.0 :AI:
We didn't understand how birds flew when we invented the plane. (competência sem compreensão). Também podemos chegar ao AGI sem compreender o cérebro mas, neste caso, é melhor perceber o cérebro, porque senão ainda fazemos alguma coisa que não devemos e somos nós que pagamos. É quase um princípio de responsabilidade saber minimamente no que nos estamos a meter antes de nos metermos em algo tão volátil como a inteligência artificial. 
"The most important thing science/history has teached us is that we are the masters of underestimating"

* Kafka's Trial: Firmness as the Product of Stupidity :Philosophy:
"A sua firmeza é o produto da sua estupidez" em relação aos guardas.
* Kafka's Trial: Belief vs Openness to the World :Philosophy:
When you make People believe, they become ignorant.
* Kafka's Trial: The Absurdity of Proving Innocence :Philosophy:
Ele é tão inocente que pensam que está a mentir.
* Kafka's Trial: The Interrogation as Theatre :Philosophy:
K. No primeiro interrogatório tenta conquistar a audiência com as piadas.
* Kafka's Trial: Inevitability and the Illusion of Choice :Philosophy:
"it is not necessary to accept everything as True, one must only accept it as necessary."
* Kafka's Trial: The Final Walk and Inevitable Fate :Philosophy:
"Bem pode a lógica ser inabalável mas não resiste a alguém que quer viver."
* Kafka's Trial: The Human as a Tethered Dog :Philosophy:
Aquele desespero de ser forçado a aceitar o destino por meio de um sistema que n conhece.
* Kafka's Trial: Facing Fate with Curiosity :Philosophy:
Nonetheless "fitou os cavalheiros com curiosidade" ele encara o destino com curiosidade.
* Kafka's Trial: Enjoying the Process of the End :Philosophy:
No fundo ele n quer acabar o processo porque entretanto apercebeu-se de que o fim n é o que deseja.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Highest Places From Deepest Depths :Life:
The highest of places comes from the deepest of depths. If you put yourself at those depths you will have space upwards, thus feel pain, do as everyone else but harder, add to what everyone does the element of hardness you need and desire, so that the equalizer comes from the height of your summit. If you start like everyone else, it won't be long till other forces will spoil your unimaginable to others longing for height. They will find you crazy beyond certain point, you won't be over human but just unhuman, that you won't live with humans, they will keep you in mind.

* Trial and Error as Underestimated Blessing :Life:
Quotes and questions:
you gotta do some shit and try some shit to be aware of the consequences, for at any moment if that thing is needed, you will know exactly what it takes, no worries. If you don't, you will have a cloud of uncertainty when it comes the time, fear grows and grows. trial and error is a very underestimated blessing, when simulations of more things are possible it will be the key to improvement.

* Love on the Loneliest Solitude :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions:
That love on the loneliest solitude is dangerous. Love is a truly powerful thing, and humans can go down the rabbit hole quite far, Nietzsche says to avoid all love except for what is alive. But actually, while the things alive give you the biggest thrill, they also give you the greatest disappointments. The rocks and mountains are consistent. Hard and consistent through time. Again the different time scales of things. You are at a different frequency than things. Sometimes you align with them, sometimes you are out of phase.

* Limits Are a Lie :Life:
Quotes and questions: Limits are a lie, feelings are a lie. Everything that someone has ever said that was psychological is a total illusion. Once you get beyond these things, thirst, hunger, tiredness, you get this flow, no questions asked from your body. Infinite energy. Every moment you are new.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Human Flows Highest and Deepest :Philosophy:
Human flows the greatest into the highest mountains, as well as the deepest abyss.

* Knowledgeable vs Wise :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions:
To be knowledgeable is to know, to be wise is to know better.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Courage as the Greatest Killer :Life:
Courage, courage to go higher, is a confidence that comes from knowing the depths, that one may not fear them, or at least one knows them, even if one fears them one moves up. Courage is the greatest of killers, says Nietzsche. It kills all bullshit. It kills all death. If that's life, one may go at it again, says Nietzsche. Recurrence.

* One Has Children Because He Loves Himself :Life:
Quotes and questions: that one has children because he loves himself

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- To Desire Is to Lose Oneself :Philosophy:
To desire is to lose oneself says Nietzsche. Yes, you need nothing more than yourself, it is easier to not desire if you see yourself. Desire should curve away at your sight.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Accept the Abyss: Pain Goes Away :Life:
When one accepts the abyss and the deep pain, when one embraces and waits expects for it, one may even find the pain to go away.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Climbing as Diversion of the Soul That Wants to Fly :Philosophy:
That climbing great heights may be a diversion of the soul whose actual goal is to fly.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Able to Go When the Gun Goes Off :Life:
There is nothing like being able to go when the gun goes off. Doing things when they are there, being well when you need to do stuff, that can't be taken away from you. If at the possibility of getting closer to flying you immediately smell upwards, rather than downwards of fear, that's value like value can't be bought. You can feel all pain afterwards and be tired and rest afterwards, but once the opportunity presents itself, you are on your highest game.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Beware the Blissful Hour :Life:
Be very very weary and careful and suspicious of the blissful hour. That time where you seem to be good, just enjoying something that in theory doesn't add anything to your life, like cooking, or cleaning the house, or just feeling good with some music. This time you will regret once you realize how much more you could be getting out of your life, and that you are letting time slip away. You will understand how dangerous it is to fall for this fake contempt. No, when cooking or cleaning the house you should be angry and insulted that you have to do this, get it done as fast as possible. Its not ok, you should feel bad.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Extreme Feeling Under the Skin, Peace on the Surface :Life:
You gotta have that extreme feeling burning under your skin not to waste time, yet over the surface be able to contain it at will and thus seem at peace.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The World Deeper Than Any Dive :Philosophy:
The world is deeper than any dive humans made into it. Be blessed and protected by the day that only has 24 hours, that after some 50 hours you fall asleep and away from your digging. Blessed that you can never be pushed by unwanted currents into the depths where light can reach anymore and all is still. That is only intentional and exploratory. May the darkness and increasing depth as you swim away from the safe surface not scare you to do so! That you may find another island along the way

* Trip to Barcelona :Life:
Chegamos à estacao de barcelona, e apos o primeiro de muitos mercadonas, onde o tesouro dos doriakis foi desvendado, ficamos a tentar comprar bilhetes, o que se mostrou bastante dificil, ate que tivemos expert advice da irma do hugo, e fomos ate terrasa. Terrasa o lugar onde estava o alojamento, que so nesta altura reparamos se localizar no caralho mais velho. Apanhamos chuva, mas entramos. Os quartos eram fixes, havia um gym. Preparamo nos para o sono beleza e no dia seguinte partimos, menos o antonio, que ficou em casa a descansar e a tomar banho e a comer atum. So much winning he couldnt take it anymore. Com grandes ambicoes partimos, tinhamos uma lista da Maria a admin de 30 coisas a visitar, mas nesse dia so conseguimos umas 3. Sagrada familia, cujo interior deixou o lourenco um apreciador de arte moderna, parque guell, onde xandas hugo e lourenco brincaram à exploracao de um manifoldo tridimensional atraves de iterancia de decisao em cada nodo do grafo de caminhos com maior e menor gradiente, e fomos ate à marina, onde nos sentimos uns reis com uma vista a dubai. A irma do hugo chegou e levou nos a conhecer um bar de fadas, onde estivemos a discutir biologia durante umas 2 horas. Era a ultima noite do hugo em barcelona, e apesar de cansados, e de sermos obrigados (mas tambem queriamos) no dia seguinte a acordar cedo para ir visitar a cidade, decidimos ir sair a noite, numa quinta feira onde poucos bares e discotecas gratis estavam abertos. Demos settle nuns shots de absinto e cocktails num bar com excelente musica, para comecar a hypar para a noite. O gil ja tinha ativado o modo freak, ja so falavamos espanhol, e numa decisao impulsiva pagamos 18 paus por uma discoteca de 5 salas, o razzmataz. Chegamos la e descobrimos que tinhamos bilhetes pra o dia seguinte. Tentamos varias solucoes, fomos chatos com os segurancas mas admitimos a derrota. O hugo nao admitiu derrota estando disposto a pagar um total de 40 paus por 30 minutos na discoteca, mas a vibe ja estava perdida. Fomos para casa, nao propriamente saindo nem propriamente dormindo. O hugo despediu se e embarcou para uma jornada de 1 ano na dinamarca, os outros acordaram cedo para ir ver essa barcelona. Fomos pelo centro, encontramos a anastacia a caminho, petiscamos no mercado, e fizemos rotting no jardim. Fomos ate ao aeroporto, fazer mais rotting, ate que nos disseram que o voo tinha sido cancelado. Mesmo depois de termos conseguido enfiar tudo nas malas, pareciamos uns traficantes pelintras que se recusavam a pedir a mala de cabine extra. Uns foram para o hostel e sairam no razmataz obtendo a sua vinganca nos segurancas que haviam arruinado a noite anterior, xandas antonio e maria. Ja o Lourenco ficou a dormir no aeroporto e a continuar a ver os videos de cozinheiros masterchef que tinha visto nessa tarde. O gil foi para o hotel de 4 estrelas que a easy jet abancou ter um sono de qualidade pela primeira vez na viagem. No dia seguinte todos menos o gil foram de rede expressos de 16 horas para lisboa, ja estavam fartos de passar horas em comboios com calor e entao foram passar horas com calor e apertados num autocarro. O gil foi explorar a cidade, nesse e no dia seguinte, onde tomou um banho majestoso de imersao no novo hotel, completou a lista da maria ganhando pontos para o seu ego, e foi de aviao. Gastamos no revolut 3800 euros, conseguindo ir variando bastante a divida entre uns e outros, tendo coletivamente tambem atingido uma divida enorme de sono. Cada um voltou as suas vidazinhas de merda, voltamos todos a miseria, ao lodo, apenas com a recordacao de alguns momentos bons que passamos no interrail, broken em dinheiro e em alma. Vamos no entanto conseguir fazer um video e apresentacao incrivel para os futuros viajantes, onde vamos engana los sobre o nosso insano interrail, e esperamos receber novo financiamento dos nossos patrocinadores, atraves do esquema de piramide, patrocinadores esses o revolut, a discover eu e a easy jet, que, espero eu, vai abancar o almoco de paella de 40 euros do gil e a viagem de bus de 60 do xandas maria e antonio. Saimos desta viagem jovens mais cultos, adultos, melhores a jogar sueca e todas as suas variantes, melhores a viver mesmo sem sono, melhores a digerir leite com lactase e a saber aproveitar o pao nosso de cada dia, melhores a carregar merdas e guarda las numa mochila. Buscamos as fronteiras desse mundo, subindo as montanhas mais altas e vendo as melhores vistas, cheirando os piores peidos e fazendo as maiores caganeiras. Em suma, estamos prontos para a vida de trincheira e caso de guerra.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Great Ambitions Find Others' Virtues Small :Life:
You that have great ambitions find the virtues of others ever smaller, find joy in seeing them content, that they can live with small virtues, that they can leave this earth without knowing a lot of things, that they can forever live in this earth and not seek escape. That they can make of the bit they are enough. You are glad that there are such people, you are glad people that don't know what you know can still have something going for them. You find peace in knowing you could never be them, and they could never be you, and you support them in their direction, and they in yours, even if no one understands each way.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Happy to See Someone Caring for the Earth :Life:
You are happy to see that someone is taking care of this earth and loving it, even if not loving it really, but still they do company to it, where you who love it more have to go away from it and onto greater platforms.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Blissful Hours: Rediscovering Childhood :Life:
Some blissful hours where you remember what it was like in bliss, in childhood. Being alone away from home and coming back to that swing which you abandoned, swinging some time forgetting time, just swinging, this thing we did way before. Immersion bath the same thing. Away from home rediscovering these lost feelings.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Einstein on Ideas and the Outflow :Technology:
Einstein said if you only see others' ideas your brain starts to lose its idea generating power. For you superintelligence overhuman that have huge inflow of ideas, you must find ways of discharging, having also a huge outflow, the usual combo of superintelligence with universal constructor, know and make, feeding one another.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Humans as Their Own Best Domesticated Animal :Philosophy:
Humans have become humans' best domesticated animal, says Nietzsche. This the overhuman avoids. Humans have lost their claws, their rupturing teeth have recessed, and their hunter eye has faded. They have lost the ability to hunt and kill. Yet the overhuman hides, but still has these killing monster abilities. He doesn't fall in the thin illusion that civilized modernity doesn't require and is very far away from this primal level of instinct and mode of negotiation. And being domesticated by other humans is the biggest of fall backs. Letting oneself drift down the food chain, unknowingly, to be at the hands of others. Maintain yourself up there so you have full steering ability over your destiny.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Mediocrity Hidden Behind Moderation :Life:
That mediocrity may be hidden behind the labeling of moderation.

* Life Has Infinite Value :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions: people say life doesn't have infinite value, or else they would absolutely do everything in their way as to not die, like staying at home just eating so as not to risk anything. First that is fallacious, living my life is what can be debated to have infinite value, not just any life, not just being alive. But then still people may say the value is finite, cause of course it is it only makes sense for it to be real that way. Yet they are wrong, life does have infinite value, now we don't want to admit that cause then there is infinite pressure to live it to the maximum at every second, which people don't do and would thus see themselves as failures if they allowed themselves to see the truth. It is infinite value. Never forget this. Cause submitting to life is easier. It is easier to have no purpose, and people think the contrary, it is easier to be searching for purpose even if not finding one, and letting one go with the waves. And people really believe that if they thought just being alive had infinite value that they would do everything in their possession not to die, total lie, that they think they are rational agents. They think they do things that they know are right, when in reality that is the problem of most people, that they know what they have to do yet don't do it. And furthermore all it takes is other things besides being alive to have infinite value that all of a sudden you wouldn't rationally any longer give all of yourself, all of the finite or infinite you can give to the pursuit of being alive. And its a strangling feeling, one where you start to lose air and your body demands for movement, stress as some would say, that you realize the value of your life is infinite, and you gotta go on this very moment. So I understand why the human, not the overhuman, runs away from it. And its truly when you realize you are wasting that urgency comes and you start going. If you know your sleeping time is in 20 minutes you will give your max until then, and this major rush at night doesn't come because its night as one may think, it just happens to coincide with night because its when you know today's journey is ending, when you realize you haven't achieved anything today for example, you have nothing to show for, and that gives you energy. Yet if you know you are going to prolong the night it will be shit, so no need to postpone your sleeping time.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Note: Diary Entry :Writing:
Adicionar ao diario cena da velha da suica a gritar da propriedade privada

* Life Has Infinite Value (Continuation) :Philosophy:
Quotes and questions continuation:
and its with that feeling of urgency that people feel bad, instead of grateful, it hurts but it pushes you like many other feelings. People start diverting it and misdirecting into other things, releasing through listening to sad music for example. Yet embrace it. Feeling like a loser is a privilege. If you felt like a loser even as you conquered the whole world and became a god you would push more. If you feel like a loser after winning multiple nobel prizes you would probably win more. Yet if you have the ability to push yourself regardless of these feelings you are in a position of even more power.
gift and curse at the same time, like elon musk said that you get bombarded with ideas. It is not supposed to be easy to make the bridge between the overhuman and the earthly, between the godly matters in which you are enrolled and transmitting that into ideas for this godless world. It will hurt like crap, cause the pressure gradient is tremendous between those realms. The difference of temperature is tremendous, and you the intermediary have to have tremendous resistance and will suffer a bit in letting things pass from one world to the other. You have a small tub and huge things from the godly world will have to fit, and only you can handle the strength on it that it will exert on its way to the earthly.

* The Paradox of Giving It All and Burnout :Life:
Quotes and questions:
being in the flow and floating in the clouds, that is something that can't be bought. When you have more responsibilities like children and what not you won't feel good playing video games. There simply will be too many things circulating your mind unimpeding you from giving your full attention to the game. You know you will have only 2 hours. Maybe even if you had nothing to do you would still have two hours, but just not having it, just being constrained in this limit makes you unable to be fully present. You know there will be consequences. This is also the same trap as when you start believing someone out there is working harder than you. It is the most powerful thing, but at the cost of consuming you deeply. You won't be able to do nothing else but give your maximum in your craft, the drive to be number one through this mechanism takes a lot of stress and suffering thoroughly, resting only in the end, not in the middle. People don't understand what that actually means. Resting in the end not in the middle means you will never rest for as long as you want to be number one, and when you rest to celebrate you won't be number one anymore. It really means you must forget resting, or simply living with the pain of knowing it but not knowing it in that it is an option to you but you keep choosing no. This is half of the main danger of greatness and what keeps people away even if subconsciously from it. It will take everything from you. The other half is you will take everything from it. From the world, that you will have a whatever it takes attitude, which may translate in being ruthless, a dictator of sorts on the world, which can include humans that work with you for example.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Urgency of Knowing Others Will Get Your Ideas :Business:
There also emerges the urgency of knowing that to any idea you get others will get, and you must capitalize on that exclusive short lived so knowledge.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Paradoxically Always Against the Current :Life:
Paradoxically you are always against the current. When you haven't achieved nothing others can see, even if you have already achieved a lot internally and know you are the best, they will say you are a failure and you will say you are a winner, yet when you win so much everybody will tell you you are a winner, you've done it already, but if you want to keep improving beyond the realm of humans and into the sky you will have to have a part of yourself that considers yourself a failure. Its not necessary of course, but it helps, in that it increases the chances. But I won't need it, for enough will power and over power the stagnation. True, you will have no particular reason to keep flying to greater heights, yet that makes it paradoxically have so much more reason, you have your internal reasons, you will have no one pushing you there but yourself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Silence That Shadows Power :Life:
A silence that shadows one's deeper will, masks through cold and snow and dark the absolute firepower of a star underneath, one slips by, one moves through. Unannounced power.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Dark Silence, Muddy Waters, Hidden Sun :Philosophy:
Dark silence and muddied waters. One may not see the depths nor what hides there nor what hides under the earthly surface one is allowed in. The absolute sun that keeps that habitable, yet that maintains for the human inhabitability close to that core. Yet the capability to, at one's own gaze, and at will turn all the water into a clear and transparent body, to have nothing to hide to oneself. The gap between what you know of yourself and what others know is totally on your hands. So you hide nothing from yourself, you've been through all the depths and still digging further, further into the core that powers you without you knowing, that core of the earth, yet building the sun, the core that you devised to power you, and if needed have that completely shown naked.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Happiness That Pours From Creating :Writing:
And if people saw the happiness that poured out of your creating and created sun they couldn't handle it, they would probably find refuge from its overarching and sharp cutting hot rays by not believing it. That good willful things come out of joy. And its that deepest of the deepest joy that your sun runs on, a love of life greater than they can conceive, that is the light that keeps you awake through the stillest and darkest and coldest hours of the winter that makes you and your sun stronger.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude Knocks for Different Reasons :Life:
Solitude comes knocking on the door for some who are wrong among a sea of right people, and for others who are right among a sea of wrong people.

* Meaningless Night and Meaningless Movies :Life:
Quotes and Questions:
with the same light and of the same sickness I see them suffer, those meaningless wasteful in the night and those meaningless wasteful watching movie after movie. They just direct nothingness into different things.

* Mechanistic Interpretability and Human Experts :AI:
Quotes and questions:
mechanistic interpretability helps land some jobs on people who actually know subjects deeply, those subjects that AI has long overtaken humans at. Cause we can actually analyse its reasoning, studying the patterns of the AI in his thoughts to see how it actually works and how to improve. So still people that are damn good at coding will be helpful, the bar that is required then to help in this portion is high, cause if you are a worse programmer than someone else, considerably, perhaps it is not so unreasonable to think you won't be able to add anything to their report. Yet the scale of examples of problems solved by AI and what not will be immense, so many people can work at this at the same time. Its about removing the blackbox that we are still placed in with AI, actually understand what is going on, which has ramifications for AI safety too and the study of intelligence and our own brain. Human experts can still steer the AI. Your hard work on learning things and knowing better things than other humans will not be in vain, even if its the AI making the discoveries you can help it make more better than those who only see numbers, those who only know there is a benchmark and hope it keeps increasing, namely through throwing scale and money at it. Our task is to follow AI, take the ride. It is faster than us, which finally gives us something to hang on to. Keeping the pace with it, it pushes us more. Don't think you will be spared of the work. Don't think physicists will be spared of deep hard thinking, on the very opposite, and this is a widespread mistake in people's vision of AI, the deep and hard thinking will only increase. And all it takes is us not holding onto the lace for some time to not be able to come back at it, but that is losing all control. If we get to the point where it holds tremendous authority over us, simply because we don't know a single stuff of why he does what it does, it can do whatever he wants with us. So keep trying to understand why he makes a certain play on the chess board. It may even be the case that in rigorous disciplines such as physics and maths he lights the way, he shows possibilities but it is still duty of the humans and now enhanced humans and overhumans to actually go there and explore, to see many more things. It has the answers but we have to have the reasoning to those answers, let him tire himself in the huge search space, with its huge speed and scale advantage, and show us where gold is possibly lying, we then take it from there and go find it. We don't want it to give us fish, we want it to teach us to fish a fish.....

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Fundamentally Dissociate from Groups :Life:
Fundamentally dissociate from groups. Never see yourself through the light of a group, I am not part of any group, its they not us. Always save at least an extra layer for yourself over the group, because you matter more than the group.

* Overhuman vs Navy Seals Tests :Life:
Quotes and questions:
for an overhuman navy seals tests and the like are necessary material. If he knows he's done it its just for the others to believe, its even easier to him, that only knows he can damn well do it, just doesn't have done it. If he hasn't done it then it proves great for himself. Now you ask the hardness of these tests of special forces worldwide, the symbolic quitting bell to ring in the navy seal, it may be irrational. At first glance it may be worse for the navy seals, they will lose a lot of smart and capable guys that won't join the game of pointless suffering, yet this is a psy op, thats why it becomes effective. Its effectively mind controlling the seals to serve relentlessly. They dare them to chose the irrational route, and now they are so attached to it and invested through what they have given that they couldn't go back, they are stuck to this mistake of life. Thats how you select for slaves, intentional slaves one may add. That is for the humans. The overhuman always puts himself, again, in a layer above any group, a layer above any ritual, any act, any idea. You can never actually get to him if he doesn't so please. He is always damn protected and there is always a new day sunshine for him even if all else conspires against him. Thats how he is also able to move through obstacles, just willingly and with unseen power eat the grass, go ahead and breaking all walls along the way, you can't affect him, and he can also normalise greatness to a larger extent. Because at the end of the day its greatness of the human, he has already transcended that, he can win and move right through to the next goal post.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Build Your Bed in the Layer Above :Philosophy:
Now about that layer in which yourself live, above all else of the earthly and of the real. You should build yourself a bed there, with a good mattress, so that you never fall from it. A solid base to be forever, cause forever and from ever you have lived and will continue to live there. You become independent of the world. Its one way of immortality, not complete still, but it makes anything that can possibly happen not take you down. Still you rise

* The Generalist's Unification :Life:
Quotes and questions:
you can see and think and recognize the way of the generalist as that of unification. You see many dispersed parts in the world, and you put together the pieces of the puzzle. But you can go further by creating a new puzzle.

Now that layer in which you live

* Two Types of People and Time :Life:
Quotes and questions:
there are two types of people: those to whom, the more time passes the less their potential on this world, for others, the opposite, through time they keep expanding their ceiling.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude Among Humans: Wild and Strange :Philosophy:
Solitude among human beings will always be wild and strange, says Nietzsche. Well yeah, it is that overhuman that has the extra layer, where only he can see through, where he can make refuge, in solitude thus. Some run away from it. Some find overhumanly power in it. Think of godliness, gods don't walk among humans forever, they have a sky up there where they can be in solitude.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- In Solitude: Free of Mind, Spirit, and Body :Philosophy:
Here in solitude you can be free of mind and of spirit and of body. You can be truth seeking. You can engage the self, waken the beastly beings underneath, waken the hidden desires, get a better grasp on all of you.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Reading a Book Twice: The Solution :Writing:
The solution to the paradox of reading slowly and getting the deepest insight while reading fast enough for having it all inside the context window is solved through reading the book two times. This is a great method of exploration. Dedicate all context window to a place inside the surface, getting and registering the highest of peaks and the smallest of valleys, the smaller irregularities, and after having the topography traced, you can now look at the whole map. Most people jump right away into the whole map, having no nuance in their words. They outline the general aspects of a book for example, but weak is the power behind that image, uncertain. Uncertain and thus, to fill in the blanks they have to use what they already know, what is already instinctive, what is thus most likely already part of the collective intelligence knowledge, so low quality air these thoughts breathe. A cloud chamber this air fills.
this applies to all scales. You can dive into many things deeply in your life, and then run through them, most retrospectively, to get the general portion of the world map you have with you.

* Comparative Advantage in Life :Life:
Quotes and questions:
have in mind the power of comparative advantage in your life. If you right now due to the environment surrounding you can do better something relative to another thing, you have to do both things, and you want to maximize your output, it is smarter to do the specialty thing, that which relatively at the moment is the easier to do, or that you can get out more of. So even if you get more out of reading one book right now, if reading another one will be more valuable now than later, and the other one maintains value, you ought to read the other one. You trade tasks in your time economy with all versions of yourself through time. All it takes is knowing what there is to do. Now keep in mind there are things with negative output, things you don't want to do, even if right now is the perfect moment to do them probably don't.

* Reacting vs Acting :Life:
Quotes and questions:
many times people fall in the trap of reacting rather than acting. They see things exaggerated one way, and instead of grounding themselves they position themselves too much the opposite way, even if that is not their home, to bring some sort of balance, which only cultivates discussion and anger and revenge building desires. One should be unshakeable, memoryless, independent, in that the condition probability doesn't condition on anything.

* Opportunity Cost and Rising Bar :Business:
Quotes and questions:
as you develop your sense of opportunity cost then it becomes harder to appreciate things. Your drive aligns what you find pleasure in doing and what you know you should do, for its painful to go against this drive, to be doing the wrong thing against your well developed opportunity cost sense. You don't want to do it.
it makes a difference especially in that many things good to you aren't the best things for you, and the bar of what you go after thus rises quite a bit. Something like going to the beach with one friend, which you know is good for you, and for which you previously had the feeling that oh its good so you are only winning, impossible to waste time. Yet its possible and now you know it. This also if you are coherent eliminates all negative things, if you don't even have time for good things, only great ones, imagine the bad things.

* Hard Conversations as Checkpoints :Life:
Quotes and questions:
hard conversations are those that make you evolve, checkpoints. Just like much less over time you see your parents, much less will they have the hard conversations with you. You should be grateful if you still get lectured. Embrace it. Hopefully they haven't yet given up on you; they still want to carry you to a good port

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Getting Outside the World :Philosophy:
Getting outside the world and measuring it. When you've gone beyond the world you get the ability to measure it in some way you please. Feeling its weight, its size its power its potential. This is not for everyone.

* Hungry Lions and Ferocity :Life:
Quotes and questions:
now there are two types of people in this world. Those that get dizzy and soft with hunger, and those that turn into hungry lions, those whose ferocity increases and those whose ferocity decreases.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Commanding Your Values to Yourself :Life:
Realize that even if there were no humans, talking to yourself and commanding your values onto yourself and singing to yourself is still of value. Just because there are and we use voice for them doesn't mean that if there weren't we wouldn't do so. You should still show yourself what you like, as if having visitors at home and showing your product, showing the decoration of where you live, your space, and your headspace, reminding yourself of your motives as much as you show them to others. And talking to yourself, and being curious about yourself. And revisiting yourself often, and preparing for your visit, cleaning your place as you do to others.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Imaginary Friends as Parts of the Self :Life:
The power of imaginary friends comes when you recognize they are all part of the self. Here you can leverage what external projection gives you while knowing you have full internal control over all of it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Receiving Yourself in All Forms :Life:
So receiving yourself in all your forms, quite as literally as you so please. Don't shy of having dinners with your monsters, or have business meetings with them that you will have to share the same space, so better strike some deals. It is as great a tragedy to lose contact with someone, as in you haven't maintain a relationship sufficiently that now its inaccessible to you, to lose some parts of yourself through this process.
the internal world is at least as great as the outer world, this goes unnoticed at times.
point being theres a lot to do with just yourself, in fact everything you would do with someone else, with humans, and if of a overhuman were talking, only in yourself will you find it most likely. Play with yourself, tease, have fun with yourself.

* Group Depth vs Individual Depth :Life:
Quotes and questions:
with friends you notice most groups stall at some level of depth, that more depth is not taken into account, not treated well. Not given the respect it deserves. Now the level of depth with each you dive into individuals of that group is orthogonal to the depth allowed by that group, they are uncorrelated.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Imposing Good and Evil Upon the Self :Philosophy:
That the imposing of good and evil and other similar bastards upon the self takes away from the fun of what you are. That you start carrying this heaviness, that some parts of you under these labels may start to not show up to the dinners with yourself, repressed, no voice on the arguments at the table.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Right Time to Part :Philosophy:
And for all the nay sayers of eternity, those that think they are glad of death, for them I have a challenge, that they find in themselves the right time to part. To win in this way you would choose the right time, a time in which you are glad to go. If you let the choice slip your hands, then you will most likely be regretful, and yet it is hard to be right here and say that you only want one more minute of life. I would respect and as I've said in the past its a way of freeing yourself from the death's taking, that you knew when to part. Either plan or simply decide. But the truth is the fundamental dilemma, either you are here and can do anything you want, or you aren't and there's nothing, you can never use all your will to live at present. If you are alive, it is here.
and for them I say come the next. Those that of earth start being lazy and underestimating and taking for granted and despising, move on then. What if only the top x number of people who want most to be here would be here?

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Fernando Pessoa on the Error of the World vs the Self :Philosophy:
Fernando pessoa:
the point by Caeiro that we as our consciousness is the error, not the world, that the world everyone sees and shows itself through things of the world that exist. That you being an error is an error of you yourself, whereas if the world was an error it would be an error of everyone. Furthermore to strengthen even more his point he notices that before we think and see the self we see the world. Me: Only after the world do you wake up from it. Now this issue of primacy of the world or self is only a strong case if you believe them different. The argument of the number of errors could be diminished, without saying the whole world is the same as the self, by just pointing out that that world which Pessoa thinks everyone looks at may not be what every or anyone is looking at, that all of them are looking at a mix of the world and the self, which means that if the self was an error then again everyone would be wrong, it equalizes the self beyond the world.

* Fun Is Not the Most Important :Life:
Quotes and questions:
if having fun was the most important, you wouldn't need to say that fun is the most important. The commentator says djoko still got that touch on the tennis ball, and more importantly he still has fun. Bullshit. If he means that by having fun he is able to play at previous level, it means that fun wasn't the most important. And what made djoko and continues to make him is not having fun. so in saying fun is the most important the commentator is really just exercising in remembering that fun still is a factor even tho what he does with djoko has nothing to do with fun.

* On the Desire to Dance :Life:
Quotes and questions:
its hard to contain that excitement and will to dance and live. Sometimes you feel music hitting without you even hearing it. Sometimes singing to yourself the music has greater effect than the transitory listen people give it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Eternal Recurrence and Multitude :Philosophy:
Eternal recurrence also relates tightly to my principle of overhuman, one of them, that is multitude dominating the branching of the universe. Eternal recurrence is about yourself becoming yourself, over and over. It is not as if by recurrence you mean in the next life you improve, or make some tweaks, or are an alternate version of you. No, you only have this one. So multitude has to be gotten from this very own. There is no excuse that later you diversify yourself.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- What You Do Echoes in Eternity :Philosophy:
This idea that what you do in life echoes in eternity allows you to have faith on something other, allows you to cease eternity through means of maximizing everything but eternity and the lies of the other worlds, you go for this very life.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Pseudo-Scientific Grounding of Eternal Recurrence :Physics:
Maybe a pseudo scientific principle we can arrive at here. An idea.
First, as says Nietzsche, when you die you are gone in an instant, no soul keeps living on. But he says that you are entwined in the causes.
what I can do is say that your permanent entanglement of the universe, by its injectivity, makes it so that you live on through the entanglements of you, and in an infinite universe by the ergodic principle you may infinitely recur. And so eternal recurrence need not even be so abstract, a thought experiment. Just the thought of it makes me attack the moment, but you can ground it in these terms, and see that eternal recurrence is in the infinite of the universe, your life will echo for the rest of the universe, in space and time.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Tower of Babel of All Writings :Technology:
If you had a tower of babel but rooted in order, as in a digitalized to be easier form of all writings of mankind we could see which sequences of words hadn't yet been combined, and play with them, though it would of course be all noise if you don't impose your will on it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Going Under Like the Sun :Philosophy:
The movement of the going under as a sun which illuminates a whole living sets. Thus does the great soul hoover itself to the grounds.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Having Children with Eternity :Philosophy:
To have your children with eternity, Nietzsche says. Thus Zarathustra is entwined with all walks of life. Does do the animals love him, and he is in connection with nature, with his soul, with himself, with life, with eternity, with humans, he has conversations with all of them.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Note: To Search :Life:
To search:
diving deep into all things that take away brain capability by killing neurons or stuff. For example, alcohol, brain damage from contact sports....

* The Paradox of Giving It All :Life:
Quotes and questions:
the paradox of giving it all. It's both so easy and hard at the same time. Its so simple, so clear what you have to do, yet giving your maximum is so hard, people usually are well below their maximum when they think its their maximum.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Note: To Search — Johnny Manziel Charisma :Life:
Search: johnny manziel charisma

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- I Believe in Eternity :Philosophy:
I believe in eternity. I'm a human of eternity. Most of all eternity through moment. The most finite thing, a moment, has such infinite value that I equate it to eternity.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Not Too Attached to Your Humanness :Philosophy:
That you may not get too attached to your humanness, having no problem in traversing the bridge toward the higher human in you, to never be afraid of your heights. There is more. There is more beyond comprehension. This is common comprehension. There are levels over, there are levels under, and you are unable to see all of the levels. You may see some heights, just know there is much beyond that.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being Ahead of the Curve: The Right Tree :Business:
Being ahead of the curve. If someone is always on the search for what gives fruit now, he will never get fruit, for as he gets closer the fruit on that tree ends and its those of another one that get to feast. What you see now is not what you pursue, you must pursue a tree before people recognize its potential for fruit to get the most out of it.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- God's Equality and the Relative :Philosophy:
Nietzsche paints the picture that God made humans all equal, it prevented the overhuman from rise over and beyond the human. If there is no absolute, there is relative. All was ever relative in this world. And that means we can perfect and improve.

* From Bruce Springsteen :Physics:
Quotes and questions:
from bruce springsteen movie, they say that where you came from is gone, and where you thought you were headed was never there. Don't get too attached to where you were headed, enjoy where you are right now. It just is so that where you were headed was projected by you before, a retarded potential, what you are now is a vibration of what you were in the past. Now that mean again reaping what you have of your direction. Not waiting till that driving direction is gone, now waiting for that flame you discovered dies out. Thus the retarded potential can actually take into account the predicted future. It is retarded it only has information that was in the past but through it you can be already and act already as you think you will.

* The Power of the Capital :Business:
Quotes and questions:
sometimes you realize the power of being in the capital. Sure, people mention that everything is there. But that is huge. Anything you want to try, a sport, a route in life, its all there. For a generalist overhuman it is thus super valuable to be in the capitals of the world, and be everywhere and do everything all at once.

* On Caring and Numbness :Life:
Quotes and questions:
from Yudkowsky's sequences, on caring. That how big the world is and yet how small we are and how little our scope is, that makes big problems not feel so big, it drives us powerless through numbness. And not just global problems, or problems of the scales of the universe. But all the problems with their respective scale. We may not be able to feel the same pain, or even see how high it is, when for example someone has a family member that has passed. Yet we do our internal calculations of how painful that is, we trust our mind over our feelings to help them. Now actually this is just right, for if everyone could feel and carry the weight of the problems in the world and in their lives at all times, at all times they would be in bed, unable to lift themselves up. Powerless through hypersensitivity, the opposite of numbness. It takes an ever stronger soul to bear and confront the height of these things, without feeling small. To take it on and thus elevate itself to those heights, to see eye to eye with those things. So we notice that the first kind of powerlessness was powerlessness to help, not powerless to do anything, on the other hand, you are very free. The best of both worlds is to help and be free. To not feel is a privilege, for you are not stopped to act on it, but you are free from its burden. Things are all under your control. But feeling when in your control is also damn good. The best of both worlds the overhuman strikes is feeling when he wants to feel what he wants to feel. If he wants to know what it feels like something, he feels that. He can tune the inputs, the different melodies he hears. And he acts as though the world is really as big as he believe it is. This is massive. The world also, but this also, so I believe it. I sometimes enter these vicious loops, that means they are independent, they need something to get going and looping. That something is my belief

* On Caring (Continuation) :Life:
Quotes and questions
continuation
this means you are the limit on how much things matter, on the value of life. You are the limiting factor, meaning as much more as you believe and put into the world the more you will live and get out of the world. Now this numbness and inability to feel also makes people not act, because they base themselves on feelings and when they don't feel strongly enough, they don't correspondly act. Like smokers who say they want to quit, but they don't actually feel the consequences of keeping on smoking, they don't feel as bad as they should, perhaps they hide themselves from the part of themselves that could make them feel so bad as to quit, make the weight of not quitting greater than the pain of not ever again smoking.

* No Vacation, No Work Days :Life:
Quotes and questions:
in the limit of the overhuman's life, there is no such thing as vacation or work days. Its all the same. In all you do what you want. You get away from society's limits and live in the continuous exponential. Time is one and you don't live of borrowed time, its value doesn't grow or shrink depending on context, its just an exponential market, that you make your time exponentially more valuable. The thing about the exponential, as many AI people know but not so many know how they can apply it to their life, is that one in the beginning you see very small gains. You may think it is constant, but its still possible to gain awareness that you're actually riding an exponential. Oh and when you know it, oh boy keep it going. The exponential also enjoys the memoryless property, meaning at all times you can feel the same, and you can feel anything, namely, you can feel that you are just now starting to lift off, to start really growing in the exponential, you can at the same time feel you are riding high after so much compounding, as well as just starting and just in the flat line phase, knowing in the future you'll be growing even faster.
and there is also no such thing as burnout. You ride that fine line between it and just working hard, but by enjoying what you are doing, by being able to feel it right and not just believe that doing this thing that you otherwise wouldn't have like to be doing hard will give you results, there is no burnout. Burnout is then out of the scope of bounds of the range of effort you can put in. Even if you put max effort, burnout is so far away because you enjoy what you are doing that it will never come.
now because there is so much numbness out there, but not for the good reasons, it is an amazing feeling to see great feelings. To see people care so much for something. That is something I've always searched, perhaps because my level of attributed value and caring for life is so so great. But the overhuman doesn't find in humans enough great feelings and caring. He is alone in this, this can be a pain for him, but he has to search inside, which makes his caring even more his and genuine.
now one thing that can make you sad by there being no walls between work days and vacation, and this kind of thing and a new kind of thing, is that you are numb to the passage of time. There are no big marking points. You finish your student years, like having the last class of your life, but it doesn't affect you, you move on right away to the next thing.

* On Procrastinating the Next Step :Life:
Quotes and questions:
I've been reported as procrastinating on taking the next step. But there is real power in having the best world model. Of course some procrastinations lead to nothing, but other forms of investing into your world model pay off, and when you come back to the real world per say where you act, you act damn quickly. You let the real world steps process in the unconscious while you are doing other things, and when you come back you do so with a solution.
Now this may explain why people don't want to be where they are at, always. They let themselves be filled. They can't rise to the occasion, they can't rise to grow enough to be bigger than what was imposed on them, which was what was imagined and put on the line by the other person, or entity, or institution.

what a time to be alive, and perhaps I should really take advantage that we are in the time of perhaps the birth of superintelligence and the greatest change in human history.
Hassabis's solving intelligence to solve everything else could be imagined very explicitly as someone for example having built a superintelligence, that you interfaced with and gave you, proportional to the quality of your input but just orders of magnitude over any variation in the input, the right answers and solutions, to physics problems and stuff alike. one single person could make discoveries in one day worth 100 nobel prizes today, and if they did a paper on this technology since everybody would start using it it would have like hundreds of millions of citations just off humans alone, with ai agents contributing a lot to probably.
now this assumes some kind of discontinuity. Most likely these things are gradual and so systems will adapt to it, it will never be this level of shock. Although if you think about the exponential, since the timestep that humans work with is more or less constant (again a huge assumption given that AI could help with this), eventually the increases would feel very discontinuous.

* Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Sadness Is Not Deeper Than Happiness :Philosophy:
And people there are who think sadness is deeper than happiness. They leave out the apparently obvious way of living for it is shallow, they don't get to appreciate the infinite depth one can reach via the good way, without ever having to turn to other ways. Any one thing in life is deep enough to be the object of your attention and study for the whole of your life. You can always go deeper and make progress. Progress is the one truth of the world, besides time and those other perhaps more fundamental. It can be said, if time is the truth of the world then progress is the truth of life.
